internships.description: Paid summer internships placing high school students from under-represented communities in Houston-area law firms, corporate legal departments, and judicial chambers to expose them to legal careers.
internships.whyApply: Paid legal career exposure with top Houston firms and judges.

internships.title: Dallas Fed High School Fed Challenge
internships.description: Academic competition where high school teams analyze current economic conditions, present monetary policy recommendations to Fed economists, and compete regionally and nationally. Includes coaching, resources, and site visits.
internships.whyApply: Direct engagement with Federal Reserve economists on real monetary policy.

internships.title: TXRX Labs Maker Apprenticeship
internships.description: Paid summer apprenticeship at Houston's largest makerspace, training students in CNC machining, welding, woodworking, 3D printing, and advanced manufacturing alongside professional makers and instructors.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on fabrication skills and a paycheck at a leading makerspace.

internships.title: Texas House of Representatives Page Program
internships.description: High school students serve as pages during the biennial Texas Legislative Session, delivering messages on the House floor, supporting state representatives, and observing state lawmaking in action.
internships.whyApply: Front-row seat to state lawmaking during the legislative session.

internships.title: Heard Natural Science Museum Youth Volunteer Program
internships.company: Heard Natural Science Museum & Wildlife Sanctuary
internships.description: Teen volunteers support wildlife care, trail maintenance, exhibit interpretation, and education programs at the 289-acre sanctuary north of Dallas. Junior docent and summer camp counselor tracks available.
internships.whyApply: Wildlife rehabilitation and natural history experience in a genuine sanctuary setting.

internships.title: Girlstart Summer Camp Teen Leader Program
internships.description: High school girls serve as teen leaders and near-peer mentors at Girlstart's STEM summer camps for elementary/middle school girls, gaining teaching experience while deepening their own STEM skills.
internships.whyApply: Leadership, teaching, and STEM role-modeling experience — strong for education and CS applicants.

internships.title: Texas Comptroller Summer Internship Program
internships.description: Paid summer internships in the state's chief financial office for students interested in accounting, tax policy, public finance, and data analysis. Includes exposure to economic forecasting and state budgeting.
internships.whyApply: Paid public-finance experience at the Texas state treasury.

internships.title: Texas Biomedical Research Institute SURE Program
internships.description: Summer Undergraduate Research Experience (SURE) also accepts exceptional high school rising seniors for 10 weeks of hands-on biomedical research alongside Texas Biomed scientists in infectious disease and genetics labs.
internships.whyApply: Real biomedical research in infectious disease at a top independent research institute.

internships.title: CPS Energy Summer Youth Employment Program
internships.description: Paid summer internships for San Antonio high school students at the nation's largest municipally-owned energy utility. Placements across engineering, IT, sustainability, communications, and operations.
internships.whyApply: Paid engineering exposure at one of the largest public power utilities in the US.

internships.title: Houston Area Urban League Youth Leadership Program
internships.description: Youth leadership and civic engagement program for Houston high school students, including mentorship, community service projects, college access workshops, and annual National Urban League Youth Summit attendance.
internships.whyApply: Deep civil-rights history paired with real leadership development and national network.

internships.title: UT Austin Longhorn Center for Academic Equity Bridge Program
internships.company: UT Austin — Longhorn Center for Academic Equity
internships.description: Summer enrichment and college readiness program for first-generation and underrepresented Texas high schoolers preparing for university STEM, humanities, and leadership coursework at UT Austin.
internships.whyApply: Strong academic bridge and mentoring pipeline into a top public research university.

internships.title: NYU ARISE (Applied Research Innovations in Science and Engineering)
internships.description: Free, selective 7-week full-time summer research program for NYC high school students. Participants work in active NYU Tandon labs across bioengineering, molecular engineering, robotics, computer science, AI and machine learning alongside faculty and graduate-student mentors.
internships.whyApply: One of the most prestigious free high school engineering research programs in NYC — direct access to Tandon labs.

internships.title: NYU CS4CS (Computer Science for Cyber Security)
internships.description: Free 3-week summer program for NYC 9th-11th graders training in digital forensics, steganography, hacking, and cryptography. One of the only free cybersecurity programs for high schoolers nationally. No prior CS experience required.
internships.whyApply: Best free cybersecurity pipeline into college CS and cyber programs.

internships.title: Morgan Stanley JumpStart High School Program
internships.description: Paid 6-week program for high school juniors and seniors in NYC introducing students to the finance industry through shadowing, workshops, and networking. Students gain exposure to sales & trading, wealth management, and investment banking.
internships.whyApply: Rare paid high school exposure to a top-tier Wall Street firm — competitive and highly selective.

internships.title: Futures and Options Internship Program
internships.description: Paid summer internships placing NYC public high school students at financial firms, marketing agencies, nonprofits, and corporate partners. Includes work-readiness training, resume and interview prep, and field visits to companies like EY and JPMorgan Chase.
internships.whyApply: Established NYC pipeline placing students at top business partners — strong alumni network.

internships.title: Bloomberg Arts Internship
internships.description: Paid 6-week summer internship for NYC public high school juniors placing them at major cultural institutions (museums, performing arts organizations, libraries). Interns rotate through departments and complete a capstone project under professional mentorship.
internships.whyApply: Most prestigious paid high school arts internship in NYC — rotates through top cultural institutions.

internships.title: Brooklyn Museum Teen Apprentice Program
internships.description: Paid teen programs including the Museum Apprentice Program, Creative Practice, Teen Night Planning Committee, and InterseXtions. Teens explore art, education, activism, and museum careers while contributing to real programming and exhibitions.
internships.whyApply: Paid teen program at a major art museum — exceptional for aspiring arts professionals.

internships.title: Weill Cornell HPREP (Health Professions Recruitment & Exposure Program)
internships.description: Free 10-week Friday-afternoon program held January–April. NYC high school students hear from physicians and health professionals, explore health career pathways, and engage in interactive sessions on medicine, public health, and research.
internships.whyApply: Direct access to Weill Cornell faculty and pipeline programs — excellent for aspiring doctors.

internships.title: NewYork-Presbyterian Summer Volunteer Program
internships.description: Summer-only volunteer program for students 16+ requiring 120 service hours across select NYP campuses. Volunteer roles include patient services, child life programs, and clerical/administrative support in clinics and nursing units.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on hospital experience at one of NYC's top academic medical centers.

internships.title: Brooklyn Navy Yard Employment Center Internship
internships.description: Paid summer internship placing Brooklyn high school students at businesses within the Brooklyn Navy Yard including tech startups, manufacturing companies, and creative agencies. Interns gain exposure to modern urban industrial work.
internships.whyApply: Unique hands-on exposure to modern urban manufacturing and industrial design at the Brooklyn Navy Yard.

internships.title: CUNY Career Launch
internships.company: The City University of New York (CUNY)
internships.description: CUNY Career Launch hires thousands of student interns across dynamic industries at $20/hour for 6 weeks. Placements span tech, healthcare, government, business, and nonprofit sectors. Open to CUNY students and graduating NYC high school seniors.
internships.whyApply: Large-scale paid internship pipeline connecting NYC students to industry partners at $20/hr.

internships.title: AMNH Lang Science Program
internships.description: Selective 5-year (Grades 8–12) science enrichment program at AMNH. Students engage in hands-on investigations, field trips, lab work, and mentored research alongside AMNH scientists across biology, earth science, astrophysics, and anthropology.
internships.whyApply: Exceptional long-term immersion in real museum science — one of AMNH's most competitive programs.

internships.title: New York Hall of Science Explainer Program
internships.description: Paid positions for high school and college students serving as floor educators at NYSCI, engaging visitors with hands-on science demos, exhibits, and workshops. Explainers develop STEM communication skills and deepen content knowledge through weekly training.
internships.whyApply: One of the oldest and most respected paid STEM youth programs in NYC — strong professional development.

internships.title: Carnegie Hall Weill Music Institute Youth Programs
internships.description: Carnegie Hall's Weill Music Institute offers programs for teen musicians including NYO2 (for 14-17 year-old orchestral musicians), NYO Jazz, and Musical Connections. Students receive world-class mentorship from Carnegie Hall artists and perform at major venues.
internships.whyApply: Unparalleled mentorship and performance opportunities for serious young musicians at Carnegie Hall.

internships.title: NYC Urban Park Rangers Junior Ranger Program
internships.description: Volunteer programs run by NYC Urban Park Rangers connecting youth with nature through guided hikes, ecological restoration, wildlife monitoring, and conservation education across all five boroughs. Teens can progress to leadership roles in the Junior Ranger program.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on urban ecology and conservation experience with NYC's professional park ranger corps.

internships.title: Genesys Works New York
internships.description: Year-long paid professional internship program for NYC high school seniors from underserved communities. Students complete 8 weeks of training in IT, business operations, or accounting followed by paid corporate internships at Fortune 500 companies.
internships.whyApply: Rare paid senior-year corporate internship pipeline for NYC students from underserved communities.

internships.title: Cooper Hewitt Teen Design Scholars
internships.description: Paid summer intensive at Cooper Hewitt for NYC high school students interested in design. Scholars learn design thinking, prototyping, and visual communication while collaborating with museum curators, educators, and professional designers on real-world projects.
internships.whyApply: Only Smithsonian design museum program — rare paid design career exposure for high schoolers.

internships.title: The Bell Voices Reporter Internship
internships.description: Paid journalism training and reporting internship for NYC high school students. Students learn reporting, news writing, research, interviewing, and investigative techniques, then publish original stories about the NYC public school system.
internships.whyApply: Student-led newsroom publishing real investigative reporting — excellent portfolio-builder for aspiring journalists.

internships.title: Cornell 4-H Career Explorations
internships.description: 3-day residential career exploration conference at Cornell University for New York 4-H members in grades 8-12. Students choose tracks spanning agriculture, animal science, veterinary medicine, food science, engineering, and natural resources, working with Cornell faculty and graduate students.
internships.whyApply: Direct access to Cornell faculty and labs in agriculture and life sciences through New York's 4-H program.

internships.title: NYPL Teen Internship Program
internships.description: Paid summer internship placing NYC teens in NYPL branches and research centers. Interns support literacy programs, help run summer reading, assist with tech training, and contribute to teen programming while gaining library science and public service experience.
internships.whyApply: Behind-the-scenes experience at one of the world's most iconic library systems while getting paid.

internships.title: Bank of America Student Leaders — NYC
internships.description: 8-week paid internship placing NYC high school juniors and seniors with local nonprofits like Boys & Girls Clubs and Habitat for Humanity. Includes a fully-funded week-long Student Leaders Summit in Washington, DC to develop leadership skills and meet peers nationwide.
internships.whyApply: Prestigious national leadership program with paid nonprofit placement and DC summit experience.

internships.title: NYU Tandon ARISE
internships.description: Applied Research Innovations in Science and Engineering - a 7-week fully-funded program for NYC rising high school juniors and seniors from underrepresented backgrounds. Students complete bioengineering, robotics, or materials science research with NYU faculty mentors and present at a final poster symposium.
internships.whyApply: Fully funded engineering research program at one of the country's top engineering schools, designed for students who would not otherwise access university labs.

internships.title: Cooper Union Summer STEM Program
internships.description: 6-week intensive engineering and computer science program for rising high school juniors and seniors. Students work in small teams on a culminating design project (robotics, genetic engineering, digital VLSI, architecture, or chemical engineering) with Cooper Union faculty and graduate student mentors.
internships.whyApply: Rigorous hands-on engineering immersion at a legendary engineering school with a real capstone team project to put on college applications.

internships.title: Roswell Park Summer Research Experience Program for High School Students
internships.description: Competitive 8-week paid summer research program placing rising seniors in cancer research labs at one of the nation's oldest NCI-designated cancer centers. Students work alongside principal investigators on projects in oncology, immunology, pharmacology, and cancer genetics, and present at a research symposium.
internships.whyApply: Paid cancer research experience at a world-class NCI cancer center - exceptional credential for pre-med and research-oriented students in Western NY.

internships.title: NYS Assembly Intern Program
internships.description: Legislative internship in the NY State Assembly in Albany. High school seniors and college students shadow lawmakers, draft legislative memos, research bills, and observe committee hearings and floor sessions during the spring session. Limited high school slots available through constituent assembly member offices.
internships.whyApply: Direct access to state lawmaking - a rare credential for high schoolers interested in government, policy, and law.

internships.title: Thurgood Marshall Summer Law Internship Program
internships.description: Competitive 6-week paid summer internship placing NYC public high school sophomores and juniors from underrepresented backgrounds at top law firms and legal departments. Interns work on research projects, attend legal seminars, shadow attorneys, and explore law school and legal careers.
internships.whyApply: Paid placements at elite Manhattan law firms - a rare and life-changing credential for aspiring lawyers.

internships.title: Girls Who Code Summer Immersion Program - NYC
internships.description: Free 2-week virtual summer immersion program for high school girls and non-binary students. Students learn computer science fundamentals, build a final project, and meet women engineers at partner companies including Goldman Sachs, Bank of America, and JPMorgan Chase based in NYC.
internships.whyApply: Free, well-known CS program with a strong community of women in tech and direct exposure to NYC tech employers.

internships.title: All Star Code Summer Intensive
internships.description: Free 6-week summer coding intensive for young men of color in grades 9-11. Students learn web development, JavaScript, and design thinking, visit top tech companies in NYC, and build a final capstone project presented to industry mentors at a showcase.
internships.whyApply: Transformative free tech program specifically designed for young men of color with a tight-knit community and industry exposure.

internships.title: Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory Urban Barcode Research Program
internships.company: Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory DNA Learning Center
internships.description: Free year-long DNA barcoding research program for NYC high school teams. Students learn PCR and bioinformatics at the DNA Learning Center, design a biodiversity research project, collect samples, sequence DNA, and present findings at a citywide symposium with CSHL scientists.
internships.whyApply: Authentic molecular biology research with CSHL scientists - a world-class credential for students interested in biology and genetics.

internships.title: Billion Oyster Project High School Programs
internships.description: Hands-on marine restoration programs for NYC high school students. Volunteers help monitor oyster reefs in NY Harbor, process shell for recycling, collect water-quality data, and assist with field research from the project's Governors Island base. Partner schools run semester-long restoration curricula.
internships.whyApply: Unique marine restoration experience in NY Harbor - tangible environmental impact and hands-on field science in the heart of NYC.

internships.title: GrowNYC Learn It Grow It Eat It
internships.description: Paid teen apprenticeship at GrowNYC's Bronx youth farm. Teens grow and harvest produce, run a youth-operated farmers market, lead educational workshops, and learn about food systems, nutrition, and urban agriculture throughout the growing season.
internships.whyApply: Paid hands-on urban farming and food justice work in the Bronx - rare combo of agriculture, business, and public health experience.

internships.title: Jazz at Lincoln Center Summer Jazz Academy
internships.description: Intensive residential jazz training program for top high school musicians nationwide, featuring performances at Jazz at Lincoln Center in NYC. Students study with Wynton Marsalis and members of the Jazz at Lincoln Center Orchestra and work in small combos and big band ensembles.
internships.whyApply: Study jazz with Wynton Marsalis and perform at Jazz at Lincoln Center - the pinnacle for young jazz musicians.

internships.title: ICP Teen Academy
internships.description: Free after-school and summer photography programs for NYC high school students at the International Center of Photography. Teens learn darkroom and digital photography, visual storytelling, photojournalism, and exhibit their work in an end-of-year student showcase.
internships.whyApply: Free access to world-class photography instruction and equipment at the premier photography institution in NYC.

internships.title: Urban Word NYC Youth Leadership Programs
internships.description: Free spoken-word, creative writing, and youth leadership programs for NYC teens. Participants take workshops with professional poets, perform at open mics, compete in the NYC Teen Poetry Slam, and develop as writers, performers, and activists.
internships.whyApply: The country's leading teen spoken-word program - an incubator for nationally recognized young writers and activists.

internships.title: Prep for Prep Contingent
internships.description: Long-running NYC leadership development program preparing high-achieving students of color from 5th-7th grade for placement at independent schools, followed by ongoing advising, summer internships, and college counseling through high school and beyond.
internships.whyApply: Life-changing full-service leadership program with a powerful alumni network across top schools and colleges.

internships.title: Rock The Street, Wall Street - NYC
internships.description: Free financial literacy and career exposure program for high school girls in NYC. Weekly workshops led by women finance professionals cover investing, markets, and careers, plus field trips to Wall Street firms and a spring capstone mentorship matching students with finance pros.
internships.whyApply: Direct exposure to Wall Street and mentorship from women in finance - a rare opportunity for high school girls interested in finance careers.

internships.title: NFTE BizCamp NYC
internships.description: Entrepreneurship summer program for NYC middle and high school students from under-resourced communities. Students develop an original business concept, build a pitch deck and financials, compete in a business-plan competition, and meet NYC entrepreneurs and investors.
internships.whyApply: Build and pitch a real business idea with feedback from NYC entrepreneurs - a proven gateway to business school and startup culture.

internships.title: Virtual Enterprises International High School Program
internships.description: In-school class that runs a simulated business for a full academic year. NYC teams compete in a citywide trade show and national business plan competition. Students take roles in marketing, finance, HR, and operations while running a virtual company with global trading partners.
internships.whyApply: Run a real simulated business all year and compete at national trade shows - a hands-on alternative to traditional business internships.

internships.title: Brooklyn Public Library Today's Teens, Tomorrow's Techies (T4)
internships.description: Paid teen tech internship program at Brooklyn Public Library branches. Teens provide one-on-one tech help to patrons, teach basic computer classes for seniors, and complete projects on digital literacy, media, and coding while earning a paycheck.
internships.whyApply: Paid tech tutoring and digital-literacy work in your own community - meaningful, resume-building, and accessible.

internships.title: RIT K-12 Summer Programs
internships.description: Residential and commuter summer academies for high school students in engineering, computing, game design, photography, imaging science, and business at RIT. Students work in small cohorts with RIT faculty, tour labs, and complete a hands-on capstone project.
internships.whyApply: High-quality pre-college STEM and creative tech programs at a top engineering university in Upstate NY.

internships.title: Summer High School Research Program
internships.company: University of Rochester — Laboratory for Laser Energetics
internships.description: Two-month summer immersion at the Laboratory for Laser Energetics for high school juniors from the Rochester area. Students conduct mentored research in laser physics, optics, materials science, plasma physics, or scientific computing and present findings in a formal paper and talk.
internships.whyApply: Rare chance to do real laser/plasma research at a DOE-funded national laboratory — strong pipeline to top physics programs.

internships.title: Cornell Precollege Studies Summer Program
internships.description: Rising juniors and seniors take regular Cornell undergraduate courses for real college credit on the Ithaca campus. Residential, commuter, and online options available across 100+ courses in architecture, engineering, pre-med, veterinary medicine, hotel management, law, humanities, and more.
internships.whyApply: Ivy League college experience with real credits transferable to Cornell or elsewhere — one of the broadest pre-college programs in the country.

internships.title: Boyce Thompson Institute Plant Science Summer Research Internship
internships.description: Immersive 8-week summer program for high school students at an independent life-science research institute on Cornell's campus. Projects span plant science, molecular biology, bioinformatics, plant-microbe interactions, and biological engineering of plant systems.
internships.whyApply: World-class plant genomics research experience at an independent institute — rare paid high school lab placement.

internships.title: Cornell AgriTech Summer Research Scholars
internships.company: Cornell University College of Agriculture and Life Sciences
internships.description: Summer research scholars program at Cornell AgriTech in Geneva, NY. Scholars work on projects in agricultural research, food science, horticulture, viticulture, entomology, and food-and-beverage entrepreneurship. Some opportunities open to advanced high school students alongside undergraduates.
internships.whyApply: One of the only high school–accessible pathways into agricultural and food-science research at an Ivy-adjacent research station.

internships.title: Summer Research Immersion (SRI) / CSTEP
internships.description: Full-time summer research experience for STEM-bound students. High school affiliates enter through the CSTEP (Collegiate Science and Technology Entry Program) pathway, working 40 hrs/week for 5-10 weeks on real research problems with Binghamton faculty in biology, chemistry, engineering, and computing.
internships.whyApply: Authentic STEM research at a top SUNY campus with an explicit focus on supporting underrepresented students in science.

internships.title: Lang Youth Medical Program
internships.description: Six-year medical pipeline program for 14 talented 6th graders from Washington Heights and Inwood. Students attend Saturday sessions during the academic year and full-time sessions in July on the NYP/Columbia campus, learning anatomy, physiology, pathology, and public health while being mentored by leading physicians.
internships.whyApply: One of the longest-running hospital pipeline programs in the country — students who complete it often matriculate into top pre-med tracks.

internships.title: Syracuse University Summer College
internships.description: Residential, hybrid, and online pre-college program for high school juniors and seniors. Students take credit-bearing Syracuse courses in 100+ subjects including engineering, forensics, architecture, communications, sport management, and public health, and earn real college credit.
internships.whyApply: 60+ year-old pre-college program with a guaranteed admission pathway (with tuition scholarship) for students taking two credit-bearing courses.

internships.title: PREFACE Summer Engineering Design Program
internships.description: Two-week residential engineering program for rising juniors and seniors traditionally underrepresented in STEM. Students complete modules in electronics, engineering design, engineering analysis, CAD, and manufacturing processes, taught by RPI faculty.
internships.whyApply: One of the oldest continuously-running engineering pre-college programs in the US — running since 1978 with an explicit diversity mission.

internships.title: Summer@Rensselaer
internships.description: Collection of summer academic enrichment programs for elementary, middle, and high school students at RPI. Options include research experiences, engineering design camps, architecture, computing, and laboratory work. Residential and commuter tracks available in most programs.
internships.whyApply: One of the few summer programs that serves the full K-12 range at a top technological university.

internships.title: BEYOND ALBERT High School Research Program
internships.company: Albert Einstein College of Medicine / Montefiore
internships.description: Eight-week summer research program at Einstein College of Medicine for Bronx high school students ages 16+ entering 11th or 12th grade. Full-time lab research in cancer, HIV, and other chronic diseases, plus physician shadowing, biomedical education, and workshops. Continues as an after-school program through the academic year.
internships.whyApply: A paid hands-on research program explicitly for Bronx students — rare equity-focused pipeline into biomedical careers.

internships.title: NYC Mayor's Office Internship Program
internships.company: Office of the Mayor, New York City
internships.description: Three-month paid internship in public service and city government for NYC high school students 16+ with working papers. Interns are placed in a department of interest (policy, communications, community affairs, operations), attend a bi-weekly Speaker Series at City Hall with deputy mayors and commissioners, and complete a civic service project.
internships.whyApply: Behind-the-scenes exposure to the largest city government in the US — direct access to commissioners and deputy mayors.

internships.title: Hofstra University Summer Science Research Program (HUSSRP)
internships.description: Four-week summer program where 10th and 11th grade students are matched with a Hofstra science faculty mentor to conduct independent research in biology, chemistry, physics, mathematics, or psychology. Culminates in a poster session open to families.
internships.whyApply: One of the few Long Island programs that gives sophomores access to real faculty-mentored research.

internships.title: Junior Volunteer Program
internships.description: Volunteer program for students ages 15-17 at Mount Sinai's South Nassau campus. Junior volunteers work after school or during school breaks, assisting patients or performing clerical/courier duties in clinical and administrative areas. Seniors may qualify for the Dorothy M. Sharer Health Care Scholarship.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on hospital exposure on Long Island with an associated scholarship program for graduating seniors.

internships.title: Summer Junior Volunteer Program
internships.company: Long Island Jewish Medical Center (Northwell Health)
internships.description: Summer volunteer program for students ages 14-17 at Northwell's flagship Long Island Jewish campus. Junior volunteers serve in clerical, greeter, and patient-support roles with a 50-hour minimum commitment. Application requires working papers and a guidance counselor/teacher recommendation.
internships.whyApply: One of the few hospital volunteer programs that accepts students as young as 14 — a rare early exposure to clinical environments.

internships.title: Volunteer & Junior Docent Program
internships.description: Junior docent program for high school students ages 14+. Teens assist with guest education, animal care support, and conservation outreach at New York's only municipal zoo. Rolling applications and placement depends on availability.
internships.whyApply: The only zoo-based teen docent program on Staten Island, with hands-on exposure to animal care and education.

internships.title: High School Volunteer Program
internships.description: Volunteer program for students ages 16+ at the New York Blood Center. Volunteers host school and community blood drives, educate and register donors, and provide donor care during drives. Training is provided and students can earn community service hours recognized on college applications.
internships.whyApply: A direct community-health experience with one of the largest blood centers in the country — meaningful civic impact with real training.

internships.title: New York Youth and Government High School Assembly
internships.description: Statewide civic education program for grades 9-12. Delegates research policy issues, draft bills, and convene at the state capitol in Albany for a 3-day mock legislative session (March 2026) held at the Desmond Hotel and the real NYS Capitol Building. Builds public speaking, advocacy, and legislative skills.
internships.whyApply: Students actually debate and vote on their own legislation inside the NYS Capitol — a rare immersive civics experience.

internships.title: Art & Culture Scholars Program
internships.company: Metropolitan Museum of Art + NYCHA + Trinity Church Wall Street
internships.description: Paid high school internship for NYCHA residents exploring the arts, culture, and museum careers. Scholars work with The Met education staff on artivism themes and draw parallels between art in NYCHA communities and the Met's collections. Includes career exploration and public-facing projects.
internships.whyApply: A prestigious paid arts internship at one of the world's great museums, with an explicit mission to support NYCHA youth.

internships.title: University of Rochester Pre-College Programs
internships.description: Summer pre-college programs for high school students ages 13+ at the University of Rochester. Residential, commuter, and online formats available. Students explore STEM, humanities, business, music (Eastman), and medicine through 2-week sessions with Rochester faculty.
internships.whyApply: Access to a top-tier research university and the renowned Eastman School of Music in a single pre-college experience.

internships.title: Timbuctoo Climate Science and Careers Summer Institute
internships.description: Free state-funded climate science institute for high school students in NYC and the Adirondacks. Three 2-week sessions in July and August across NYC, Syracuse, and the Adirondacks focused on climate research, environmental careers, and hands-on field science. Designed to diversify the environmental workforce.
internships.whyApply: Fully-funded climate science experience that takes NYC students into the Adirondacks for real fieldwork — rare equity-forward environmental program.

internships.title: Brookhaven National Laboratory High School Research Program (HSRP)
internships.description: Six-week summer research program at Brookhaven National Laboratory for rising juniors and seniors in Suffolk County who have completed biology and chemistry. Students work alongside BNL scientists on active research in physics, chemistry, biology, environmental science, and computing.
internships.whyApply: Rare chance to do real national-lab research as a high schooler — DOE-funded stipend and access to world-class facilities.

internships.title: NASA GISS Climate Change Research Initiative (CCRI)
internships.company: NASA Goddard Institute for Space Studies / CCNY
internships.description: Year-long STEM engagement program placing NYC high school students and teachers into active NASA GISS climate research groups. Students work with NASA scientists on climate modeling, remote sensing, and Earth system analysis, culminating in a research symposium.
internships.whyApply: Direct research mentorship from NASA climate scientists — extraordinarily rare for high schoolers.

internships.title: Mianus River Gorge Research Assistant Program
internships.description: Multi-year field ecology research program for Westchester/Fairfield high school students. Participants conduct original field research on forest ecology, wildlife tracking, invasive species, and watershed health in an old-growth preserve, and present findings at scientific conferences.
internships.whyApply: One of the most respected high school field ecology programs in the country — students have co-authored peer-reviewed papers.

internships.title: Wave Hill Forest Project Internship
internships.company: Wave Hill Public Garden & Cultural Center
internships.description: Paid environmental stewardship internship for Bronx high school students. Interns restore urban woodlands, remove invasive species, collect ecological data, and learn about horticulture, conservation, and environmental careers.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on paid conservation work in NYC — great for Bronx students interested in environmental careers.

internships.title: AMNH Science Research Mentoring Program (SRMP)
internships.description: Yearlong authentic research program pairing NYC high school juniors with AMNH scientists in astrophysics, genomics, paleontology, anthropology, or earth science. Students complete original research projects and present at the museum's Colloquium.
internships.whyApply: Real research mentorship from curators and postdocs at one of the world's top natural history museums.

internships.title: Rockefeller University Summer Neuroscience Program (SNP)
internships.description: Free two-week intensive summer program for NYC high school students exploring neuroscience through lectures, hands-on labs, and mentoring from Rockefeller faculty, postdocs, and grad students. Covers neural circuits, behavior, and neurological disease.
internships.whyApply: Elite neuroscience exposure at a legendary biomedical research university — completely free.

internships.title: Genspace Teen Urban Biodesign Program
internships.description: Hands-on synthetic biology and biodesign program at Brooklyn's community biology lab. Teens learn CRISPR basics, bioart, DIY lab techniques, and bioethics while working on small group projects. No prior lab experience required.
internships.whyApply: One of the first community biolabs in the country — unique access to DIY synthetic biology as a teen.

internships.title: BioBus Junior Scientist Program
internships.description: Paid multi-year science research program for NYC middle and high school students in Harlem and the Lower East Side. Participants use research-grade microscopes and learn cell biology, neuroscience, and microbiology from BioBus scientists.
internships.whyApply: Rare paid science research opportunity open to middle schoolers and underserved NYC neighborhoods.

internships.title: Fresh Air Fund Career Awareness Program
internships.description: Multi-year paid career development program for NYC teens from under-resourced communities. Students explore careers in healthcare, STEM, law, business, and arts through workshops, internships, and mentoring.
internships.whyApply: Long-term career development and paid internships specifically for NYC teens — strong alumni network.

internships.title: Global Kids Youth Leadership Programs
internships.description: Human rights, global affairs, and civic engagement program for NYC high school students. Participants learn about international issues, advocate for policy change, visit the UN, and lead community action projects.
internships.whyApply: Direct engagement with the UN and global policy issues — strong pipeline to international affairs careers.

internships.title: New York Philharmonic Very Young Composers
internships.description: Free composition program for NYC students ages 12-18 who have no prior composition experience. Students compose original orchestral works with guidance from teaching artists, and selected pieces are performed by NY Philharmonic musicians.
internships.whyApply: Your original composition performed by NY Philharmonic musicians — rare and prestigious for student composers.

internships.title: Sponsors for Educational Opportunity (SEO) Scholars Program
internships.description: Intensive 8-year academic and career preparation program for high-achieving Black, Hispanic/Latinx, and Native American students from NYC public high schools. Includes rigorous academic coaching, college counseling, and career exposure in finance, law, medicine, and more.
internships.whyApply: One of the most intensive college-readiness programs in the country — free for qualifying NYC students.

internships.title: NYPD Explorers Program
internships.description: Career exploration program for NYC youth ages 14-20 interested in law enforcement. Explorers participate in weekly meetings, ride-alongs, community service, and training in first aid, self-defense, and leadership.
internships.whyApply: Structured exposure to law enforcement careers with real officers — good fit for students considering criminal justice.

internships.title: Queens Botanical Garden Teen Green Team
internships.description: Paid environmental education and stewardship program for Queens high school students. Teens maintain gardens, lead community programs, learn about sustainability and urban ecology, and develop public speaking and leadership skills.
internships.whyApply: Paid green jobs program specifically for Queens teens — rare in an outer borough.

internships.title: Riverkeeper Volunteer Program
internships.description: Volunteer opportunities for Hudson Valley high school students with the leading Hudson River watershed protection organization. Volunteers help with water sampling, shoreline cleanups, sweep days, education, and advocacy campaigns.
internships.whyApply: Work with a nationally recognized environmental advocacy group protecting the Hudson River.

internships.title: Exemplary Student Research Program (ESRP)
internships.description: Teams of Chicago-area high school students conduct authentic research projects at Argonne with scientist mentors. Students use advanced instrumentation and present findings at a symposium. Topics span physics, chemistry, biology, and computing.
internships.whyApply: Rare chance to do authentic research at one of the country's top national labs while still in high school.

internships.title: TARGET Program
internships.description: Eight-week paid summer internship for Chicago-area high school juniors and seniors in physics, engineering, computing, and technical support at Fermilab. Students work alongside scientists and engineers on real accelerator and detector projects.
internships.whyApply: Paid summer research at Fermilab — one of the world's leading particle physics laboratories.

internships.title: Research in the Biological Sciences (RIBS)
internships.description: Four-week intensive molecular and cell biology lab program for rising high school seniors. Students do hands-on work with techniques like PCR, gel electrophoresis, microscopy, and microbiology while living on the UChicago campus.
internships.whyApply: One of the most respected pre-college lab science programs in the country.

internships.title: Collegiate Scholars Program
internships.description: Free three-year college access program for Chicago Public Schools students starting sophomore year. Scholars take UChicago courses, receive mentoring, and get intensive college admissions support across all subject areas.
internships.whyApply: A premier college access program for Chicago Public Schools students — completely free.

internships.title: Medill-Northwestern Journalism Institute
internships.description: Five-week residential summer program for rising high school seniors interested in journalism. Students learn reporting, writing, photojournalism, multimedia storytelling, and media ethics from Medill faculty and working journalists.
internships.whyApply: Training from the Medill School, one of the most prestigious journalism programs in the country.

internships.title: Science and Math Excellence (SMART) Program
internships.description: Summer enrichment program for Chicago-area high school students underrepresented in medicine. Participants shadow clinicians, attend lectures, and explore health careers at Rush University Medical Center.
internships.whyApply: Pipeline program into medicine from a major academic medical center in Chicago.

internships.title: College First Program
internships.description: Paid two-summer science research and college-prep program for Chicago-area high school students. Participants conduct plant biology and conservation research at one of the country's leading public gardens and research institutions.
internships.whyApply: Paid multi-year research program at one of the world's leading botanic gardens.

internships.title: Teen Internship Program
internships.description: Paid year-round internship for Chicago high school students in natural history research, collections work, and public science education at The Field Museum. Teens work alongside curators and educators.
internships.whyApply: Paid internships at one of the world's top natural history museums.

internships.title: High School Marine Biology Program
internships.description: Intensive summer program for Chicago-area high school students interested in marine biology and aquatic conservation. Students learn animal husbandry, water chemistry, and conservation research at Shedd Aquarium.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on marine biology training at one of the largest indoor aquariums in the world.

internships.title: Partners in Fieldwork
internships.description: Paid summer research program pairing Chicago high school students with Lincoln Park Zoo scientists on conservation and wildlife research projects. Students collect and analyze field data and present results.
internships.whyApply: Paid conservation research alongside professional zoo scientists — and the zoo is free and public.

internships.title: Teen Council / Teen Lab
internships.description: Paid teen leadership program at the Art Institute of Chicago where high school students plan public programs, create art, lead tours, and develop museum and curatorial skills alongside staff.
internships.whyApply: Paid teen leadership at one of the country's most prestigious art museums.

internships.title: Adler Teen Programs
internships.description: Paid teen programs at Adler Planetarium including citizen science, telescope building, and astronomy research. Teens collaborate with astronomers and science educators.
internships.whyApply: Paid astronomy and space science opportunities at Chicago's iconic planetarium.

internships.title: Student Science Minors (Achievers) Program
internships.company: Museum of Science and Industry Chicago
internships.description: Science enrichment and college-prep program for Chicago high school students. Participants take advanced STEM coursework, engage with MSI exhibits and scientists, and build a cohort around science identity.
internships.whyApply: Long-running STEM pipeline program at one of the largest science museums in the western hemisphere.

internships.title: High School Fed Challenge
internships.description: Team-based monetary policy competition where high school students analyze economic data and present a policy recommendation to judges from the Chicago Fed. Teams research inflation, employment, and financial conditions.
internships.whyApply: Engage with real Federal Reserve economists and get a serious taste of monetary policy as a high schooler.

internships.title: Minority Mentoring Program
internships.description: Mentoring program pairing Chicago-area high school students from underrepresented backgrounds with practicing attorneys. Students attend court visits, mock trials, and legal career panels through the Chicago Bar Association.
internships.whyApply: Direct mentorship from practicing Chicago attorneys — great for students exploring law.

internships.title: Issues to Action Summer Fellowship
internships.description: Paid civic engagement fellowship where Chicago high school students research community issues, meet with elected officials, and develop action campaigns. Mikva is a nationally recognized youth civics organization.
internships.whyApply: Paid hands-on civics training — Mikva alumni include city council members and state reps.

internships.title: One Summer Chicago
internships.stipend: Minimum wage or higher, 6-week paid job
internships.description: Citywide paid summer jobs program placing 14-24 year olds into employment and internship opportunities across government, nonprofits, and private employers in Chicago. Placements span every career field.
internships.whyApply: The largest paid summer jobs program in Chicago — thousands of placements across fields each year.

internships.title: After School Matters Teen Apprenticeships
internships.description: Paid apprenticeships and pre-apprenticeships for Chicago teens in arts, STEM, sports, communications, and career-skills programs. Delivered after school and in the summer across Chicago neighborhoods.
internships.whyApply: Largest paid teen program in Chicago — thousands of paid apprenticeships available each year.

internships.title: Genesys Works Chicago
internships.description: Paid year-long IT and business internships for Chicago high school seniors at Fortune 500 companies. After summer bootcamp training, students work 20 hours per week during their senior year in real corporate roles.
internships.whyApply: One of the few paid corporate internship pipelines for high school seniors in Chicago.

internships.title: IMSA Allies Program / Summer Enrichment
internships.description: Summer enrichment and outreach programs at Illinois' statewide residential STEM academy, open to Illinois middle and high school students. Programs include hands-on labs, mathematics, computing, and research exposure.
internships.whyApply: STEM programming at Illinois' top statewide math and science academy — open to students from across IL.

internships.title: Northwestern University Research Program for High Schoolers (NURPH)
internships.description: 7-week paid summer program bringing Chicago and Evanston-area high school sophomores, juniors, and seniors into Northwestern's Materials Science and Engineering Department labs. No prior research experience required.
internships.whyApply: Paid lab research at Northwestern's top-ranked Materials Science department. No prior experience needed — strong pathway for Chicago-area STEM students.

internships.title: CIERA Research Experience in Astronomy (REACH)
internships.description: Summer research program in astrophysics at Northwestern's Center for Interdisciplinary Exploration and Research in Astrophysics. Students learn Python programming, data analysis, and work on hands-on research projects mentored by professional astronomers.
internships.whyApply: Rare astronomy research opportunity for high schoolers with direct mentorship from working astronomers at a top research center.

internships.title: Regenerative Engineering Summer Research Program (REM)
internships.company: Northwestern University Center for Advanced Regenerative Engineering
internships.description: Summer research program at the intersection of biology, engineering, materials science, and medicine. Students learn essential lab techniques: cell culture, microscopy, 3D design and printing, cell viability assays, and advanced imaging.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on skills with real lab techniques — 3D printing, imaging, cell culture — that translate directly to college research roles.

internships.title: Discovering Healthcare Careers (DHC)
internships.company: Ann & Robert H. Lurie Children's Hospital of Chicago
internships.description: 6-week paid clinical summer internship for CPS juniors and seniors interested in healthcare careers. Students shadow medical professionals in the morning and attend workforce development workshops (resume writing, clinical skills, team building) in the afternoon.
internships.whyApply: Paid direct shadowing at the top pediatric hospital in Illinois — specifically designed for CPS students to build clinical exposure.

internships.title: Junior M.E.D. Program
internships.company: Ann & Robert H. Lurie Children's Hospital of Chicago
internships.description: 11-week hospital placement program partnering with Noble Charter ITW David Speer Academy. Students rotate through Lurie Children's departments, shadow staff, take on projects, and present a final product at the end of the program.
internships.whyApply: Deep, sustained hospital exposure with an individual project and final presentation — stronger than most shadowing programs.

internships.title: SHARP! Program
internships.company: University of Illinois Chicago College of Engineering
internships.description: 11-month research-intensive program immersing high school students in biomedical investigation at the interface of AI, robotics, and tissue engineering. Includes scientific lectures, career mentorship, lab methods, and collaborative projects.
internships.whyApply: Unusually long (11-month) commitment produces genuine research depth. Cutting-edge focus on AI in biomedicine.

internships.title: Young Scholars Program at UIC (YSP@UIC)
internships.description: Free four one-week summer sessions introducing diverse Chicago high schoolers to topics in advanced mathematics not covered in typical high school curriculum — with applications to science, technology, and society.
internships.whyApply: Free advanced math exposure — perfect for students whose schools don't offer higher-level math topics.

internships.title: Pre-Engineering Summer Programs
internships.description: Residential pre-college engineering programs at Illinois Tech including robotics with Arduino, a 2-week biomedical engineering intro, and chemistry research rotating through bioanalytical, environmental, forensic, and materials chemistry.
internships.whyApply: Variety of specific engineering disciplines in a college campus setting. Strong for students trying to narrow engineering specialization.

internships.title: Summer Volunteer Internship
internships.description: Competitive summer volunteer internship pairing students with an Assistant State's Attorney across various divisions. Work includes legal research, memo preparation, case management, and observing procedural requirements.
internships.whyApply: Direct mentorship from an Assistant State's Attorney in one of the largest prosecutor offices in the US. Real legal research and case exposure.

internships.title: Illinois House Honorary Page Program
internships.description: Students volunteer as Honorary Pages in the Illinois House, serving on the House floor by taking orders from legislators, delivering messages, and observing the legislative process firsthand. Sponsored by a state representative.
internships.whyApply: Unique Illinois-specific civics experience. See a live legislative session from the House floor — few students ever get this access.

internships.title: Future Leaders in Planning (FLIP)
internships.description: Free leadership development program for northeastern Illinois high schoolers exploring urban planning, urban design, sustainability, public policy, transportation, and community development through sessions led by regional planning experts.
internships.whyApply: Rare direct exposure to city planning careers — from transportation to water supply to sustainability. Free and led by working planners.

internships.title: Goodman Theatre Youth Arts Council (GYAC)
internships.description: Young Chicagoland artists use their passion for theater to connect communities and inspire change. Members collaborate to produce events, engage peers, and amplify diverse voices throughout the academic year.
internships.whyApply: Youth voice is central at one of Chicago's leading regional theaters. Real event production and community organizing experience.

internships.title: Musical Theater Intensive
internships.description: Free 4-week pre-professional program for Chicago-area students ages 14-18 building musical theatre skills in voice, acting, and movement with working Goodman artists.
internships.whyApply: Free and pre-professional at one of Chicago's top theaters — an exceptional opportunity for musical theatre students.

internships.title: Steppenwolf Teen Workshops
internships.description: Free series of hands-on theatre workshops for Chicago teens led by Steppenwolf teaching artists. Workshops take place at Steppenwolf's Loft education space and community partner sites throughout the city.
internships.whyApply: Led by actual Steppenwolf artists — one of America's most celebrated ensembles. Free and open to Chicago teens.

internships.title: King Conservation Science Scholars
internships.description: Leadership development program focused on conservation science and community advocacy. High school scholars participate in workshops at Brookfield Zoo and beyond, building environmental literacy and leadership skills.
internships.whyApply: Deeper than standard zoo volunteering — structured leadership development in conservation science from a major zoological society.

internships.title: Youth Volunteer Corps
internships.description: Teen volunteer program focused on conservation, wildlife, and science. Two tracks available; both include free trips to natural areas and museums throughout the year.
internships.whyApply: Entry-level on-ramp into zoo conservation volunteering with added field trips to other natural sites.

internships.title: Morton Arboretum Youth Volunteer Program
internships.description: Summer volunteer program for students entering grades 7-9 (multi-year returning through high school) with branches in Naturalist (Children's Garden), Gardener, Tree Monitor (climate change citizen science), and Science Camp.
internships.whyApply: Rare opportunity accepting middle schoolers. Multi-year pathway through high school with real citizen science (tree monitoring) component.

internships.title: Youth Climate Action Program
internships.description: 12-week program for high schoolers to engage with climate change by leading their own local climate action project rooted in collaborative, nature-based solutions.
internships.whyApply: Student-led project model — high schoolers design and execute their own climate action, a strong portfolio piece for environmental applicants.

internships.title: YOUmedia Teen Program
internships.description: Free creative teen maker space available at 29 CPL branches across Chicago. Teens work with 3D printers, laser cutters, DSLR cameras, music production, graphic design, photography, animation, and coding with skilled mentors.
internships.whyApply: Completely free access to 3D printers, laser cutters, and pro-level creative tools across 29 Chicago library branches. Mentor-guided projects.

internships.title: Student Trainee Program
internships.description: Paid 12-week student trainee program for high school juniors and seniors (2.8+ GPA) at Caterpillar's Peoria-area facilities. Placements in engineering, accounting, IT, and clerical roles.
internships.whyApply: One of the only large-scale paid high school engineering placements in central Illinois at a Fortune 100 manufacturer.

internships.title: Center for Talent Development (CTD) Summer Program
internships.description: Intensive academic summer courses for high-ability students in grades K-12 at Northwestern. Options in STEM, humanities, creative writing, engineering, and social sciences. Commuter and residential formats.
internships.whyApply: One of the nation's premier academic summer programs for gifted students, hosted at Northwestern University.

internships.title: Junior Volunteer Program
internships.description: Summer hospital volunteer program for high school students at Northwestern Memorial in downtown Chicago. Positions include patient escort, gift shop, information desk, and unit support. Training provided.
internships.whyApply: Direct exposure to a leading academic medical center in the heart of Chicago.

internships.title: Volunteer Services — Junior Program
internships.description: Summer volunteer opportunities for high school students at UChicago Medicine. Students assist with patient experience, family services, and administrative support at an academic medical center on Chicago's South Side.
internships.whyApply: Hospital volunteer experience at one of Chicago's top academic medical centers, with strong teaching culture.

internships.title: Volunteer at Rush Program
internships.description: High school volunteer program at Rush University Medical Center in the West Loop. Students gain exposure to hospital operations, patient services, and health sciences professions.
internships.whyApply: Volunteer at one of Chicago's largest academic medical centers, with strong opportunities in pre-health exploration.

internships.title: Teen Creative Agency (TCA)
internships.description: Paid year-round program where Chicago teens plan exhibitions, create programming, and engage peers with contemporary art. Members work with curators and artists on real museum initiatives.
internships.whyApply: Rare paid opportunity for teens to co-curate programs at a major contemporary art museum.

internships.title: Negaunee Music Institute Programs
internships.description: Teen music programs offering mentorship, performance opportunities, and community engagement through the CSO's Negaunee Music Institute. Includes the Civic Orchestra Fellows pipeline and community music initiatives.
internships.whyApply: Direct mentorship from Chicago Symphony Orchestra musicians — unique for serious teen musicians.

internships.title: Lyric Opera of Chicago Education Programs
internships.description: Education programs at Lyric Opera include student matinees, backstage tours, and youth engagement initiatives connecting Chicago students with world-class opera and performance production.
internships.whyApply: Rare opportunity to engage with one of America's leading opera companies as a teen.

internships.title: Chicago History Museum Teen Programs
internships.description: Teen programs at the Chicago History Museum include the teen council, educational workshops, and youth-led exhibition development focused on Chicago history and civic identity.
internships.whyApply: Work with one of Chicago's most prominent history institutions on real exhibition and programming projects.

internships.title: Civic Action Project
internships.description: Classroom-to-community civics program where Chicago-area high school students research a local policy issue and design civic action projects. Culminates in student presentations at City Hall and state-level events.
internships.whyApply: Trains Chicago students in real civic engagement and policy advocacy with mentorship from legal professionals.

internships.title: Illinois Supreme Court Commission on Professionalism — Pipeline Programs
internships.company: Illinois Supreme Court Commission on Professionalism
internships.description: Pipeline programs introducing Illinois students to legal careers through mentoring, court visits, and meetings with judges and attorneys. Partnership with Chicago Bar Foundation and other bar associations.
internships.whyApply: Direct mentorship from judges and practicing attorneys through the Illinois Supreme Court's professionalism commission.

internships.title: Future Founders Youth Entrepreneurship Program
internships.description: Chicago-based youth entrepreneurship nonprofit providing startup training, mentorship, pitch competitions, and programming for middle and high school students across Chicagoland.
internships.whyApply: Leading Chicago youth entrepreneurship nonprofit with deep connections to local startups and mentors.

internships.title: Junior Achievement of Chicago Programs
internships.description: Financial literacy, entrepreneurship, and career readiness programs for K-12 students across the Chicago region, including JA Finance Park, JA BizTown, and JA Company Program.
internships.whyApply: Oldest and largest youth financial literacy and entrepreneurship nonprofit, with strong Chicago business partnerships.

internships.title: Bank of America Student Leaders — Chicago
internships.description: Highly competitive paid summer internship placing Chicago juniors and seniors at local nonprofits. Includes all-expenses-paid week-long Student Leaders Summit in Washington DC.
internships.whyApply: Prestigious paid civic leadership program with a summit in Washington DC — highly selective.

internships.title: i.c.stars Internship Program
internships.description: Rigorous tech workforce program open to young adults (including recent high school graduates) from underserved Chicago neighborhoods. Combines full-stack development training with consulting placements.
internships.whyApply: Unique tech+leadership program that turns high school graduates into employed technology consultants.

internships.title: Manufacturing Connect
internships.description: West Side Chicago program connecting high school students to careers in advanced manufacturing. Includes machining certifications, shop floor experiences, and paid internships at local manufacturers.
internships.whyApply: Unique paid pathway into advanced manufacturing for Chicago students — earning industry-recognized credentials.

internships.title: Illinois 4-H Youth Development
internships.description: Statewide 4-H program offering STEM, agriculture, leadership, and civic engagement projects for youth ages 5-18. Includes state fairs, summer camps, and teen leadership councils in all 102 Illinois counties.
internships.whyApply: Statewide reach with hands-on agriculture and STEM projects — strong for rural and suburban Illinois students.

internships.title: Illinois FFA
internships.description: Statewide FFA chapters supporting Illinois agriculture students through leadership development, Supervised Agricultural Experiences (SAE), career development events, and state/national conferences.
internships.whyApply: Primary pipeline for Illinois students into agricultural careers with leadership experience and SAE projects.

internships.title: Discovery Partners Institute (DPI) Pre-College Programs
internships.description: Pre-college tech and innovation programs at DPI's Chicago hub. Students explore AI, data science, cybersecurity, and entrepreneurship through project-based learning with University of Illinois faculty and industry partners.
internships.whyApply: Flagship innovation hub for the University of Illinois system, located in the heart of downtown Chicago.

internships.title: Peggy Notebaert Nature Museum Youth Programs
internships.description: Programs for Chicago teens interested in urban ecology and environmental justice. Students engage in citizen science, exhibit engagement, and environmental advocacy in Lincoln Park.
internships.whyApply: Combines environmental science with urban ecology and environmental justice advocacy in Chicago.

internships.title: Becoming A Man (BAM)
internships.description: School-based counseling and mentoring program for young men in grades 7-12 across Chicago Public Schools. Focuses on social-emotional development, leadership, and community engagement with trained counselors.
internships.whyApply: Nationally recognized Chicago-based mentoring program with proven outcomes for young men's leadership development.

internships.title: JJ Vance Memorial Summer Internship
internships.company: University of Miami Miller School of Medicine (Hussman Institute for Human Genomics)
internships.description: Eight-week paid summer research program at UM Miller School of Medicine for Florida resident high school juniors interested in biological and computational sciences. Students are paired with faculty and research staff at the Hussman Institute for Human Genomics.
internships.whyApply: Rare paid high school research position in genomics at a top-tier academic medical center.

internships.title: CREATE Cancer Research Program
internships.company: Sylvester Comprehensive Cancer Center, University of Miami
internships.stipend: Stipend + housing on Coral Gables campus
internships.description: Mentored cancer biology research experience for rising juniors and seniors at Sylvester Comprehensive Cancer Center. Scholars work in faculty labs learning cutting-edge oncology tools and concepts, with stipend and housing provided.
internships.whyApply: Funded residential cancer research at a top NCI-designated cancer center in Florida.

internships.title: FIU Summer Research Internship Program (Cardiovascular Health)
internships.description: Eight-week cardiovascular health research program blending curriculum (case studies, journal clubs, workshops) with ~15 hours/week of hands-on lab research in FIU College of Arts, Sciences & Education.
internships.whyApply: Structured curriculum + mentored research at South Florida's largest public university.

internships.title: Center for Translational Science High School Summer Internship
internships.description: High school summer internship program at FIU's Center for Translational Science in Port St. Lucie. Students work alongside researchers on translational biomedical projects including drug discovery and infectious disease.
internships.whyApply: Exposure to real drug-discovery and translational research methodology at a university research center.

internships.title: Summer Youth Internship Program (SYIP)
internships.description: Five-week paid summer internship for rising 10th-12th grade Miami-Dade County Public Schools students. Places teens in real-world work sites across industries including healthcare, government, nonprofit, and business for career exploration.
internships.whyApply: Rare PAID county-sponsored summer work experience for Miami-Dade public school students.

internships.title: Summer Youth Internship Program
internships.description: Five-week paid clinical internship for rising 10th-12th grade Miami-Dade Public Schools students. Interns spend ~30 hours/week working with mentors at Nicklaus Children's, applying medical knowledge while assisting with patient care and observing clinical procedures.
internships.whyApply: Paid clinical exposure at one of the top pediatric hospitals in the Southeast.

internships.title: OSTEM High School Internship
internships.description: NASA Office of STEM Engagement (OSTEM) internship at Kennedy Space Center for high school students 16+. Interns work alongside NASA scientists and engineers on real spaceflight, aeronautics, and earth science projects. Fall, spring, and summer sessions available.
internships.whyApply: Work at NASA's primary launch facility — a resume-defining opportunity for aerospace-bound students.

internships.title: Florida Senate Page Program
internships.description: Week-long program during the Florida legislative session for students ages 15-18. Pages distribute legislative materials in the Senate Chamber and participate in a mock session where they draft legislation and learn the bill-filing process.
internships.whyApply: Direct immersion in Florida state government during active legislative session.

internships.title: HIP IMO (High School Internship in Integrated Mathematical Oncology)
internships.description: Eight-week interdisciplinary research program for students 16+ at Moffitt's Integrated Mathematical Oncology department. Interns complete an individual research project combining mathematical modeling and cancer biology, culminating in a 15-minute presentation and scientific report.
internships.whyApply: One of the few programs in the US that combines mathematical modeling with cancer research at the high school level.

internships.title: USF-Moffitt Explorers Oncology Program
internships.company: University of South Florida / Moffitt Cancer Center
internships.description: One-week immersive summer program for rising 11th-12th graders exploring cancer care, research, pharmacy, genetics, and modern healthcare at USF and Moffitt Cancer Center.
internships.whyApply: Broad introduction to oncology careers at a top cancer center — accessible one-week commitment.

internships.title: Conservation Teen Scientist (CTS)
internships.description: Volunteer program for high school students 14-18 to develop practical skills in environmental conservation, wildlife biology, zoo operations, and public engagement. Teens assist with guest interpretation and animal encounters.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on wildlife experience and public speaking practice at a major accredited zoo.

internships.title: SPARK Research Mentorship Program
internships.description: Mentored biomedical research program for rising juniors and seniors from Duval or St. Johns Counties. SPARK scholars design and carry out research projects within Discovery and Translation labs at Mayo Clinic, committing 25+ hours/week during summer.
internships.whyApply: Mayo Clinic research mentorship available only to local NE Florida students — high acceptance odds for motivated applicants.

internships.title: High School Volunteer Internship Program
internships.description: Volunteer internship at the Office of the Miami-Dade State Attorney Katherine Fernandez Rundle. High school students observe courtroom proceedings, work alongside Assistant State Attorneys, and gain exposure to the criminal justice system.
internships.whyApply: Direct exposure to working prosecutors and live courtroom proceedings.

internships.title: PAMM Teen Arts Council (PTAC)
internships.description: Teen-led, museum-facilitated council for high schoolers interested in creative arts. PTAC members develop programming, respond creatively to exhibitions, and host Free Second Saturdays: Teen Takeover events at PAMM.
internships.whyApply: Build a curatorial portfolio and arts leadership experience at a major contemporary art museum.

internships.title: High School Summer Research Internship
internships.company: Max Planck Florida Institute for Neuroscience
internships.description: Six-week paid research internship for high school juniors and seniors from Palm Beach or Martin Counties. Interns work on projects in Neuroscience, Scientific Programming, or Mechanical Engineering tracks alongside MPFI researchers.
internships.whyApply: One of very few high school neuroscience research internships in the US, at a Max Planck research institute.

internships.title: Teen Volunteer Program (TVP)
internships.description: Competitive marine science volunteer program where high schoolers act as Aquarium Guides at Mote. Teens receive training in marine science, Mote research, interpretation, and public speaking, then interpret exhibits and facilitate animal encounters.
internships.whyApply: Training and hands-on interpretation experience at one of Florida's top marine research institutions.

internships.title: JA Finance Park & Advanced Programs
internships.description: JA Finance Park is JA's capstone personal finance and career exploration program. Students complete 16-20 hours of classroom curriculum then visit JA Finance Park to simulate adult budgeting with assigned identities, income, and expenses. The advanced high school version adds long-term education, savings, and credit decisions.
internships.whyApply: Real-world budgeting simulation that teaches credit, taxes, and savings decisions — rare hands-on finance for teens.

internships.title: Student Science Training Program (SSTP)
internships.company: University of Florida Center for Precollegiate Education and Training
internships.description: Seven-week residential summer research program at UF for rising seniors considering science, medicine, math, computer science, or engineering careers. Students work with a UF faculty mentor on ongoing research, attend a lecture series, and take a UF honors seminar for potential dual enrollment credit.
internships.whyApply: One of the oldest and most respected residential high school research programs in the country.

internships.title: Florida 4-H Youth Development Program
internships.description: Statewide 4-H program administered by UF/IFAS Extension. Youth participate in hands-on projects spanning agriculture, animal science, food science, STEM/robotics, civic engagement, and mock government through community clubs, workshops, and county fairs.
internships.whyApply: Largest youth-development program in Florida — projects span agriculture, livestock, robotics, and civic engagement.

internships.title: Florida Attorney General's Internship Program
internships.company: Office of the Florida Attorney General
internships.description: Internship program at the Office of the Florida Attorney General with placements across divisions including consumer protection, civil rights, and criminal appeals. High school interns observe legal proceedings and support paralegal work at state offices in Tallahassee and other regional locations.
internships.whyApply: Exposure to statewide legal work at the office of Florida's top legal officer.

internships.title: Young Scholars Program (YSP)
internships.description: Six-week residential summer program for 40 Florida high school students with demonstrated ability in science and mathematics. Students work alongside FSU faculty on original research projects in physics, biology, chemistry, computer science, and engineering.
internships.whyApply: Florida's premier residential STEM research program for rising seniors, fully funded.

internships.title: Summer Scholars Program
internships.description: Pre-college program where rising juniors and seniors take UM undergraduate courses for college credit while living on campus. Over 20 academic tracks including marine science, business, health professions, and engineering.
internships.whyApply: Earn college credit at a top R1 university while exploring potential majors.

internships.title: Summer Aerospace Academy
internships.description: Residential aerospace program for high school students featuring hands-on flight simulation, aircraft design, rocketry, aviation maintenance, and meetings with pilots and aerospace engineers at the nation's leading aviation university.
internships.whyApply: Immersive aviation experience at the world's top aerospace university.

internships.title: Summer High School Programs
internships.description: STEM-focused residential and day camps for high school students in cybersecurity, data science, engineering, and computer science at Florida's only public polytechnic university.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on STEM experience at a specialized polytechnic university.

internships.title: Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Teens ages 13-17 serve as exhibit interpreters, help staff hands-on science demonstrations, assist with camp programs, and engage with museum visitors across physics, biology, and engineering exhibits.
internships.whyApply: Teach science to the public while building STEM communication skills.

internships.title: Museum Volunteers & Teen Programs
internships.company: Phillip and Patricia Frost Museum of Science
internships.description: Teens work in the aquarium, planetarium, and science galleries assisting with visitor engagement, live animal encounters, and hands-on STEM activities at Miami's flagship science museum.
internships.whyApply: Combines aquarium, planetarium, and museum experience at a top science institution.

internships.title: Junior Docent & Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Teens ages 14-17 train as junior docents to interpret marine exhibits, assist with animal care demonstrations, and support conservation education programs at one of the nation's top-ranked aquariums.
internships.whyApply: Direct marine conservation experience and animal interpretation training.

internships.title: Teen Zoo Crew
internships.description: Teens ages 13-17 assist zookeepers, lead interpretive talks for guests, help with enrichment activities for animals, and participate in conservation education at an accredited zoo caring for 1,300+ animals.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on zoo experience ideal for aspiring vets and wildlife biologists.

internships.title: Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Teens ages 14-17 engage guests at animal exhibits, assist with zoo camps, conduct biofact demonstrations, and support conservation outreach at Jacksonville's AZA-accredited zoo.
internships.whyApply: Build wildlife career skills at North Florida's premier zoo.

internships.title: Junior Achievement of Central Florida Programs
