internships.description: Programs including JA Company, JA Finance Park, and JA BizTown teach financial literacy, entrepreneurship, and workforce readiness. Teen participants start and run student-led businesses with mentorship from local executives.
internships.whyApply: Run an actual student business with real-world financial decisions and mentors.

internships.title: Junior Volunteer Program
internships.description: Teens ages 15-17 serve as junior volunteers in patient areas, assisting with wayfinding, clerical support, patient experience initiatives, and observing clinical workflows at West Central Florida's only Level I trauma center.
internships.whyApply: Direct clinical exposure at Tampa's academic Level I trauma center.

internships.title: Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Teens ages 15-17 serve in patient-facing and support roles across AdventHealth's flagship Orlando campus — one of the largest hospitals in the US — gaining insight into diverse medical specialties and hospital operations.
internships.whyApply: Healthcare exposure at one of America's largest hospital campuses.

internships.title: Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Teen volunteers support pediatric patients and families at Nemours Children's Hospital, assisting with playroom activities, family support, and wayfinding while experiencing pediatric healthcare firsthand.
internships.whyApply: Meaningful pediatric healthcare experience with children and families.

internships.title: Summer Research Experience for High School Students
internships.description: High school students are paired with FAU faculty mentors to conduct original research in biology, chemistry, neuroscience, ocean engineering, and data science, culminating in a research symposium presentation.
internships.whyApply: Mentored faculty research on a coastal R1 campus with a symposium.

internships.title: Upward Bound Math-Science
internships.description: Federally funded TRIO program serving first-generation and low-income Central Florida high school students with intensive STEM academic enrichment, tutoring, and a 6-week summer residential academy at UCF.
internships.whyApply: Federally funded STEM prep program for first-gen students at UCF.

internships.title: Teen Leadership Programs
internships.description: Teens participate in Keystone Club leadership development, Youth of the Year competition, Career Launch job readiness, and Junior Staff training while mentoring younger members at club sites across Miami-Dade County.
internships.whyApply: Build leadership skills while mentoring younger kids in your community.

internships.title: Teen Volunteer Opportunities
internships.description: Teens volunteer as gallery interpreters, living history actors, camp counselors, and digitization assistants at the Smithsonian Affiliate museum telling 12,000 years of Florida history.
internships.whyApply: Smithsonian-affiliated museum experience for aspiring historians.

internships.title: University Scholars Program
internships.description: Undergraduate research program that also accepts exceptional high school students. Participants work with UF faculty mentors on original research projects across all disciplines and publish in UF's Journal of Undergraduate Research.
internships.whyApply: Publish research in UF's undergraduate journal as a high schooler.

internships.title: Young Entrepreneurs Academy (YEA!)
internships.company: UCF College of Business / YEA Orlando
internships.description: 30-week program where middle and high school students develop an original business or social movement, write a business plan, pitch to investors, and launch an actual company with seed funding.
internships.whyApply: Actually launch a real business with investor funding as a student.

internships.title: PACE Center for Girls Teen Programs
internships.description: Teen volunteer and leadership opportunities at a nationally recognized nonprofit serving at-risk girls through education, counseling, and life skills. Volunteers tutor, mentor, and support programs at PACE sites statewide.
internships.whyApply: Mentor at-risk girls at a nationally recognized gender-responsive nonprofit.

internships.title: JJ Vance Memorial Summer Internship
internships.company: University of Miami Miller School of Medicine (HIHG)
internships.description: Paid 8-week summer research internship at the Hussman Institute for Human Genomics for outstanding juniors from Miami and Broward-area high schools. Interns pair with HIHG staff, postdocs, or faculty on biological and computational genetics research.
internships.whyApply: Paid genomics research at a top medical school, open only to local juniors.

internships.title: ACLU of Florida Summer Legal Internship
internships.description: Summer legal internship with the ACLU of Florida supporting civil liberties litigation and advocacy. Interns assist on First Amendment, voting rights, reproductive freedom, and criminal justice reform matters at offices in Miami, Tallahassee, or remotely.
internships.whyApply: Work on landmark civil liberties cases at one of the nation's most prominent legal advocacy organizations.

internships.title: GeneLab for High Schools (GL4HS)
internships.description: 12-week asynchronous summer program for rising juniors and seniors using real NASA spaceflight omics data. Students learn bioinformatics, space biology, and data analysis techniques used by NASA scientists.
internships.whyApply: Analyze authentic NASA space biology data from your own home under Ames scientist mentorship.

internships.title: Nemours Children's Hospital Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Hospital volunteer program for students 16+ who have completed sophomore year. Volunteers support patient care through age-appropriate assignments across the pediatric hospital, committing at least 75 hours over 6 consecutive months.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on pediatric hospital experience at a leading children's health system.

internships.title: Orlando Health ORMC Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Year-round teen volunteer program at Orlando's only Level 1 trauma center. Teens 16+ with 3.0+ GPA work in patient support, information desks, and clinical areas learning hospital operations firsthand.
internships.whyApply: Volunteer at Central Florida's Level 1 trauma center.

internships.title: Arnold Palmer Hospital Teen Volunteer Services
internships.description: Teen volunteer program at one of Florida's most prominent pediatric hospitals. Selected volunteers support child-life programming, family waiting areas, and patient experience functions after an extensive screening process.
internships.whyApply: Pediatric care experience at an iconic children's hospital.

internships.title: NAF Academy of Finance — Krop Senior High
internships.company: Michael M. Krop Senior High (NAF / Miami-Dade CPS)
internships.description: Four-year career-academy pathway at Krop HS preparing students grade 9-12 for careers in banking, accounting, investments, and financial planning. Culminates in a paid industry internship during junior or senior year.
internships.whyApply: Structured multi-year finance pathway with a real paid internship placement.

internships.title: Mote Marine Laboratory High School Internship
internships.description: Competitive 8-10 week summer program for Sarasota-Manatee area high school students to work on marine biology and ocean conservation research projects. Interns participate in fieldwork, data collection, and lab work alongside Mote scientists.
internships.whyApply: Direct marine research at one of the nation's most respected independent marine labs.

internships.title: Embry-Riddle SKY-CARE Summer Program
internships.description: Eight-week summer immersion in aviation and aerospace research for Volusia County high school students. Hands-on work in uncrewed aerial systems labs plus field trips to Kennedy Space Center and the Piper Aircraft Factory.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on aerospace engineering at the nation's leading aviation university.

internships.title: HIP-IMO: High School Internship in Integrated Mathematical Oncology
internships.description: Eight-week interdisciplinary cancer research internship for students 16+ at Moffitt's Integrated Mathematical Oncology department. Interns deliver a 15-minute research presentation and write a scientific report at program end.
internships.whyApply: Real mathematical oncology research at one of the world's top cancer centers.

internships.title: Moffitt VolunTeen Program
internships.description: Volunteer program for students ages 15-17 who have completed their first year of high school. Introduces teens to healthcare careers while providing hands-on volunteer experience at an NCI-designated comprehensive cancer center.
internships.whyApply: Early exposure to oncology careers at Florida's only NCI-designated cancer center.

internships.title: Jacksonville Zoo ZooTeens
internships.description: Year-round youth development volunteer program for teens 14-18. Volunteers conduct animal observations, lead educational activities, monitor behavioral data, and support camp programs. Earn 100+ service hours and documentation for scholarships.
internships.whyApply: Real animal care and conservation experience at an accredited zoo.

internships.title: Jacksonville Zoo W.I.L.D. Program
internships.description: Paid employment program hiring culturally diverse teens 14-19 to serve as Zoo ambassadors. Participants build job skills while leading outreach and conservation projects across Jacksonville communities.
internships.whyApply: Get paid to build job skills while representing the zoo in your community.

internships.title: Florida YMCA Youth in Government
internships.description: Statewide model-government program operating since 1957. High school delegates write, debate, and pass legislation in the real Florida Capitol chambers, filling roles as senators, representatives, press, and executive staff.
internships.whyApply: Debate bills you wrote on the actual Florida Senate and House floors.

internships.title: Florida YMCA Youth Advocate Program (YAP)
internships.description: Statewide hybrid advocacy training program for grades 9-12. Students develop real-world civic skills through workshops, networking, and special projects focused on legislative advocacy and policy change.
internships.whyApply: Learn how real advocacy campaigns work from statewide YMCA organizers.

internships.title: Florida Oceanographic Society Teen Environmental Program
internships.description: Teen environmental volunteer and internship opportunities including exhibit guide and conservation ambassador roles. Students gain hands-on experience with Indian River Lagoon ecosystem restoration, oyster reef building, and sea turtle conservation.
internships.whyApply: Restore one of North America's most biodiverse estuaries.

internships.title: Breakthrough Miami Teaching Fellows
internships.description: Paid summer teaching fellowship for high school and college students. Fellows teach academic subjects to middle-school students from under-resourced backgrounds under the 'students teaching students' model, gaining real classroom experience.
internships.whyApply: Lead your own classroom and change lives while getting paid.

internships.title: South Florida Water Management District Youth Volunteer
internships.description: Volunteer opportunities with the regional agency responsible for Everglades restoration and flood control across 16 counties. Youth volunteers assist with public outreach, stewardship events, and ecosystem monitoring.
internships.whyApply: Contribute to Everglades restoration, one of the largest environmental projects in U.S. history.

internships.title: Vizcaya Museum & Gardens Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: High school volunteers assist with public programs, tours, and community events at this historic National Historic Landmark estate, learning museum operations, horticulture, and Miami history.
internships.whyApply: Work inside a 1916 National Historic Landmark villa and gardens.

internships.title: Florida Museum of Natural History Junior Volunteer
internships.company: Florida Museum of Natural History, University of Florida
internships.description: Teens ages 13–17 volunteer at the state's official natural history museum, assisting visitors in exhibit halls, helping with youth programs like Collector's Day, and learning about paleontology, biodiversity, and anthropology.
internships.whyApply: Gain experience at a premier natural history museum inside a top public research university.

internships.title: The Ringling Student Volunteer Program
internships.company: The John and Mable Ringling Museum of Art
internships.description: Student volunteers at Florida's official state art museum help with public programs, educational events, and visitor engagement across the museum's art, circus, and historic house collections.
internships.whyApply: Work at Florida's state art museum inside a historic Gilded Age estate.

internships.title: Florida Holocaust Museum Student Docent Program
internships.description: High school students train as docents to lead tours, assist with educational programs, and engage visitors with Holocaust history and human rights themes at one of the largest Holocaust museums in the Southeast.
internships.whyApply: Teach visitors about Holocaust history while building public-speaking and historical-research skills.

internships.title: Tampa Museum of Art Teen Council
internships.description: Selective teen program where high school students plan events, curate teen-led exhibitions, and collaborate with museum staff on public programming, developing leadership and curatorial skills.
internships.whyApply: Curate exhibitions and plan programs at a leading Gulf Coast art museum.

internships.title: Kravis Center Young Friends of the Arts
internships.company: Raymond F. Kravis Center for the Performing Arts
internships.description: High school students engage with performing arts at a major regional venue, assisting with community programs, arts education initiatives, and behind-the-scenes operations for concerts, theater, and dance performances.
internships.whyApply: Get behind the curtain at South Florida's flagship performing arts center.

internships.title: Naples Botanical Garden Youth Volunteer
internships.description: Teens volunteer in conservation, horticulture, and visitor education at a 170-acre tropical botanical garden, participating in plant propagation, habitat restoration, and public outreach.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on experience with rare tropical plants and conservation practice.

internships.title: Bok Tower Gardens Teen Volunteer
internships.description: Teens assist with horticulture, endangered-plant conservation, and visitor programs at this historic National Historic Landmark garden and sanctuary home to the Rare Plant Conservation Program.
internships.whyApply: Work on conservation of endangered Florida native plants.

internships.title: Baptist Health South Florida Teen Volunteer
internships.description: High school students 16+ volunteer across Baptist Health's network of hospitals in Miami-Dade and Monroe counties, working in patient transport, reception, pharmacy support, and unit assistance while exploring healthcare careers.
internships.whyApply: Direct hospital exposure at South Florida's largest healthcare network.

internships.title: Cleveland Clinic Florida Volunteer Services (Teen)
internships.description: Teen volunteers (16+) serve in patient-facing and support roles at Cleveland Clinic's Florida campus, gaining exposure to clinical environments, physician-led programs, and hospital operations.
internships.whyApply: Volunteer with one of the nation's top-ranked hospital systems.

internships.title: Joe DiMaggio Children's Hospital Junior Volunteer
internships.company: Memorial Healthcare System / Joe DiMaggio Children's Hospital
internships.description: High school students age 15+ volunteer at one of South Florida's leading pediatric hospitals, assisting in child-life activities, family support areas, and non-clinical departments while exploring pediatric medicine.
internships.whyApply: Pediatric hospital experience ideal for aspiring children's medicine careers.

internships.title: Halifax Health Junior Volunteer
internships.description: Junior volunteer program for students 14–17 at Volusia County's largest hospital system, with placements in patient transport, visitor services, gift shop, and nursing unit support.
internships.whyApply: Entry-level hospital exposure for East Coast Florida students.

internships.title: Florida FFA Leadership Programs
internships.description: Agricultural education, leadership camps, and SAE (Supervised Agricultural Experience) projects for middle and high school students across Florida, with hands-on ag science training and state conventions.
internships.whyApply: Develop real-world agriculture and leadership experience.

internships.title: Florida Department of Environmental Protection Volunteer
internships.description: Teen and family volunteers assist at Florida's 175+ state parks with trail maintenance, wildlife monitoring, interpretive programs, and resource management projects across the state.
internships.whyApply: Gain park ranger–style experience across Florida's state park system.

internships.title: Orlando Science Center Teen Leader Program
internships.description: Teens serve as youth educators and floor leaders, assisting with interactive exhibits and hands-on STEM workshops for visitors while building teaching, communication, and leadership skills.
internships.whyApply: Teach STEM concepts to young visitors at a major science museum.

internships.title: Children's Hospital of Philadelphia (CHOP) Volunteer Program
internships.description: High school and college volunteers (15+) serve in patient-facing and non-clinical roles at the nation's #1-ranked pediatric hospital, including family waiting areas, child life activities, and clinic support.
internships.whyApply: Work at the #1 children's hospital in the country.

internships.title: University of Pittsburgh Medical Center (UPMC) Teen Volunteer
internships.description: Teen volunteer program (14+) across UPMC's network of academic hospitals, with placements in patient transport, gift shops, ICU waiting rooms, and pediatric units.
internships.whyApply: Hospital exposure at western PA's largest integrated health system.

internships.title: Carnegie Mellon University SAMS (Summer Academy for Math & Science)
internships.description: Free, residential six-week summer program for rising high school seniors from underrepresented backgrounds offering intensive STEM coursework, research projects, and college prep at CMU.
internships.whyApply: Fully funded intensive STEM program at a top engineering university.

internships.title: University of Pennsylvania Management and Technology Summer Institute
internships.description: Selective three-week residential program for high school students combining business (Wharton) and engineering (SEAS) coursework, including finance, marketing, and robotics projects.
internships.whyApply: Unique dual-degree exposure combining Wharton and Penn Engineering.

internships.title: Philadelphia District Attorney's Office Youth Advisory Council
internships.description: Paid youth council program where Philadelphia high school students advise the DA's office on criminal justice policy, community engagement, and juvenile justice reform initiatives.
internships.whyApply: Shape criminal justice policy from inside a major prosecutor's office.

internships.title: MITES Summer
internships.stipend: Fully funded — all tuition, housing, and meals covered
internships.description: Six-week residential STEM enrichment program for rising high school seniors from underrepresented or underserved backgrounds. Students take rigorous college-level courses in math, physics, biology, chemistry, humanities, and engineering design while living on MIT's campus and working directly with MIT faculty and staff.
internships.whyApply: One of the most prestigious free pre-college STEM programs in the country. MITES alumni have exceptional college admissions outcomes and lifelong MIT community access.

internships.title: MITES Semester
internships.description: Six-month virtual program (June-December) for rising high school seniors. Provides the rigor of MITES Summer in a flexible online format: college-level STEM courses, mentorship from MIT faculty and students, and a culminating independent project. Includes a one-week on-campus experience in Cambridge.
internships.whyApply: All the benefits of MITES without having to relocate — exceptional mentorship and college prep for students who can't commit to a residential summer.

internships.title: Beaver Works Summer Institute (BWSI)
internships.company: MIT Lincoln Laboratory / MIT Beaver Works
internships.stipend: Free — no tuition charged for accepted students
internships.description: Four-week project-based summer institute for rising high school seniors. Tracks include autonomous RACECAR, cog*works, embedded security and hardware hacking, remote sensing for disaster response, assistive technology, UAV and drone engineering, quantum computing, and more. Students complete a capstone challenge with MIT Lincoln Lab engineers.
internships.whyApply: Unique hands-on engineering experience with MIT Lincoln Lab — one of the few advanced technology programs that is both free and residential at MIT.

internships.title: PRIMES (Program for Research in Mathematics, Engineering and Science)
internships.description: Year-long research program for high school sophomores and juniors in the Greater Boston area. Students are paired with MIT graduate student mentors to work on original research projects in mathematics, theoretical computer science, and computational biology, culminating in research papers presented at the PRIMES conference.
internships.whyApply: Produces genuine original research with MIT mentors — PRIMES students regularly win top prizes at Regeneron STS, ISEF, and other national research competitions.

internships.title: PROMYS (Program in Mathematics for Young Scientists)
internships.stipend: Need-based financial aid up to 100% of costs
internships.description: Six-week intensive residential program in number theory and higher mathematics for high school students. Daily problem sets, lectures, and discussions with university mathematicians push students to discover mathematics through exploration. Application includes a challenging problem set.
internships.whyApply: Considered one of the premier math enrichment programs in the world. Strong emphasis on discovery-based learning and a guiding principle of 'no financial barriers to participation.'

internships.title: Research Internship and Summer Experience (RISE)
internships.stipend: Paid tuition program ($75 application fee; financial aid available)
internships.description: Six-week selective pre-college research program for rising seniors. Two tracks: the Internship Track places students in a BU research lab under a faculty or postdoc mentor (biology, chemistry, physics, computer science, neuroscience, astronomy, public health, psychology, engineering, and more); the Practicum Track offers a structured Computational Neurobiology course. Culminates in a research poster symposium.
internships.whyApply: One of the best structured high school research experiences on the East Coast. Final poster session gives students a taste of real academic research communication.

internships.title: Harvard Pre-College Program
internships.stipend: Paid program ($6,100/session + $75 application fee; limited financial aid)
internships.description: Two-week residential pre-college program for rising juniors and seniors. Non-credit courses taught by Harvard faculty with small class sizes (~15 students). Students receive Harvard transcripts and written evaluations. Three sessions offered throughout the summer across disciplines from medicine to creative writing.
internships.whyApply: Live and learn on Harvard's campus with world-class faculty and a curated cohort of ambitious peers from around the world.

internships.title: Harvard Secondary School Program (SSP)
internships.stipend: Paid tuition ($4,180-$15,735 depending on format; limited scholarships)
internships.description: Seven-week or four-week credit-bearing summer program for high school students. Take real Harvard College courses (200+ options in the 7-week session) taught by Harvard faculty. Residential, commuter, and online formats available. Earn college credit that can transfer to many universities.
internships.whyApply: Earn actual college credit from Harvard while experiencing college-level academics alongside current undergraduates.

internships.title: WPI Frontiers
internships.description: Two-week residential pre-college program for rising 10th-12th graders. Students explore a STEM or Business major (robotics, biotechnology, computer science, aerospace engineering, game development, and more) paired with a humanities, arts, or business minor. Includes college-prep workshops, field trips, and hands-on lab work at WPI's Bartlett Center.
internships.whyApply: Project-based learning in WPI's hallmark style — students spend most of the day in labs building real things rather than sitting in lectures.

internships.title: Boston Leadership Institute Summer Research Programs
internships.stipend: Paid program ($699 one-week to $4,597 three-week residential)
internships.description: Three-week intensive research programs and one-week intro courses for middle and high school students. Over 25 programs spanning medical research, biotech, engineering, robotics, forensics, neuroscience, and business. Students conduct independent research projects culminating in presentations. Includes field trips to Boston hospitals, labs, and companies.
internships.whyApply: Unusual breadth of research topics for middle and high schoolers, and one of the few Boston-area programs that actively serves rising middle schoolers alongside high school students.

internships.title: Research Science Institute (RSI)
internships.company: Center for Excellence in Education / MIT
internships.stipend: Fully funded (tuition, room, board, travel — free to participants)
internships.description: Six-week cost-free summer research program hosted at MIT. 100 of the most accomplished rising high school seniors worldwide. One week of intensive STEM coursework with MIT faculty, followed by five weeks conducting individual research under scientists and engineers in MIT labs, area universities, and companies. Culminates in a written research paper and symposium presentation.
internships.whyApply: Widely considered the most selective free high school STEM research program in the world. ~5% acceptance rate. RSI alumni dominate Regeneron STS, ISEF, and top college STEM admissions.

internships.title: MIT THINK Scholars Program
internships.stipend: Up to $1,000 project funding + all-expenses-paid trip to MIT
internships.description: National STEM research initiative run by MIT undergraduates. High school students submit original project proposals; 4-6 finalists receive mentorship from MIT students and faculty, up to $1,000 in seed funding to implement their project, and an all-expenses-paid trip to MIT for lab visits, class attendance, and research presentations. Open to U.S. high schoolers.
internships.whyApply: One of the only programs that funds and mentors high schoolers to actually build and execute their own original STEM projects, rather than slotting them into a pre-existing lab.

internships.title: MathROOTS
internships.stipend: Free (fully funded — no tuition, travel covered)
internships.description: Two-week residential summer program at MIT for high-potential high school students from underrepresented backgrounds or underserved communities who have a talent for mathematics. Intense mathematical problem-solving, lectures by MIT faculty and graduate students, and mentorship. Free to admitted students.
internships.whyApply: Highly selective, fully funded, designed specifically to open math research careers to talented students from backgrounds underrepresented in the mathematical sciences.

internships.title: Brandeis Genesis Precollege Program
internships.stipend: $5,850 tuition; need- and merit-based scholarships + travel stipends up to $500
internships.description: Three-week pluralistic, academically focused Jewish learning and identity-building summer precollege program for students ages 15-18. Participants take college-level courses (2 hours/day, 5 days/week), participate in Shabbat celebrations, explorations, spiritual practice, evening activities, and weekly Boston-area trips while residing on the Brandeis campus.
internships.whyApply: Rare residential humanities-focused precollege program at a top research university with substantial need- and merit-based aid available.

internships.title: Harvard Summer School — Secondary School Program
internships.stipend: $4,180-$15,735 depending on format (online/commuter/residential); limited financial aid
internships.description: Seven-week (June 20-Aug 8) and four-week (July 12-Aug 8) credit-bearing programs where motivated high school students take actual Harvard Summer School courses alongside undergraduates. 200+ course options across humanities, social sciences, sciences, and math. Residential, commuting, and online formats available.
internships.whyApply: Students earn real Harvard credit and experience college-level academics at one of the world's most prestigious universities, with broad course selection across nearly every discipline.

internships.title: Young Scholars Program (YSP)
internships.company: Northeastern University Center for STEM Education
internships.description: Six-week commuter research program for Massachusetts high school juniors entering 12th grade. Students conduct hands-on research in Northeastern's engineering, science, and health sciences laboratories. Program runs late June through late July, Monday-Thursday 8:30am-4:30pm, and includes career seminars, college counseling, and field trips to corporate and government research sites. Limited to permanent MA residents within commuting distance.
internships.whyApply: One of the best free research opportunities exclusively for Massachusetts high school students, with genuine lab research and strong college counseling support built in.

internships.title: Junior Summer Volunteer Program
internships.description: Eight-week volunteer program for rising high school juniors and seniors, with preference given to Boston residents. Volunteers commit to at least one 3-hour weekly shift in outpatient settings (ambassador programs, food pantry, pediatric clinics) plus a mandatory weekly lecture series delivered by Boston Medical Center Health Systems professionals. Program accepts ~70 students; fills quickly.
internships.whyApply: Genuine hospital exposure inside New England's largest safety-net hospital, with a structured weekly lecture series from actual clinicians — excellent for students exploring medicine.

internships.title: Babson Summer Study for High School Students
internships.stipend: Tuition varies; $95 application fee (waived for need-based aid applicants); partial scholarships available
internships.description: Three-week experiential pre-college program for rising high school juniors and seniors (must be at least 15 by start date). Students earn 4 college credits while exploring social, economic, and environmental problems through an entrepreneurial lens framed by the UN Global Goals. Covers entrepreneurship, leadership, marketing, finance, business communication, and venture creation. Residential, commuter, and online options.
internships.whyApply: Babson is #1-ranked for entrepreneurship in the U.S., and this is one of the few pre-college programs that actually awards college credit while immersing students in real business and venture-creation coursework.

internships.title: Tufts Summer Research Experience
internships.stipend: Tuition applies; limited financial aid (scholarships typically covering 75-100% of program fee based on demonstrated need)
internships.description: Hands-on collegiate research program for high school students on the Tufts Medford campus. Students work directly with Tufts researchers and faculty on real projects across sciences and engineering. Residential and commuter options available. Part of Tufts University College's pre-college portfolio.
internships.whyApply: Authentic university research experience at a top-30 research university in the Boston area, with a structured mentor match and financial aid for qualifying students.

internships.title: Tufts Pre-College Mini Med School
internships.stipend: Tuition applies; limited financial aid available (75-100% scholarships for qualifying need)
internships.description: Intensive pre-college program introducing high school students to medicine, clinical practice, and medical school coursework through lectures, interactive case studies, and simulation exercises taught by Tufts School of Medicine faculty. Students explore anatomy, physiology, pathology, and the daily realities of being a physician.
internships.whyApply: Direct access to Tufts Medical School faculty and curriculum, including clinical simulation exercises — far more immersive than most shadowing or lecture-only pre-med programs.

internships.title: Northeastern Accelerate Pre-College Programs
internships.stipend: Tuition varies by program; financial aid available
internships.description: Immersive residential and online pre-college programs for rising juniors and seniors led by Northeastern faculty on the Boston campus. Offerings span STEM, business, global affairs, health sciences, design, and the humanities. Students engage in experiential, project-based coursework aligned with Northeastern's signature co-op/experiential learning model.
internships.whyApply: Northeastern's signature experiential-learning approach applied to high schoolers — programs emphasize real-world problems and professional skills, not just lectures.

internships.title: Summer Science and Engineering Program (SSEP)
internships.description: Two-week residential precollege program for exceptional high school students (grades 9-12) interested in science and engineering. Lab-based courses taught by Smith faculty across life sciences, physical sciences, mathematics, and engineering. Two session options each summer.
internships.whyApply: One of the longest-running residential STEM programs for young women in the country. Hands-on faculty-led lab courses on a beautiful liberal arts campus.

internships.title: Broad Summer Scholars Program (BSSP)
internships.company: Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard
internships.description: Six-week paid summer research program for rising seniors attending high school in Massachusetts within commuting distance of the Broad Institute. Scholars are paired with Broad scientists for hands-on genomics, computational biology, and biomedical research projects. Includes scientific talks, a poster session, and college preparation activities.
internships.whyApply: Direct access to one of the world's leading genomics research institutes. Paid research experience with Broad scientists — exceptional college admissions credential.

internships.title: Hampshire College Summer Studies in Mathematics (HCSSiM)
internships.description: Six-week intensive residential program in college-level mathematics for talented and highly motivated high school students. Focus on actively doing mathematics rather than just learning results. Daily structure includes 4 hours of morning classes (Mon-Sat), Prime Time Theorem, and evening problem sessions. Students live in Hampshire College dorms.
internships.whyApply: One of the most respected and longest-running residential math programs in the country (54+ years). Strong pipeline to top STEM colleges and a tight-knit mathematical community.

internships.title: Pre-College Summer Studios
internships.company: Massachusetts College of Art and Design (MassArt)
internships.description: Four-week immersive studio art program for students entering grades 11-12 passionate about the visual arts. Students choose a major studio area (painting, sculpture, illustration, animation, etc.) plus electives, and work alongside MassArt faculty in MassArt's Boston studios. Strong portfolio-building focus for art-school applicants.
internships.whyApply: MassArt is the only freestanding public art college in the US. Pre-College Summer Studios is one of the strongest portfolio-building experiences in the Northeast for art-school applicants.

internships.title: UMass Amherst Summer Pre-College Programs
internships.description: UMass Amherst's University Without Walls offers 25+ residential and online summer pre-college programs for high school students, including six-week immersive Research Intensives in biology and food science (lab-based). Programs span academic disciplines from data science to creative writing to sustainability.
internships.whyApply: Public R1 university experience with strong research intensives and broad subject coverage. Residential and online options accommodate different students' needs and budgets.

internships.title: Aspire: Five-Week Music Performance Intensive
internships.description: Berklee's flagship five-week residential summer program for musicians ages 15+ from over 70 countries. Includes weekly private lessons on principal instrument, ensemble placements (jazz, pop/rock, funk/fusion, salsa, bluegrass, world music), elective coursework in songwriting/production/business, visiting artist clinics, and 200+ final-week concerts. College credit option available.
internships.whyApply: The world's most comprehensive summer music performance program — direct access to Berklee faculty, visiting artists, and a global community of serious young musicians.

internships.title: Wentworth ImpactLab
internships.description: Two-week or four-week residential pre-college engineering and design program for students entering grades 11-12. Non-credit Wentworth-faculty-led instruction across engineering, computer science, architecture, and design disciplines. Includes hands-on labs, networking with peers internationally, social activities, and workshops.
internships.whyApply: Project-based engineering and design exposure on a hands-on technical campus right in the heart of Boston's Fenway. Strong fit for students considering applied engineering programs.

internships.title: NEC Summer Programs (Orchestra / Vocal / Jazz / Chamber / Band Institutes)
internships.stipend: Tuition varies; full-scholarship Chamber Music Young Artist Program available
internships.description: New England Conservatory's residential summer institutes for ages 13-18, including the Summer Orchestra Institute (4-week full symphony program), Summer Vocal Institute (2 weeks), Summer Band Institute (1 week, wind/brass/percussion), Jazz Lab (1 week), and the fully-funded Summer Chamber Music Young Artist Program (9-day immersive festival). Conservatory-level instruction with NEC faculty.
internships.whyApply: NEC is one of the oldest and most prestigious music conservatories in the country. The summer institutes give serious young classical/jazz musicians direct conservatory-style training and exposure to NEC faculty.

internships.title: Launch Summer Day Camp
internships.stipend: Tuition-based; $125,000+ in K-12 financial aid available
internships.description: WPI's pre-collegiate summer day camp for grades 9-11 offering hands-on, project-based STEM exploration in WPI's signature project-based style. Students work in teams on engineering and science challenges with WPI faculty and staff at the Bartlett Center on the WPI campus.
internships.whyApply: Direct exposure to WPI's renowned project-based learning model — a great preview for students considering applied engineering and STEM colleges. Significant K-12 financial aid available.

internships.title: Princeton Summer Journalism Program
internships.stipend: Fully funded — travel, housing, meals, and equipment covered
internships.description: Year-long college-prep program for high-achieving high school juniors from low-income backgrounds interested in journalism. Online workshops begin in late June culminating in a 10-day residential experience on Princeton's campus in late July/early August. Students work with professional journalists from major national outlets on reporting, writing, photojournalism, and multimedia projects.
internships.whyApply: Fully funded program taught by top journalists. Strong alumni pipeline into college journalism programs and media careers.

internships.title: Laboratory Learning Program
internships.description: Free science outreach program that places local NJ high school students in active Princeton research laboratories for a 5-6 week independent research experience over the summer. Students work alongside faculty, postdocs, and graduate students on real research projects in physics, chemistry, biology, engineering, and more. Open to students who live within commuting distance of Princeton.
internships.whyApply: Rare opportunity to conduct genuine research in a Princeton lab as a high schooler at no cost. Excellent preparation for selective STEM college apps.

internships.title: James Madison Program Summer Seminar
internships.stipend: No tuition; students responsible for travel
internships.description: One-week residential seminar for upper-level high school students and rising college first-years on the principles of American politics, constitutionalism, and political philosophy. Students engage in close readings of foundational texts and Socratic discussions led by Princeton faculty. Explores the fundamental questions of equality, liberty, and civic life.
internships.whyApply: Rigorous exposure to foundational texts of American political thought with Princeton faculty. Excellent for students interested in law, government, or political philosophy.

internships.title: Princeton University Preparatory Program (PUPP)
internships.description: Intensive three-year college preparation program for high-achieving students from low-income backgrounds attending one of five local high schools (Ewing, Lawrence, Nottingham, Princeton, Trenton Central). Includes summer institutes on Princeton's campus, year-round academic enrichment, cultural programming, college counseling, and application support.
internships.whyApply: Comprehensive, fully funded college prep pipeline with strong outcomes to selective universities. Substantial academic and personal support.

internships.title: Governor's School in the Sciences
internships.company: Drew University / New Jersey Governor's School
internships.description: Tuition-free three-week residential program for high-achieving New Jersey high school juniors interested in sciences. Hosted at Drew University. Students take advanced courses in physics, chemistry, biology, computer science, and mathematics, and complete a team research project culminating in a public presentation. In session July 12 - August 1, 2026.
internships.whyApply: Highly prestigious, tuition-free NJ program. Nomination-based — strong signal to colleges. Accelerated coursework with faculty-mentored research.

internships.title: Governor's School of Engineering & Technology
internships.company: Rutgers University / New Jersey Governor's School
internships.description: Tuition-free residential summer program for top New Jersey high school juniors interested in engineering and technology, hosted at Rutgers School of Engineering. Students work on real-world engineering research projects in teams mentored by Rutgers faculty and graduate students, attend technical lectures, industry field trips, and present final research at a public symposium.
internships.whyApply: One of the most selective and prestigious engineering programs for NJ high schoolers. Nomination-based, tuition-free, and a strong credential for engineering college applications.

internships.title: Stevens Pre-College Summer Residential Program
internships.stipend: Paid program — all-inclusive tuition covering housing, meals, activities
internships.description: One- and two-week residential non-credit STEM programs on Stevens' 55-acre campus in Hoboken (across the Hudson from NYC). Hands-on coursework with Stevens faculty in engineering, computer science, data science, business, and more. Includes extracurricular activities, Manhattan boat cruise, dining in Hoboken, and NYC access. Priority deadline March 16; final deadline June 1.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on STEM immersion at a top engineering school with NYC access. Alumni eligible for $5K-$10K Pre-College Excellence Scholarship at Stevens.

internships.title: Summer STEMx Residency
internships.description: Residential summer program for 10th and 11th grade students at NJIT's Newark campus. Students live on campus and engage in hands-on STEM coursework, labs, and enrichment activities led by NJIT faculty. Offered through the NJIT Center for Pre-College Programs, which has served 3,000+ secondary students annually since 1979.
internships.whyApply: Long-running (since 1979) NJIT pre-college program with strong STEM reputation in the NY metro area.

internships.title: Summer STEMx High School Programs
internships.description: Day-program summer sessions for 10th and 11th grade students on NJIT's Newark campus. Hands-on STEM coursework, labs, and career exposure delivered by NJIT faculty and staff. Part of NJIT's long-established Center for Pre-College Programs.
internships.whyApply: Accessible NJIT STEM immersion without residential cost. Good stepping stone for NJ/NY metro students exploring engineering and CS.

internships.title: Partners in Science
internships.description: Intensive eight-week summer research program for rising high school juniors and seniors. Students are paired with mentors in professional research laboratories at Rutgers, Columbia, NJIT, Stevens, and other top institutions to conduct authentic scientific research in biology, chemistry, physics, and engineering. Program has run for over three decades.
internships.whyApply: Rare chance to be placed in active research labs at major universities as a high schooler, with mentorship, stipend, and 30+ year program track record.

internships.title: Governor's STEM Scholars
internships.company: Research & Development Council of New Jersey
internships.description: Year-long academic program for New Jersey's top STEM students from 10th grade through college. Participants attend quarterly statewide conferences featuring industry leaders, policymakers, researchers, and company site visits. Students complete a cohort research project presented at the final State House showcase. Connects participants with STEM mentors across academia, industry, and government.
internships.whyApply: Unique access to NJ's STEM ecosystem — government, industry, and academia — with cohort-based research and high-visibility State House presentation.

internships.title: Rutgers Young Scholars Program in Discrete Mathematics (DIMACS YSP)
internships.description: Intensive four-week residential summer program for mathematically talented high school students that has run at Rutgers nearly every year since 1990. Students get a rigorous introduction to discrete mathematics — combinatorics, graph theory, number theory, probability, and cryptography — with applications at the cutting edge of modern research. Applicants solve a set of math problems as part of the application.
internships.whyApply: One of the most respected math enrichment programs in the country. 35+ year track record, rigorous problem-based application, and strong preparation for math competitions and top STEM college apps.

internships.title: Waksman Student Scholars Program (WSSP) / WISE Summer Institute
internships.company: Waksman Institute of Microbiology, Rutgers University
internships.description: Year-long authentic molecular biology and bioinformatics research program for high school students and their teachers. Begins with the WISE (Waksman Institute Summer Experience) — a two-week non-residential summer institute at the Waksman Institute — then continues at students' home high schools during the academic year. Students use DNA purification, PCR, restriction digests, and gel electrophoresis to isolate and characterize novel DNA fragments from the duckweed Landoltia punctata. Student-authored DNA sequences are published on GenBank, the international DNA sequence repository.
internships.duration: 2-week summer institute + year-long at home school
internships.whyApply: Rare opportunity for high school students to publish authentic DNA sequence data on GenBank with their name attached. Deep introduction to real wet-lab molecular biology and bioinformatics.

internships.title: Rutgers Future Scholars Program
internships.stipend: Fully funded; full tuition scholarship to Rutgers on successful completion
internships.description: Five-year college access program that begins in 8th grade and continues through college graduation. Serves first-generation and low-income students in Camden, Newark, New Brunswick/Piscataway, and Rahway. Includes academic enrichment, tutoring, mentoring, cultural programming, summer institutes, college counseling, and application support. Scholars who complete the program and enroll at Rutgers receive full tuition funding.
internships.whyApply: Comprehensive, fully funded multi-year pipeline with a tuition guarantee to Rutgers. One of the country's first and most successful university-based college access programs.

internships.title: Princeton AI4ALL
internships.stipend: Fully funded — housing, meals, and travel covered
internships.description: Free three-week residential summer program on Princeton's campus for rising 11th-grade low-income students from across the U.S. and Puerto Rico. Students learn the technical and ethical foundations of artificial intelligence through lectures, hands-on research projects led by AI graduate students, and field trips — including a two-day visit to Washington, D.C. to explore AI and public policy. Program costs (housing, meals, DC trip, and travel assistance) are fully covered.
internships.whyApply: Rare fully funded residential AI program at Princeton with mentorship from AI graduate students, hands-on research projects, and a DC policy trip. Strong signal for selective CS programs.

internships.title: Young Women's Conference in STEM (YWC)
internships.description: Free day-long STEM conference for girls in grades 7-10, hosted at Princeton University by the U.S. Department of Energy's Princeton Plasma Physics Laboratory. Introduces students to female scientists and engineers through small-group presentations, hands-on activities, chemistry demonstrations, and a keynote. In its most recent year nearly 900 students attended — the largest in the conference's 23-year history.
internships.whyApply: Rare entry-point STEM exposure for middle and early high school girls at a national lab on Princeton's campus. Free, well-established, and directly connected to women scientists and engineers from industry, academia, and government.

internships.title: High School Career Explorer Program
internships.description: Career exploration program for rising high school seniors across New Jersey interested in health-care careers. Students are matched with professional coaches and experience 'a day in the life' of clinical and administrative roles within the Hackensack Meridian Health network — the largest health system in New Jersey. Participation requires attending a virtual information session, background check, and health screening.
internships.whyApply: Direct shadowing access to one of NJ's largest health systems — valuable exposure for students considering medicine, nursing, or allied health.

internships.title: Junior Volunteer Program
internships.description: Year-round hospital volunteer program for NJ teens ages 16-17 across Hackensack Meridian Health's network of hospitals and medical centers (including Hackensack, JFK, Jersey Shore, Ocean, and Palisades). Volunteers commit to a minimum of four hours per week and at least 80 hours per year, supporting patients and staff in a variety of clinical and administrative settings. Requires orientation, health screening, and background check.
internships.whyApply: Accessible entry point to hospital-based clinical exposure for pre-med students across New Jersey. Patient-contact hours that count toward medical school applications.

internships.title: New Jersey Scholars Program (NJSP)
internships.stipend: Fully funded — tuition, room, board, and all academic expenses covered
internships.description: Tuition-free five-week interdisciplinary residential summer program for 39 rising high school seniors who are New Jersey residents. Held at The Lawrenceville School, the program deep-dives each year into a single theme (past topics: Greece in the Age of Pericles, Russian Studies, the Middle Ages, the 20th Century), taught by college-level faculty. Each NJ high school may nominate up to two applicants through their school counselor. Applicants submit essays, a graded paper, and two letters of recommendation.
internships.whyApply: Exceptionally selective, fully funded humanities seminar at one of the country's top prep schools. Strong credential for top liberal arts and Ivy humanities applicants.

internships.title: Lawrenceville Summer Scholars
internships.description: Three-week summer academic enrichment program for rising 6th-9th graders held on Lawrenceville's campus. Students can choose day or residential tracks and experience boarding-school life with courses spanning humanities, math, science, arts, and athletics. Designed to help younger students build independence and join an international community of peers.
internships.whyApply: Immersive boarding school experience on a top-tier campus for middle school and early high school students. Good bridge program for families considering independent secondary schools.

internships.title: McCarter Theatre High School Intensive
internships.description: Three-week musical theater intensive for dedicated high school performers held at McCarter Theatre Center and on Princeton University's campus. Participants work with professional teaching artists to devise and perform a new work, developing performance, writing, music, and devising skills. Follows McCarter's tradition of developing new work and gives students a taste of the professional creation process.
internships.whyApply: Rigorous training from a Tony Award-winning regional theater with a reputation for premiering new work. Strong credential for students pursuing theater or musical theater in college.

internships.title: High School Assistants in Training (AIT)
internships.description: Summer volunteer program for high school students entering 10th grade and above who want to experience the behind-the-scenes work of producing theater. Assistants in Training learn directly from professional stage managers, teaching artists, and designers at McCarter Theatre Center while supporting the youth summer camps. Strong pathway for students interested in arts administration, production, or education.
internships.whyApply: Rare behind-the-scenes mentorship opportunity at a top regional theater. Excellent for students interested in the production side of performing arts.

internships.title: Rutgers Business School Summer Business Camp
internships.description: One-week experientially driven residential business camp for high school students on Rutgers' Livingston Campus. Campers explore core business disciplines — marketing, management, accounting, finance, and supply chain — through case studies, company visits, and team projects led by Rutgers Business School faculty and students. Participants live on campus for a preview of college business-school life.
internships.whyApply: Accessible, well-structured exposure to college-level business curriculum at a top-ranked public business school with strong Wall Street recruiting ties.

internships.title: Explorations in Neuroscience Research Internship
internships.company: Ohio State University College of Medicine
internships.description: Seven-week paid summer program at The Ohio State University funded by the NIH. Participants are placed in a neuroscience or biomedical research lab and work alongside faculty, graduate students, and technicians on ongoing research projects. Includes professional development, seminars, and a research symposium.
internships.whyApply: Paid, NIH-funded neuroscience research at a major R1 university — rare combination of mentorship and real lab work for high schoolers.

internships.title: AWS Foundation & Mₐ²JIC High School Internship Program
internships.company: Manufacturing & Materials Joining Innovation Center (Ma²JIC), The Ohio State University
internships.description: Paid on-campus summer internship for high school students interested in welding engineering, materials joining, manufacturing processes, and engineering design. Interns contribute to ongoing research projects under faculty, graduate researchers, and industry partners at the Mₐ²JIC center at Ohio State.
internships.whyApply: One of the only paid high school internships in welding/manufacturing engineering at a top-ranked engineering college. Industry-aligned research with real deliverables.

internships.title: CYCE High School Summer Internship Program
internships.description: Paid 8-week summer internship (June–July) providing high school students with engaging, real-world work experience in clinical, non-clinical, and advanced technology healthcare tracks. Clinical areas include Behavioral Health, Lab Medicine, Nursing, Pharmacy, Physical Therapy, Radiology, and Respiratory Care. Non-clinical tracks cover Community Health, Finance, Healthcare Administration, HR, and more.
internships.whyApply: Paid, structured, and deeply varied hospital internship at a top-5 US hospital. Strong eligibility guardrail (NE Ohio residents) means local students have a real shot at a competitive program.

internships.title: NASA Glenn High School Engineering Institute
internships.description: Week-long engineering institute for highly motivated high school students with strong STEM credentials. Participants use the Engineering Design Process to design and test prototypes in NASA Glenn focus areas: acoustic damping, power management and distribution, and simulated lunar operations. 60 students per summer across three weeks in July.
internships.whyApply: Direct exposure to NASA engineers and facilities. One of the few ways for high schoolers to work inside a federal aerospace research center.

internships.title: Young Writers Summer Residential Workshop
internships.company: The Kenyon Review at Kenyon College
internships.description: Prestigious two-week residential creative writing workshop at Kenyon College for rising juniors and seniors (ages 16-18). Students live in college dorms, write daily in small workshop groups led by published writers, and develop work in fiction, poetry, and creative nonfiction. Two sessions: June 21–July 4 and July 12–25, 2026.
internships.whyApply: One of the nation's most respected high school creative writing programs, run by The Kenyon Review — a prestigious literary journal. Strong signal on college applications for humanities-focused students.

internships.title: Medical Sciences Summer Institute (MSSI)
internships.company: University of Cincinnati College of Medicine
internships.description: Week-long full-day summer sessions for rising juniors and seniors interested in medicine and healthcare. Hands-on biomedical research in cellular/molecular biology or the new medical informatics track (AI, data science, and healthcare analytics). Multiple one-week sessions offered.
internships.whyApply: Direct exposure to medical school faculty and real biomedical research. The new AI/informatics track reflects where modern medicine is heading.

internships.title: UC Futures: Rural & Small-Town Scholars Summer Program
internships.description: Free three-day residential summer program at UC for 80 rising high school juniors and seniors from rural communities across the Ohio River Valley. Focused on college access, major exploration, campus visits, and meeting faculty and current students. Particularly targeted at first-generation and underserved students.
internships.whyApply: Rare free residential college-prep experience specifically designed for rural Ohio River Valley students. High-touch access to a major research university with no cost barrier.

internships.title: High School Summer Internship Program
internships.description: Eight-week paid pediatric biomedical research internship where rising seniors work with PhD scientists and physician researchers on cutting-edge projects in cancer biology, immunology, genetics, neuroscience, and more. Open to Cincinnati metro residents (Hamilton, Butler, Warren, Clermont counties in Ohio; Boone, Kenton, Campbell counties in KY) with 3.5+ GPA.
internships.whyApply: Cincinnati Children's is consistently ranked among the top pediatric research hospitals in the US. Paid research alongside PhD mentors is a strong credential for pre-med applicants.

internships.title: Summer Scholars Program
internships.description: Selective one-week residential summer experience for academically talented rising juniors and seniors. Students choose from 22 academic modules spanning sciences, humanities, business, arts, and engineering. Live in dorms, attend college-level classes taught by Miami faculty, and explore 10+ major areas.
internships.whyApply: Broad academic exploration with 22 module choices — useful for students still deciding a focus area. Strong fit for Ohio-based students considering Miami or similar public flagships.

internships.title: Summer Institute (Ohio Supercomputer Center)
internships.description: Two-week residential program where gifted Ohio high school students use OSC's supercomputers to solve real computational science and engineering problems. Teams work on projects like network forensics, epidemiological modeling (bird flu spread), game design, and simulation. Project-based, hands-on HPC experience.
internships.whyApply: One of the only free, residential HPC/supercomputing programs for high schoolers in the country. Real access to research-grade compute infrastructure.

internships.title: Camp CAR (Center for Automotive Research)
internships.company: Ohio State University College of Engineering
internships.description: Week-long summer day camp for students ages 14-18 exploring automotive engineering, simulation, and manufacturing at the OSU Center for Automotive Research. Hands-on exposure to vehicle dynamics, EV technology, and motorsports engineering. Taught by OSU engineering faculty and graduate students.
internships.whyApply: Direct access to one of the top automotive engineering research centers in the US. Good for students considering EV, motorsports, or mechanical engineering careers.

internships.title: Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Year-round teen volunteer program at COSI, one of the nation's top-rated science museums. Volunteers ages 14+ work directly with museum guests delivering science demonstrations, facilitating exhibits, and supporting programs and events. Application cycles run twice per year (spring and fall). Minimum commitment: 4-8 hours per day, at least twice per month.
internships.whyApply: Accessible entry point for younger students (14+) to build science communication and education experience. Strong for students interested in teaching, pediatrics, or informal STEM learning.

internships.title: Pre-College STEM Online Program
internships.description: Online pre-college STEM courses from Case Western Reserve University for students ages 13+. Current courses include Neuroscience and Medicine, Engineering (2D to 3D Modeling), Astrophysics & Evolutionary Biology, and Biomedical Engineering. Two- and four-week sessions throughout the year with video lessons from CWRU instructors, mentor support, and a Certificate of Completion.
internships.whyApply: Accessible online STEM courses from a top research university — great for students who can't travel but want real CWRU faculty-designed coursework on their transcript.

internships.title: CCAD College Preview Program
internships.description: Two-week pre-college summer program (July 12–25, 2026) for rising high school sophomores, juniors, and seniors considering a future in art and design. Students receive college-level instruction in one of 11 major areas (animation, game art & design, fashion, photography, and more) and earn three college art credits. Residential and commuter options available, culminating in a public gallery exhibition.
internships.whyApply: Earn college credit at one of Ohio's top art & design schools, plus a waived CCAD application fee upon completion.

internships.title: Pre-College Craft + Design Program
internships.description: Two-week intensive studio program at the Cleveland Institute of Art focused on craft and design. Two sessions offered: July 5–17 and July 19–31, 2026. Students may attend one or both consecutively. Options for college credit or non-credit tracks, residential or commuter. Instruction from professional artists and designers in CIA's professional-grade studios.
internships.whyApply: One of the best-equipped art school facilities in the Midwest. Scholarship priority deadline February 9 for need-based aid.

internships.title: Flute Boot Camp for High School Students
internships.description: Intensive flute performance training program at Oberlin Conservatory, June 24–28, 2026. High school flutists work with Oberlin's world-class conservatory faculty in daily master classes, individual and group coaching sessions, performance technique workshops, and audition preparation.
internships.whyApply: Oberlin Conservatory is one of the most prestigious music schools in the U.S. — direct access to its faculty on its historic campus.

internships.title: Young Composers Workshop
internships.description: Summer composition workshop at Oberlin Conservatory for high school-aged student composers. Participants work with resident and guest faculty on original compositions, attend daily master classes, receive feedback and mentorship, and present their works in recital. Held on the Oberlin College campus alongside other conservatory summer programs.
internships.whyApply: Rare chance to study composition with Oberlin Conservatory faculty — extremely valuable for students building a portfolio for music school applications.

internships.title: Law & Leadership Institute — Cincinnati Site
internships.company: University of Cincinnati College of Law
internships.description: Multi-year academic enrichment program that enrolls students as rising ninth graders and continues until high school graduation. Students participate in a summer program and academic year program at the University of Cincinnati College of Law, learning about law, leadership, and civic engagement through classes taught by law faculty, attorneys, and judges. One of eight statewide sites across Ohio.
internships.whyApply: Long-term mentorship at a law school campus — one of the only high school law pipelines in the country.

internships.title: Lindner Summer Institute (LSI)
internships.company: University of Cincinnati Lindner College of Business
internships.description: Four-day overnight pre-college program in June introducing high school students to the world of business and the college admissions process. Students meet business faculty, current Lindner students, and corporate partners, and participate in hands-on business activities across disciplines including finance, marketing, operations, and business analytics.
internships.whyApply: Short, accessible intro to business at one of Ohio's top business schools — great for students exploring finance/business majors.

internships.title: Lindner Business Immersion Camp (LBIC)
internships.company: University of Cincinnati Lindner College of Business
internships.description: Overnight residential summer program introducing high school students to a variety of business disciplines through engagements with Lindner faculty, alumni, current students, and corporate partners. Program includes case studies, company visits, and business simulations.
internships.whyApply: Corporate access + faculty instruction in a single immersive week — ideal for students deciding whether to pursue business in college.

internships.title: Teen Portfolio Project
internships.description: Advanced visual arts program for Cleveland-area high school sophomores and juniors meeting weekly across four units at the Cleveland Museum of Art. Students build a portfolio of 10 artworks while exploring drawing, painting, digital arts, and portfolio development. Participants receive an art supply kit, mentorship from professional artists and museum staff, and guidance on applying to visual arts colleges.
internships.whyApply: Free, high-quality arts mentorship at a world-class museum, with direct portfolio prep for art school applications.

internships.title: Summer Engineering Experiences (SEE-UD)
internships.company: University of Dayton School of Engineering
internships.description: Three-day residential engineering camp at the University of Dayton for rising high school juniors and seniors. Students explore diverse engineering disciplines through collaborative hands-on projects with UD faculty, participate in immersive lab activities, and engage with current engineering students. Multiple camps may be attended consecutively.
internships.whyApply: Accessible, hands-on engineering exposure from a well-regarded Catholic research university. Short format makes it easy to combine with other summer commitments.

internships.title: Multi-Ethnic Men in Engineering Camp (M2E)
internships.company: University of Dayton School of Engineering
internships.description: Week-long residential camp at the University of Dayton introducing minority high school men (rising juniors and seniors) to engineering and technology career pathways. Includes college-level classes, hands-on experiments, faculty mentorship, and exposure to the university campus experience.
internships.whyApply: Targeted pipeline program with mentorship, structured curriculum, and a focus on increasing diversity in engineering.

internships.title: Georgia Governor's Honors Program (GHP)
internships.company: Georgia Office of Student Achievement / Georgia Southern University
internships.description: Tuition-free residential summer program for Georgia's most talented rising high school juniors and seniors. Students are nominated by teachers and selected through interviews and auditions in major areas including STEM, humanities, visual/performing arts, world languages, communicative arts, and agricultural research. Held on the campus of Georgia Southern University in mid-summer.
internships.whyApply: Georgia's flagship free residential enrichment program for top high schoolers. Highly selective and entirely funded by the state — tuition, room, and board all covered.

internships.title: Summer PEAKS Programs (High School)
internships.description: One- to two-week immersive STEAM experiences at Georgia Tech for rising 9th-12th grade students. PEAKS (Programs for Enrichment and Accelerated Knowledge in STEAM) covers topics in science, engineering, computing, mathematics, and design through hands-on projects led by Georgia Tech instructors on the Atlanta campus.
internships.whyApply: Georgia Tech–branded STEM exposure with strong curriculum and a variety of thematic tracks. Great option for Georgia students exploring engineering and CS.

internships.title: Georgia Tech Summer Institute
internships.description: Week-long residential program at Georgia Tech (June 22-26, 2026) for rising high school juniors and seniors, combining hands-on STEM learning sessions, college preparation workshops, financial aid guidance, and exposure to campus life with Georgia Tech faculty and students.
internships.whyApply: Residential week on the Georgia Tech campus specifically designed for college-bound juniors and seniors — a direct pipeline into one of the country's top engineering schools.

internships.title: GTRI High School Summer Internship
internships.description: Paid summer internship at the Georgia Tech Research Institute for rising high school juniors and seniors. Interns work with GTRI engineers and scientists on real-world applied research projects in areas like cybersecurity, electronic systems, aerospace, and sensors. U.S. citizenship typically required given the defense/government research context.
internships.whyApply: One of the few paid applied-research internships in Georgia for high schoolers, based at a top-tier research institute with direct exposure to defense and applied engineering projects.

internships.title: Emory Pre-College Program
internships.description: Residential pre-college program for current sophomores and juniors offering non-credit courses taught by Emory faculty across subjects from astrophysics to creative writing. Students live on the Emory campus for two- or six-week sessions. New for 2026: the Pre-Med Institute, a separate pre-health track focused on Emory's health sciences coursework.
internships.whyApply: Top-ranked research university experience with a dedicated pre-health option. Rolling admissions make it accessible on a flexible timeline.

internships.title: Summer Ox-perience Pre-College Program
internships.description: Pre-college program at Emory's historic Oxford College campus for rising high school juniors and seniors. Students take non-credit courses with Oxford faculty while living in the residence halls, experiencing Emory's liberal arts tradition in a small-campus setting.
internships.whyApply: Smaller, more intimate version of the Emory pre-college experience on the original Oxford campus. Strong fit for students interested in liberal arts colleges.

internships.title: Winship Summer Scholars Research Program
internships.company: Winship Cancer Institute of Emory University
internships.description: Six-week paid summer research internship at the Winship Cancer Institute pairing 10-12 rising or graduating high school seniors one-on-one with a physician or lab-based researcher. Students conduct hands-on cancer research, attend faculty-led lectures on oncology and career paths, join roundtable discussions with survivors, and visit sites including the Emory Proton Therapy Center. Stipend of $2,400.
internships.whyApply: Highly competitive paid research placement with one-on-one mentorship at an NCI-designated comprehensive cancer center. Excellent for pre-med students interested in oncology.

internships.title: CDC Museum Disease Detective Camp
internships.description: Free week-long academic day camp at CDC headquarters in Atlanta for rising high school juniors and seniors interested in public health and epidemiology. Highly competitive — around 702 students applied for 27 slots in 2024. Students must be 16 by the first day of camp per CDC laboratory safety requirements. Covers disease outbreaks, epidemiology, infectious diseases, and public health careers.
internships.whyApply: Unique opportunity to learn epidemiology and public health directly inside CDC headquarters. Free of charge and highly prestigious.

internships.title: UGA CAES Young Scholars Internship Program
internships.company: University of Georgia College of Agricultural & Environmental Sciences
internships.description: Paid five-week summer internship for Georgia high school students who have completed their sophomore year. Scholars work 30 hours per week as research assistants alongside UGA faculty mentors in agricultural, food, and environmental sciences labs on the Athens, Griffin, or Tifton campuses. Program culminates in a poster session at the Young Scholars Pre-Collegiate Research Conference.
internships.whyApply: One of the strongest paid high school research internships in Georgia — real lab work with a faculty mentor and a formal research presentation at the end.

internships.title: Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta High School Research Internship
internships.description: Paid high school research internship at the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta for students interested in economics. Interns assist staff economists with quantitative research, literature reviews, library research, data entry, statistical analysis, and database management focused on regional and national economic indicators. Interns attend research seminars and gain exposure to monetary policy analysis.
internships.whyApply: Rare paid high school placement inside a regional Federal Reserve Bank. Direct exposure to economic research and monetary policy work.

internships.title: Spelman College Early College Program
internships.description: Four-week residential program (June 20 – July 17, 2026) for female high school students currently in 11th grade at this historic HBCU. Students earn college credit in English, Biology, or Dance while living on the Spelman campus in Atlanta. Requires a 3.0 cumulative GPA and 3.0 in English and Science. Cost: $4,400-$4,500.
internships.whyApply: Premier HBCU pre-college program for rising senior women — includes actual college credit and immersion in the Atlanta University Center community.

internships.title: Spelman College Prep Institute
internships.description: Summer enrichment program for current 9th and 10th grade female students at Spelman College. Includes SAT preparation, career exploration, essay writing, leadership skills development, and exposure to college life at this historically Black women's college in Atlanta.
internships.whyApply: Early-high-school introduction to HBCU college life and structured SAT/college prep, specifically for young women of color.

internships.title: Morehouse School of Medicine S.T.E.A.M. Academy 9-12
internships.description: Summer pipeline program at Morehouse School of Medicine for high school students (grades 9-12) with sessions running June 1-19 and June 6-July 10, 2026. Focuses on science, technology, engineering, arts, and mathematics with a biomedical and health sciences emphasis, mentorship from medical school faculty, and exposure to careers in medicine and research — a historically Black medical school known for its community health mission.
internships.whyApply: Direct exposure to medical school faculty and facilities at one of the nation's leading HBCU medical schools. Strong fit for students interested in medicine or public health.

internships.title: Zoo Atlanta Volunteen Program
internships.description: Year-round teen volunteer program for high school students ages 14-17. Volunteens commit to a minimum of 160 service hours over the program year (June 1 through May 31), learning in-depth information about animal species, care, and conservation while building advocacy, empathy, leadership, and customer-service skills. $30 orientation fee; scholarships available. Applications open January 1 and close January 31.
internships.whyApply: Long-term, substantive conservation volunteering with structured training. Excellent for students interested in zoology, wildlife biology, or environmental advocacy.

internships.title: Fernbank Museum FYI Youth Interpreters Program
internships.description: Year-round volunteer program for youth ages 13-17 at Fernbank Museum in Atlanta.
