Fernbank Youth Interpreters (FYI) commit to at least one year and 45 service hours, using interpretive carts to share information about biology, archaeology, and paleontology with museum visitors. Volunteers also assist with camps, Discovery Days, animal care, and special projects.
internships.whyApply: One of the few Georgia programs accepting middle schoolers as young as 13. Strong fit for students interested in natural history, science communication, or museum careers.

internships.title: Georgia Aquarium Beginner Environmental Leadership Program
internships.description: Four-month teen volunteer program at the Georgia Aquarium for high school students ages 15-17. Offered three times per year. Empowers teens to become leaders in environmental conservation through hands-on work with Aquarium staff, educational training, and visitor engagement. Attendance at an information session is required to apply; selected applicants participate in a mandatory three-hour group interview.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on marine conservation experience at one of the largest aquariums in the Western Hemisphere. Strong fit for aspiring marine biologists and environmental scientists.

internships.title: UGA Young Dawgs Program
internships.description: University of Georgia's flagship high school internship program, preparing high-achieving juniors and seniors for post-secondary education and careers. Academic Young Dawgs runs during the school year and matches students with internships in labs, offices, and departments across UGA's campus based on their interests. The Summer Science and Academic Enhancement Program places students in science labs and academic sites. Open to public, private, and home-schooled students with a 3.7+ GPA who can get to campus during the school day.
internships.whyApply: Rare opportunity for Georgia high schoolers to intern at a flagship research university across many fields, from science labs to business offices. Strong résumé and college application builder.

internships.title: SCAD Summer Seminars
internships.company: Savannah College of Art and Design
internships.description: Week-long residential and commuter workshops for rising high school freshmen, sophomores, and juniors at SCAD's Atlanta and Savannah campuses. Participants develop creative vision through intensive art and design assignments taught by SCAD professors, alumni, and graduate students. Workshops cover animation, fashion, graphic design, film, interior design, photography, and more. Class sizes are capped at ~20. Sessions run June 21-26, July 5-10, July 12-17, July 19-24 in 2026. Housing available in residence halls.
internships.whyApply: Authentic preview of life at one of the world's top art-and-design universities, taught by SCAD faculty. Scholarships available. Strong fit for students building creative portfolios.

internships.title: High Museum of Art Teen Leadership Council
internships.description: Paid year-round program for 15 rising high school juniors and seniors who share an interest in art and community engagement. Includes a mandatory six-week summer intensive (Wednesday through Friday, 9:30am-3:30pm, June 3-July 17, 2026, off the week of July 1-4) plus year-round meetings. Members get behind-the-scenes museum access, help plan Teen Nights and public events, learn about careers in museums, and engage diverse audiences. Applications open February 1 and close March 1 each year.
internships.whyApply: One of the few paid teen arts leadership programs in Atlanta. A year-long commitment opens doors to museum careers and builds tangible leadership experience.

internships.title: SPEAK Volunteer Program
internships.description: The Students Promoting Environmental Awareness & Knowledge (SPEAK) program engages high school volunteers passionate about teaching and the environment. SPEAK volunteers educate Garden visitors on sustainable gardening practices and combating climate change through short conversations at stations throughout the Garden. Applicants must be 16+ by June 2, 2026, submit a completed application by March 1, and have a non-relative adult (teacher, mentor, boss) submit a recommendation.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on public science communication experience with a major botanical institution. Excellent for aspiring environmental scientists or educators.

internships.title: Atlanta Botanical Garden Summer High School Internship
internships.description: Seven-week paid summer internship for ten high school students from metro-Atlanta Title I schools. Interns work alongside horticulture, education, and conservation staff in June and July, gaining hands-on experience in public gardens, urban ecology, and environmental education. Designed to expand access to plant and conservation careers.
internships.whyApply: Paid, equity-focused internship at one of the Southeast's flagship botanical institutions — a rare combination of pay, mentorship, and access for students from Title I schools.

internships.title: SMASH Rising at Morehouse College
internships.description: Free five-week residential STEM academy for rising 10th-12th graders from historically underrepresented backgrounds. Hosted at Morehouse College — the first HBCU campus to join the SMASH network. Scholars take college-level coursework in computer science, STEM workshops, college success, and electives, and build technical projects mentored by faculty and industry professionals. All tuition, room, board, and materials are covered by the program.
internships.whyApply: One of the most competitive free CS/STEM programs for underrepresented students — fully funded, residential at an HBCU, and with a strong alumni track record of college matriculation.

internships.title: Summer Engineering Institute (SEI)
internships.description: One-week residential program for rising 11th and 12th graders interested in engineering, science, math, or technology. Students live on the Georgia Tech campus and explore engineering through hands-on experiments, interactive workshops, and faculty-led sessions across disciplines (mechanical, electrical, biomedical, aerospace, civil, chemical). SEI is free to participants in 2026, covering tuition, housing, and meals.
internships.whyApply: Free residential week at a top-5 engineering school for college-bound juniors and seniors. Directly exposes students to Georgia Tech's engineering disciplines and admissions process.

internships.title: DiscoverU Summer Camps
internships.company: Kennesaw State University - Community and Professional Education
internships.description: Week-long summer camps for rising middle and high school students at Kennesaw State University. Students choose from hands-on camps in culinary arts, baking and pastry, computer-aided design (CAD), medical skills, career readiness, and aerospace engineering, learning directly from industry professionals on KSU's campus.
internships.whyApply: Flexible, low-commitment sampler of multiple career tracks on a Georgia public university campus. Strong fit for middle/early high schoolers exploring interests.

internships.title: ArtsKSU Summer Intensives
internships.company: Kennesaw State University - Joel A. Katz Music and Dance College of the Arts
internships.description: Week-long creative intensives for rising 9th graders through rising college freshmen at Kennesaw State's College of the Arts. Offerings span music, dance, theatre, and visual arts, giving students intensive training and performance opportunities alongside KSU arts faculty.
internships.whyApply: Focused, discipline-specific arts training at a major public Georgia university. Good stepping stone into college-level arts programs and performance portfolios.

internships.title: Mercer Great Books Summer Program for High School Students
internships.company: Mercer University - Academy for the Future Professional (AFP)
internships.description: Five-day residential summer program on Mercer University's historic Macon campus, taught by Mercer's Great Books faculty. College-bound high school students explore classic texts from Plato to Shakespeare through close reading and Socratic discussion, engaging with enduring human questions. Room in campus dorms, board, supplies, and activities are included.
internships.whyApply: Rare humanities-focused residential program in Georgia centered on seminar-style discussion of foundational Western texts. Strong fit for students interested in law, philosophy, or the liberal arts.

internships.title: VolunTEEN Program
internships.description: Eight-week summer volunteer program at Children's Healthcare of Atlanta for students ages 15-18. VolunTEENs support hospital staff from front desks to patient units, interact with patients and families, and gain exposure to the pediatric healthcare environment. Minimum commitment is one day per week for three hours. Requires proof of immunizations (MMR, Varicella, Tdap, current-season flu) and, for 18-year-olds, a criminal background check.
internships.whyApply: One of Atlanta's premier hospital volunteer programs for pre-med students. Direct patient-facing exposure at a leading pediatric health system.

internships.title: Early College Summer Enrichment Program
internships.company: Georgia State University - Alonzo A. Crim Center
internships.description: Mandatory summer enrichment component of Georgia State's Early College program, which partners with Atlanta-area high schools including Booker T. Washington, Charles R. Drew Charter School, D.M. Therrell, Coretta Scott King Young Women's Leadership Academy, B.E.S.T., and DeKalb Early College Academy. Students take academic preparedness coursework in STEM, English, and Human Communication while building cultural, social, and study skills for college success. Eligibility: 2.75+ GPA, 90%+ attendance, counselor recommendation, essay or video.
internships.whyApply: Tuition-free college preparation program specifically built for students at partner Atlanta schools. Provides a genuine on-ramp to a four-year university degree.

internships.title: The Neuroscience School
internships.description: Short summer courses for high school students at Georgia State University's Neuroscience Institute. Students learn about brain structure and function, cognition, and modern neuroscience research methods directly from GSU faculty and graduate students. Designed as an accessible introduction to neuroscience for science-curious teens.
internships.whyApply: Rare dedicated neuroscience offering for Georgia high schoolers, taught by active researchers. Strong fit for aspiring pre-med, psychology, or brain-science students.

internships.title: Pennsylvania Governor's School for the Sciences (PGSS)
internships.company: Carnegie Mellon University - Mellon College of Science
internships.stipend: Full scholarship — tuition, room, and board fully covered
internships.description: Highly selective five-week residential STEM program for talented Pennsylvania high school juniors. Held on the Carnegie Mellon campus, students take rigorous core courses in biology, chemistry, physics, mathematics, and computer science alongside team research projects led by CMU faculty. Fully funded by the Commonwealth and CMU.
internships.whyApply: The most prestigious free STEM enrichment program in Pennsylvania — flagship credential for Pittsburgh and PA STEM applicants.

internships.title: CMU Pre-College AI Scholars
internships.description: Three-week residential pre-college program for rising high school juniors and seniors focused on artificial intelligence. Students learn the foundations of machine learning, computer vision, and natural language processing through CMU faculty-led coursework and hands-on projects in one of the world's top AI departments.
internships.whyApply: Direct exposure to AI research at the university that essentially invented the field.

internships.title: CMU Pre-College Computational Biology
internships.description: Residential pre-college program for high school students at the intersection of biology, computer science, and statistics. Students learn algorithms, programming, and data analysis while applying them to genomics and biological research questions, taught by CMU's Computational Biology Department.
internships.whyApply: Unique pre-college access to computational biology in a department widely ranked #1 nationally.

internships.title: CMU Pre-College Computer Science Scholars
internships.description: Residential pre-college CS program for rising high school seniors from underrepresented backgrounds. Students take CMU computer science coursework, work on collaborative programming projects, and participate in mentorship and college-prep activities at the top-ranked CS school.
internships.whyApply: Equity-focused entry point into the #1-ranked CS program in the country.

internships.title: CMU Pre-College Architecture
internships.company: Carnegie Mellon University - School of Architecture
internships.description: Six-week residential pre-college program for high school students considering an architecture career. Students develop a portfolio of design projects and drawings, learn drafting and modeling techniques, and experience studio life at one of the country's premier architecture schools.
internships.whyApply: Build a college-ready architecture portfolio in a top-tier studio environment.

internships.title: CMU Pre-College Drama
internships.company: Carnegie Mellon University - School of Drama
internships.description: Six-week intensive pre-college program for high school students pursuing acting, musical theater, design, or dramaturgy. Students train daily with CMU School of Drama faculty in one of the most selective conservatory programs in the country.
internships.whyApply: Audition-based access to the same drama school that trains many Broadway and Hollywood professionals.

internships.title: CMU National High School Game Academy
internships.company: Carnegie Mellon University - Entertainment Technology Center
internships.description: Six-week residential pre-college program where high school students learn game design and development. Students work in interdisciplinary teams to design, prototype, and ship original games under the guidance of CMU's Entertainment Technology Center faculty.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on game development at the institution that pioneered the discipline.

internships.title: Penn State Summer Discovery (Readiness Institute)
internships.description: Six-week paid summer program for rising high school seniors in central Pennsylvania. Students explore career pathways, develop durable workforce skills, complete project-based learning, and engage with Penn State faculty and industry partners.
internships.whyApply: Get paid to spend the summer building college and career readiness on Penn State's flagship campus.

internships.title: Penn State Harrisburg STEM Summer Enrichment Program
internships.company: Penn State Harrisburg - School of Science, Engineering & Technology
internships.description: Two-week summer enrichment workshop for high school students at Penn State Harrisburg, with hands-on labs in engineering, computer science, biology, chemistry, and mathematics led by Penn State faculty. Designed to introduce STEM majors and college life in a residential setting.
internships.whyApply: Affordable, well-structured STEM exploration on a Penn State campus near Harrisburg.

internships.title: Pennsylvania School for Excellence in the Agricultural Sciences (PSEAS)
internships.company: Penn State College of Agricultural Sciences
internships.stipend: Free residential program for selected PA students
internships.description: Two-week residential program at Penn State for academically talented rising high school seniors from across Pennsylvania. Students conduct hands-on research and coursework in agricultural and life sciences including animal science, food science, plant science, and environmental science alongside Penn State faculty.
internships.whyApply: One of the few fully residential ag-science programs in the country, free for selected PA students.

internships.title: Penn State SCOPE — Summer College Opportunity Program in Education
internships.description: Four-week residential program for rising high school juniors interested in becoming teachers or studying education. SCOPE introduces participants to education theory, classroom observations, college life, and the path into a teaching career through Penn State's College of Education.
internships.whyApply: One of the most established teacher-pipeline pre-college programs in the country.

internships.title: CHOP-RISES — CHOP Research Internship for Scholars and Emerging Scientists
internships.company: Children's Hospital of Philadelphia Research Institute
internships.description: Two-summer paid research internship for Philadelphia public and charter high school students. First-year scholars complete a six-week immersive lab experience and research presentation; returning scholars complete an eight-week independent project, mentor first-year students, and present at a formal poster session.
internships.whyApply: Multi-summer paid research at the #1 children's hospital in the country, designed for Philadelphia public school students.

internships.title: Carnegie Museum of Art Youth Art Initiative (YAI)
internships.description: Student-driven high school internship at Carnegie Museum of Art focused on creating museum experiences for Pittsburgh-area teens. YAI members co-design programs, lead public events, and engage with curators while building leadership and arts-administration skills over a 10-week term.
internships.whyApply: Paid arts-leadership work inside a major American art museum.

internships.title: Carnegie Science Center Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Teen volunteer program (ages 14+) at Pittsburgh's flagship hands-on science museum. Volunteers help run summer camps, lead exhibit demonstrations, and support educational programming, building public-speaking, teaching, and STEM-communication skills. Summer commitment is 50+ hours; school-year is 100+ hours.
internships.whyApply: Accessible STEM-communication experience for younger teens at a major science museum.

internships.title: Leadership in the Business World (LBW)
internships.company: Wharton Global Youth Program — University of Pennsylvania
internships.description: Highly selective three-week residential pre-college program for rising seniors interested in business, management, and leadership. Approximately 120 students admitted per session. Taught by Wharton faculty with curriculum in strategy, negotiation, marketing, and finance, culminating in a capstone case competition. Requires 3.5+ unweighted GPA and demonstrated leadership.
internships.whyApply: Flagship Wharton pre-college business program — one of the most prestigious high-school business experiences in the country.

internships.title: Moneyball Academy
internships.company: Wharton Global Youth Program — University of Pennsylvania
internships.description: Selective three-week residential program in sports analytics at Wharton. Approximately 75 students selected each summer. Curriculum covers statistical thinking, regression, and introductory statistical programming (R) applied to baseball, basketball, and football analytics. Taught by Professor Adi Wyner. Participants earn a Wharton Global Youth Certificate of Completion.
internships.whyApply: Unique elite intersection of statistics and sports — strong signal for quantitative majors and sports-industry pathways.

internships.title: Engineering Summer Academy at Penn (ESAP)
internships.company: University of Pennsylvania — School of Engineering and Applied Science
internships.description: Three-week residential pre-college engineering intensive (July 12–31, 2026) for rigorous high schoolers. Specialized course tracks include Artificial Intelligence, Biotechnology, Computer Graphics, Computer Science, AI x Biology, Nanotechnology, and Robotics. Taught by Penn Engineering faculty on campus with evening labs and college-level rigor.
internships.whyApply: One of the most recognized pre-college engineering programs. Strong foundation in applied engineering and AI for competitive STEM applicants.

internships.title: Penn Arts & Sciences Summer Academies
internships.company: University of Pennsylvania — School of Arts and Sciences
internships.description: Subject-intensive 2-week and 3-week residential academies for high schoolers led by Penn faculty. Topics span Experimental Physics, Biomedical Research, Neuroscience, Forensic Science, Ancient World/Classics, Global Human Rights, Social Justice Research, and Coding. Tuition $6,700 (2-week) or $10,050 (3-week), residential included. School District of Philadelphia public/charter students eligible for full scholarship.
internships.whyApply: Faculty-led Ivy League academies across STEM and humanities with full scholarship pipeline for Philadelphia public-school students.

internships.title: Fox Chase Cancer Center Immersion Science Program (ISP)
internships.description: Cost-free multi-phase biomedical research training program for Philadelphia and tri-state-area high school students ages 16+ who have completed chemistry and biology. Students train in laboratory research techniques focused on nutrients and cancer signaling using Drosophila models, then advance to design and execute original research projects in the Fox Chase teaching lab. Offers bootcamp, year-long research track, and summer intensives.
internships.whyApply: Rare free cancer-research lab training at an NCI-designated Comprehensive Cancer Center, open to Philadelphia-area teens without cost barriers.

internships.title: Lehigh Summer Engineering Institute (SEI)
internships.description: Free intensive two-week residential program of classroom study and hands-on research for rising juniors and seniors. Students explore mechanical, electrical, computer, bioengineering, chemical, and civil engineering disciplines with Lehigh faculty and graduate student mentors, culminating in a team design project. Fully funded — no tuition, room, or board cost to participants.
internships.whyApply: One of the few free residential engineering pre-college programs in the Northeast — removes cost barriers to a selective hands-on engineering experience.

internships.title: Iacocca Global Entrepreneurship Intensive (IGEI)
internships.description: Three-week residential pre-college program (July 5–25, 2026) at Lehigh University's Iacocca Institute for internationally minded high school students ages 15–17 graduating in 2027 or 2028. Participants build a global business plan in teams alongside peers from around the world, exploring entrepreneurship, cross-cultural leadership, and innovation with workshops, site visits, and cultural activities.
internships.whyApply: Global cohort and entrepreneurship curriculum — distinctive for students interested in international business and venture creation.

internships.title: Temple University Summer Academy
internships.description: Pre-college program for rising juniors and seniors at Temple's Main Campus. Students take one Temple undergraduate course for college credit plus project-based non-credit workshops. Successful completion earns a Temple tuition grant for later enrollment. Residential on-campus or commuter options.
internships.whyApply: Earns transferable college credit plus a tuition grant incentive for later Temple enrollment — high ROI pre-college option.

internships.title: Temple Tyler School of Art Pre-College Summer Studios
internships.company: Temple University — Tyler School of Art and Architecture
internships.description: Residential and commuter pre-college studio experiences at Temple's Tyler School of Art and Architecture for rising 10-12 graders. Tracks include Portfolio Boot Camp, Architecture Institute, Environmental Design Institute, and Pre-College Summer Studios across fine art, design, and architecture. Hands-on studio instruction in world-class Tyler facilities.
internships.whyApply: Accredited art-school faculty instruction with direct portfolio development for art/architecture applicants.

internships.title: Curtis Summer Organ Intensive
internships.description: Seven-day intensive (June 14–20, 2026) for exceptional high school organ students at Curtis Institute of Music, one of the world's most selective conservatories. Daily lessons in repertoire, improvisation, harpsichord, and conducting with Curtis faculty, plus an excursion to Longwood Gardens and tours of historic Philadelphia-area pipe organs.
internships.whyApply: Immersion at one of the world's top conservatories with direct Curtis faculty mentorship — strong signal for classical-music admissions.

internships.title: Summer Ventures in Science and Mathematics (SVSM)
internships.company: NC School of Science and Mathematics (NCSSM) / UNC System
internships.description: Cost-free, state-funded four-week residential summer research program for academically talented rising NC juniors and seniors pursuing STEM careers. Students conduct hands-on faculty-mentored research on UNC System campuses (Appalachian State, East Carolina, UNC Charlotte, and others). Meals, housing, and tuition are fully covered by the state.
internships.whyApply: Fully-funded state STEM research program for NC students — one of the longest-running free residential research programs in the country.

internships.title: North Carolina Governor's School (East & West)
internships.description: Four-week summer residential program for gifted and talented rising seniors (with limited rising-junior spots in arts). Students are nominated and selected competitively; the program offers non-graded advanced study in English, mathematics, natural science, social science, art, choral music, instrumental music, theater, and dance. 2026 session runs June 21 to July 18 on two campuses (Meredith College and Greensboro College), with tuition of $600 and need-based scholarships available.
internships.whyApply: Historic state summer program integrating academics and arts for NC's top gifted rising seniors.

internships.title: Duke University Neuroscience Experience (DUNE)
internships.description: Eight-week full-time paid summer research internship for Durham public and charter high school juniors and seniors. Students are matched with faculty mentors in cutting-edge neuroscience labs at Duke, work on an original research project, and participate in professional development workshops covering science communication, college applications, and neuroscience careers. Monday-Friday, 9am-5pm. No prior research experience required.
internships.whyApply: Paid full-time neuroscience research inside Duke labs, specifically designed for local Durham high schoolers.

internships.title: Duke's STAR Program (Summer Training in Academic Research)
internships.description: Five-week in-person summer research program for rising seniors and seniors, pairing students with Duke faculty mentors to work on an original hypothesis-driven clinical research project. Participants produce a one-page summary, develop a full research paper, and aim for co-authorship on a peer-reviewed manuscript. Strong academic performance and interest in science required; no prior research experience needed.
internships.whyApply: Paid clinical research experience at one of the top medical research institutions in the country, with a pathway to co-authorship.

internships.title: Duke Pre-College Program
internships.company: Duke University Office of the Provost
internships.description: Residential 12-day pre-college academic program at Duke University for high school students. Courses are intensive and non-credit, with ten academic days, expert instruction, research time, leadership workshops, community service, and off-campus excursions. Students must have completed at least one year of high school and have a 3.0+ GPA (3.5+ recommended for some programs).
internships.whyApply: A taste of Duke academic life through small, immersive courses led by Duke faculty.

internships.title: Project Uplift
internships.company: UNC-Chapel Hill Carolina Higher Education Opportunity Programs
internships.description: Summer Enrichment and College Access Program for rising high school seniors at UNC-Chapel Hill. Approximately 1,000 students participate across multiple sessions each summer, learning about the college admissions process, interacting with UNC faculty and students, and engaging in educational and social programming including SAT/ACT prep and academic explorations. $50 participation fee covers meals and materials.
internships.whyApply: Long-standing and accessible UNC-Chapel Hill summer college access program — competitive but low-cost.

internships.title: Uplift PLUS
internships.company: UNC-Chapel Hill Carolina Higher Education Opportunity Programs
internships.description: Two-week intensive summer academic enrichment and college readiness program for Project Uplift alumni. NC residents only. Participants live on campus at UNC-Chapel Hill, take an essay writing course, and interact with campus partners and offices to deeply prepare for college applications and college-level academic work.
internships.whyApply: Deeper residential follow-on to Project Uplift with intensive essay writing support, exclusively for NC students.

internships.title: The Engineering Place Summer Residential Camps
internships.company: NC State University College of Engineering
internships.description: Weeklong residential engineering summer camps for rising 11-12 grade students at NC State. Students explore engineering disciplines including biomedical, aerospace, materials science, biological/agricultural, chemical and biomolecular, civil/environmental, electrical/computer, industrial, mechanical, and paper science. Hands-on lab work, faculty interactions, and campus life experience. Financial assistance available.
internships.whyApply: Broad hands-on engineering exposure at a top NC engineering school with the chance to try multiple disciplines.

internships.title: The Engineering Place Summer Day Camps
internships.company: NC State University College of Engineering
internships.description: Engineering day camps for rising K-10 students at NC State, running multiple weeks each summer. Topics include sustainable design, renewable energy, robotics, and hands-on engineering projects across multiple disciplines. Designed to spark early interest in engineering for elementary and middle school students. Financial assistance available.
internships.whyApply: One of the few NC engineering-focused programs that reaches elementary and middle schoolers, not just high schoolers.

internships.title: RKS Debate Workshop at Wake Forest University
internships.description: Residential and online summer debate workshop named for legendary Wake Forest debate coach Ross K. Smith. Offers sessions in policy, Lincoln-Douglas, and public forum formats across 1 to 5-week programs for high school debaters. Follows a 'No-Lab Curriculum' emphasizing skills development, intellectual growth, and healthy living. Staffed by the Wake Forest Debate program and elite debate coaches.
internships.whyApply: Premier competitive debate workshop run by one of the top collegiate debate programs in the country.

internships.title: UNC Charlotte STEM Summer Scholars
internships.company: UNC Charlotte Center for STEM Education
internships.description: Weekly STEM summer enrichment programming for rising 6th-12th grade students, running throughout July at UNC Charlotte. Sessions are aligned with the North Carolina Standard Course of Study and upcoming fall math and science classes. Students can register for individual weekly engagements in topics spanning STEM disciplines.
internships.whyApply: Flexible, grade-aligned summer STEM engagement in the Charlotte metro for middle and high school students.

internships.title: UNC Charlotte STEM Research Experience Program
internships.company: UNC Charlotte Center for STEM Education
internships.description: Summer research experience for high school juniors and seniors at UNC Charlotte. Students are paired one-on-one with a scientist mentor to conduct intensive research aligned with the mentor's own research agenda. Participants produce research products and present findings at UNC Charlotte showcase events.
internships.whyApply: Mentor-matched research experience in a major Charlotte-area STEM program, producing showcase deliverables.

internships.title: NC Museum of Natural Sciences Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Year-round teen volunteer opportunities at North Carolina's flagship natural sciences museum. Middle and high school students support exhibits, learning centers, research labs, and daily hands-on public programming with on-the-job training. Minimum commitment of 4 hours per month. Builds skills in science communication, public engagement, and informal STEM education.
internships.whyApply: Accessible hands-on science engagement at NC's largest natural sciences museum, open to younger teens.

internships.title: UNCSA Summer Intensives
internships.company: University of North Carolina School of the Arts
internships.description: Pre-professional summer intensives at UNCSA across dance, drama, filmmaking, music, and visual arts. Programs range from one to four weeks; high school students live on campus and study with conservatory faculty. Disciplines include classical and contemporary ballet, acting, screenwriting, classical music, jazz, and visual arts portfolio development.
internships.whyApply: Conservatory-level pre-professional arts training at the state arts conservatory — strong pipeline for arts college applications.

internships.title: ACS Project SEED at UNC Charlotte
internships.company: American Chemical Society / UNC Charlotte Department of Chemistry
internships.description: 8-week paid summer chemistry research experience for economically disadvantaged rising juniors and seniors who have completed at least one chemistry course. Students work side-by-side with UNC Charlotte chemistry faculty conducting hands-on research in synthetic, analytical, biochemical, or materials chemistry. Run by the NC Section of ACS in partnership with UNCC.
internships.whyApply: Paid hands-on chemistry research at a major research university, with national ACS recognition and networking with practicing chemists.

internships.title: NCMA Teen Arts Council
internships.description: Year-long teen leadership council that meets Thursday afternoons (4:30-6:30 pm) at the NC Museum of Art from September through June. Council members plan teen takeovers and exhibitions, attend artist workshops, learn about museum careers, and design pop-up art activities and juried exhibitions. Free, with applications opening in summer.
internships.whyApply: Real curatorial and program-design experience at the state's flagship art museum — a strong springboard for arts and museum studies college applications.

internships.title: Wake Forest Pre-College Medicine Institute
internships.company: Wake Forest University / Wake Forest School of Medicine
internships.description: Residential pre-college summer immersion for high school students exploring careers in medicine. Students gain hands-on exposure across multiple medical disciplines, work with Wake Forest School of Medicine faculty and clinicians, simulate patient care scenarios, and learn about the medical school admissions pathway. On-campus residential experience.
internships.whyApply: Direct exposure to clinical and academic medicine at a top-tier medical school — valuable for students considering a pre-med pathway.

internships.title: WFIRM Summer Research Exposure Program (SREP)
internships.company: Wake Forest Institute for Regenerative Medicine
internships.description: 4-week summer research exposure program for North Carolina high school students ages 16-18. Provides educational and hands-on, mentored research/shadowing experiences with WFIRM faculty researchers and their teams in tissue engineering, organ regeneration, biomaterials, and translational medicine. NC residents only.
internships.whyApply: Direct mentored exposure to one of the world's leading regenerative medicine institutes — rare opportunity for HS students to see translational tissue engineering up close.

internships.title: Wake Forest Medical Scholars Summer Experience
internships.company: Wake Forest University School of Medicine
internships.description: Summer experience for underrepresented and disadvantaged 11th and 12th grade students with a demonstrated interest in medical careers and academic potential. Recruits, educates, and prepares scholars for the medical school pipeline through clinical exposure, mentorship from medical students and physicians, and pre-med academic enrichment. Cost-free for accepted students.
internships.whyApply: Free, structured pipeline program for underrepresented students into medicine — direct mentorship from Wake Forest physicians and med students.

internships.title: WFSOM Center for Precision Medicine Summer Intern Program
internships.company: Wake Forest School of Medicine — Center for Precision Medicine
internships.description: Summer internship program at the WFSOM Center for Precision Medicine for students interested in genomics, computational biology, and precision medicine research. Interns work alongside faculty on active research projects spanning bioinformatics, pharmacogenomics, and translational genomic medicine.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on exposure to precision medicine and genomic research — an emerging high-impact field combining biology, statistics, and computing.

internships.title: Discovery Place Career Launchpad
internships.description: Multi-year youth development program at Discovery Place Science for Charlotte teens. Participants gain leadership skills, academic support, professional mentorship, and career exposure across STEM disciplines and museum operations. Includes paid internship opportunities for advanced cohort members. Contact YouthDevelopment@discoveryplace.org for application info.
internships.whyApply: Long-term mentorship and leadership pipeline at a major science museum — strong fit for Charlotte-area teens interested in STEM careers and informal science education.

internships.title: Explore@UNC
internships.description: One-week, on-campus pre-college program at UNC Chapel Hill for rising high school seniors. Over five days, students explore two academic majors as focused two-day tracks led by Carolina faculty and near-peer mentors. Tracks include biology, anthropology, music, political science, sports administration, archaeology, public policy, and classics. Need-based fee assistance available for SNAP/NSLP-eligible students.
internships.whyApply: Low-cost (with aid) on-campus exposure to two academic majors at a top public university — useful for students still narrowing their college focus.

internships.title: Duke Summer STEM Academy
internships.company: Duke Thomas Lord Department of Mechanical Engineering & Materials Science
internships.description: Three-week intensive engineering program for rising juniors and seniors at high schools around Durham and the Research Triangle Region. Students learn computer-aided drafting (CAD), advanced manufacturing techniques (3D printing, laser cutting, microcontroller programming, electronics, shop tools), project management, product design, and technical communication. Students develop a digital portfolio of their work.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on engineering design and fabrication experience at Duke's Pratt School — designed to build a tech talent pipeline for the Triangle region.

internships.title: Duke Research in Engineering Program (DukeREP)
internships.company: Duke University — Department of Biomedical Engineering
internships.description: 7-week summer research program hosted by Duke's Department of Biomedical Engineering for high school students with strong interest in science and engineering. Students work in active Duke research labs alongside graduate students and faculty mentors on biomedical engineering projects spanning imaging, biomaterials, neural engineering, and biomechanics.
internships.whyApply: Long-form mentored biomedical engineering research at one of the top BME departments in the country — strong signal for engineering applications.

internships.title: CU Science Discovery Summer Classes for High School Students
internships.company: University of Colorado Boulder — Science Discovery
internships.description: Pre-college, non-credit one-week STEM-focused classes on the CU Boulder campus that combine academics with hands-on labs and college life immersion. Course topics include aerospace, medicine, biomedical research, neuroscience, maker technologies, robotics, video game design, and forensics. Designed to give high school students a well-rounded introduction to college life and college-level STEM.
internships.whyApply: Affordable, hands-on STEM exposure on a flagship public research campus — useful for students testing interest in different STEM fields before college.

internships.title: CU Boulder Precollegiate Development Program (PDP)
internships.company: University of Colorado Boulder — Office of Precollege Outreach and Engagement
internships.description: Multi-year, year-round college access program culminating in a five-week summer residential experience the summer before senior year. Students live on the CU Boulder campus, take academic courses, and learn about postsecondary resources. Targets first-generation, low-income, and historically underrepresented students from Colorado.
internships.whyApply: Free, intensive college-readiness pipeline for first-gen and underrepresented Colorado students with a strong record of college matriculation.

internships.title: CU Boulder Precollege STEM Summer Program (STEM Pathfinder)
internships.company: University of Colorado Boulder — Office of Precollege Outreach and Engagement
internships.description: Two-week summer program where high school students dive into four different fields within engineering or bioscience through hands-on activities, lab tours, and faculty interactions. Two tracks: Bioscience Program (June 1-12, 2026) and Engineering Program (July 13-24, 2026).
internships.whyApply: Lets students sample multiple sub-fields of engineering or bioscience in a short timeframe — great for narrowing major interest before college applications.

internships.title: Summer Mines Engineering and Training (SUMMET)
internships.company: Colorado School of Mines — Undergraduate Admissions
internships.description: Engineering, science, and technology program for rising juniors and seniors to experience college life at Mines. Combines coursework, hands-on projects, industry tours, team-building, and social events. The program is offered at no cost to participants except a $50 confirmation fee. Strong fit for students from underrepresented backgrounds in engineering.
internships.whyApply: Free engineering immersion at a top-ranked applied science school — strong on industry exposure and especially valuable for students considering specialized engineering majors.

internships.title: CS@Mines Summer Computing Program for High School Students
internships.company: Colorado School of Mines — Department of Computer Science
internships.description: Five-week summer program where high school students work in teams to complete a substantive computer programming project at the CS@Mines department. Program runs June 22 – July 28, 2026 with a $200 program fee. Mentored by Mines CS faculty and students.
internships.whyApply: Low-cost, project-based CS experience at Mines — meaningful portfolio-quality team project for students considering computer science.

internships.title: Upward Bound Math and Science (UBMS) at Colorado School of Mines
internships.company: Colorado School of Mines — TRIO UBMS
internships.description: TRIO Upward Bound Math and Science is a federally funded six-week summer program preparing first-generation, low-income high school students for college-level math and science. The first four weeks take place at Colorado School of Mines, where participants take classes in math, science, language arts, and foreign language while living on campus.
internships.whyApply: Free, federally funded residential STEM enrichment specifically for first-gen and low-income students — pathway to STEM majors at four-year colleges.

internships.title: Denver Museum of Nature & Science Teen Science Scholars
internships.description: Competitive paid-equivalent internship that selects 20 high school students each summer to work alongside Museum scientists in the Science Division. Research areas include paleontology, wildlife disease ecology, zoology, and anthropology. Program intentionally serves students from underserved and underrepresented communities, provides daily lunch vouchers and all materials, and culminates in an end-of-summer Showcase.
internships.whyApply: One of the few authentic, mentored research internships in Colorado open to high schoolers — explicitly designed for students from underrepresented backgrounds.

internships.title: NCAR / UCAR PRECIP — Pre-College Internship Program
internships.company: NSF NCAR — UCAR Center for Science Education
internships.description: Six-week pre-college internship coordinated by the UCAR Center for Science Education for current high school juniors, seniors, and community college students. Interns work on projects with NSF NCAR scientists across atmospheric science, climate, and Earth systems labs. NCAR does not provide housing; out-of-area students must arrange a parent, legal guardian, or chaperone for the duration of the program.
internships.whyApply: Direct access to research at the nation's premier atmospheric and climate science laboratory — a strong signal for students pursuing geoscience or climate.

internships.title: Children's Hospital Colorado Junior Volunteer Program
internships.description: Junior Volunteer Program for teens ages 13 to 18 that places youth volunteers in hospital departments and supports fundraising events at Children's Hospital Colorado. Both full-year and summer-only volunteer opportunities are available. Application includes interview and health screening. Volunteers may receive credit toward scholarship applications.
internships.whyApply: One of the most accessible structured medical volunteer programs in Colorado — direct exposure to a top-ranked pediatric hospital.

internships.title: Medical Career Collaborative (MC²)
internships.description: Multi-year health careers pathway program for high school students interested in healthcare professions. Provides paid internships, mentorship, certification opportunities, and career coaching. Offered at Denver Health and several Children's Hospital Colorado locations including Anschutz Medical Campus (Aurora), North Campus (Broomfield), South Campus (Highlands Ranch), and Colorado Springs. Applicants must be sophomores in Denver metro or Colorado Springs at time of application; students start the program at the beginning of junior year.
internships.whyApply: Rare two-year paid healthcare pathway for Colorado students — combines clinical exposure, mentorship, and certifications.

internships.title: CU Anschutz K-12 Outreach — Pre-Health Programs
internships.company: University of Colorado Anschutz Medical Campus — Office of Access & Engagement
internships.description: K-12 outreach programs at the CU Anschutz Medical Campus including the CU Pre-Health Scholars (CUPS) High School Track and CADAVERS — a Colorado AHEC outreach providing high school students hands-on experience with anatomy in a medical university setting (lectures plus gross anatomy labs). Focus on reaching students from all Colorado regions interested in health sciences.
internships.whyApply: Direct access to a top-ranked medical campus and hands-on anatomy exposure — distinctive credential for pre-med-bound high schoolers.

internships.title: Colorado College Pre-College Summer Program
internships.description: Pre-College Block invites rising juniors and seniors to take a single, immersive, for-credit course over two and a half weeks under Colorado College's distinctive Block Plan. Course offerings include environmental studies, history, economics and business, and human anatomy. Students live in CC residence halls and participate in fieldwork, labs, and hands-on experiences in Colorado's landscape. Total cost $7,250 (tuition, housing, meals, fees).
internships.whyApply: Try Colorado College's unique Block Plan and earn college credit on a single subject — meaningful for liberal arts students interested in deep focus.

internships.title: Stroud Scholars Program at Colorado College
internships.description: Three-year college access program for talented Colorado public high school students from historically underrepresented backgrounds. Students participate in summer residential programming on the CC campus before their sophomore, junior, and senior years, plus year-round support. Designed to prepare scholars for selective four-year colleges.
internships.whyApply: Free three-year mentorship and selective-college prep pipeline for Colorado students from underrepresented backgrounds.

internships.title: Denver Botanic Gardens Summer Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Summer volunteer program for current high school students (rising freshmen through graduating seniors). Participants explore experiences in horticulture and guest engagement that build professionalism and independence — gardening, science communication, and customer service. Volunteer at any of three locations (York Street, Chatfield Farms, Plains Conservation Center) and participate at least once per week from June 3 – August 2.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on plant science and public engagement experience at one of the country's leading public gardens — strong fit for students interested in environmental science.

internships.title: CSU Global Business Academy
internships.company: Colorado State University — College of Business
internships.description: Ten-day, action-packed summer business program for high school students ages 15-18 enrolled for fall. Students learn how to use business to create a better world while collaborating with peers from around the globe. Hosted by the CSU College of Business in Fort Collins.
internships.whyApply: Short, intensive immersion in business and finance with a global lens — strong fit for students considering business school.

internships.title: GoGetMath@CSU
internships.company: Colorado State University — Department of Mathematics
internships.description: Free five-day, non-residential summer math program for Colorado high school students entering grades 10-12. Held in the Department of Mathematics on the CSU Fort Collins campus. Students engage with university faculty in advanced math topics beyond the standard high school curriculum.
internships.whyApply: Free advanced math program at a major research university — strong fit for math-inclined students looking beyond their high school curriculum.

internships.title: Denver Art Museum Teen Studio
internships.description: Museum-based art workshops at the Denver Art Museum where teens experiment with materials, tackle creative challenges, and develop their own artistic voice. Workshops blend gallery exploration with hands-on studio work, led by experienced educators and professional teaching artists. Three workshops are offered for ages 12-14 and ages 15-17 (July 13-31). Full-day studios cost $450 (members) / $500 (nonmembers); one full scholarship is offered per camp.
internships.whyApply: Studio art experience at one of the West's leading museums, with scholarship support — meaningful creative-portfolio building for teens.

internships.title: Ted R. Lilley CURE Program
internships.description: Eight-week biomedical cancer research internship at OHSU Knight Cancer Institute, June 22 to August 14, 2026. Portland-area high school students from socially or economically disadvantaged backgrounds are placed in Knight Cancer Institute labs with faculty mentors. Supported by the Center for Diversity and Inclusion. Applications for 2026 closed; 2027 cycle opens late fall.
internships.whyApply: One of Oregon's flagship paid high-school biomedical research programs, embedded in a top cancer research institute with a strong diversity pipeline.

internships.title: PSI Program (Partnering in Science & Medicine Internship)
internships.description: A 16-week evening course (Wednesdays 6-8pm PT, Jan 14 - May 27, 2026) for high school sophomores, juniors, and seniors interested in medicine or biomedical research. Taught by OHSU physicians and scientists via Webex. Qualifying students are matched with an OHSU research lab for a summer internship. Students must be 16+ at time of summer placement.
internships.whyApply: Structured pathway into OHSU research labs for Portland-area teens with a genuine interest in medicine — rare combination of coursework and hands-on lab matching.

internships.title: STEMPrep-OHSU Internship Program
internships.description: OHSU partners with the national STEMPrep program to host high school and college trainees for an 8-10 week basic science research internship from mid-June to mid-August. Students conduct a hypothesis-driven research project mentored by OHSU faculty, post-docs, and graduate students. Housed at Portland State University with commute to OHSU via trolley.
internships.whyApply: Full residential research experience with housing included — equivalent to a college-level REU, extremely rare for high schoolers.

internships.title: Apprenticeships in Science & Engineering (ASE)
internships.description: Saturday Academy's ASE program connects rising 10th-12th grade students with paid 8-week full-time summer internships mentored by STEM professionals at companies like Intel, Nike, OHSU, Oregon Department of Fish and Wildlife, OSU, PSU, and Oregon national labs. Internships take place throughout Oregon and SW Washington (Portland, Eugene, Corvallis, Vancouver, Bend). ~75 placements per year. 2026 applications closed March 6; matching begins April 15.
internships.whyApply: Oregon's flagship paid STEM apprenticeship pipeline — project-based placement with a real mentor at top-tier Oregon companies and labs.

internships.title: Summer SAIL
internships.description: Free weeklong day program July 20-24, 2026 on the UO Eugene campus for Oregon high school students. Participants explore academic subjects with University of Oregon faculty across various departments (architecture, business, education, environmental science, journalism, etc.). Designed to build college readiness and aspirations for first-gen and underserved students.
internships.whyApply: Zero-cost, faculty-led college preview for Oregon teens — one of the best free pre-college taster programs in the state.

internships.title: OSU-Cascades Summer Academy
internships.description: One-week residential or commuter pre-college experience for students entering grades 10-12. Two 2026 sessions: July 19-24 and July 26-31. Students explore academic tracks (science, engineering, outdoor, tourism, business) on OSU's Bend campus. Overnight option $1,500; day option $1,000; scholarships available.
internships.whyApply: Only Central Oregon residential pre-college — lets teens experience college life outside Portland/Willamette Valley.

internships.title: SESEY - Summer Experience in Science & Engineering for Youth
internships.company: Oregon State University College of Engineering
internships.stipend: Free with scholarships for residential fees
internships.description: One-week residential engineering camp at OSU Corvallis, July 19-24, 2026, for female and underrepresented minority students in grades 9-11. Participants are embedded with a CBEE (Chemical, Biological, Environmental Engineering) research group for hands-on lab work and present their findings at the end. Scholarships cover the full cost for qualifying students.
internships.whyApply: Free residential research camp for girls and URM students at OSU — directly addresses STEM diversity pipeline.

internships.title: OSU STEM Academy High School Summer Camps
internships.description: Week-long summer camps on the OSU Corvallis campus taught by OSU experts. Tracks include Engineering, TekPets, Computer Science, Forensic Science, Ocean Science, Lego Robotics, and Science & Art. Open to students in grades 6-12, with scholarships available. Registration opened March 2, 2026.
internships.whyApply: Broad menu of STEM camps accessible to both middle and high school students, with scholarships — one of Oregon's most flexible STEM enrichment pipelines.

internships.title: Oregon Zoo Apprenticeship Program (ZAP)
internships.description: Multi-year paid apprenticeship at the Oregon Zoo for high school-aged youth 15 and older. Combines educational programming, career exploration in wildlife and conservation fields, and direct connections to zookeepers, educators, and conservation staff. Apprentices gain hands-on experience with zoo operations and conservation-focused projects.
internships.whyApply: Extremely rare paid multi-year zoo apprenticeship — exceptional for students serious about zoology or conservation careers.

internships.title: Oregon Zoo Wildlife Leaders (OWLs)
internships.description: Teen volunteer and education program for youth ages 13-15 passionate about animals and conservation. Participants volunteer at the Oregon Zoo, engage in hands-on conservation work in the Portland metro area, and learn about wildlife and conservation career fields. 2026 applications have closed.
internships.whyApply: One of the few structured conservation-focused teen programs in Oregon for middle schoolers — great early pipeline into environmental careers.

internships.title: OMSI Career Pathways Program
internships.company: Oregon Museum of Science and Industry
internships.description: Year-long program for Portland-area high schoolers ages 16-19 with a paid summer internship aligned to career interests. Participants explore STEM, museum, media, and design careers while developing professional skills. Requires a year-long commitment including monthly workshops plus a paid full-time summer placement.
internships.whyApply: Structured career-exploration program with a paid internship component — top option for Portland teens unsure which STEM path to pursue.

internships.title: OMSI Teen Volunteer Program
internships.company: Oregon Museum of Science and Industry
internships.description: Teen volunteer opportunities at OMSI's Portland campus. Volunteers support public engagement in exhibit halls, lead hands-on science activities for museum visitors, and assist with special events. Open to middle and high school students. Strong pathway into OMSI's more selective Career Pathways and Teen Tech Center programs.
internships.whyApply: Low-barrier entry point into OMSI's teen ecosystem and a great introduction to science communication for Portland-area students.

internships.title: PNCA Pre-College Summer Intensive
internships.company: Pacific Northwest College of Art (Willamette University)
internships.description: Two-week pre-college summer intensive at PNCA in downtown Portland for high school students interested in art and design. Students build technical skills, develop portfolio pieces, and explore professional practice in disciplines like illustration, graphic design, painting, and animation. Taught by PNCA faculty and practicing artists.
internships.whyApply: Oregon's most established art-school pre-college — essential for teens building a portfolio for BFA applications.

internships.title: EOU ECI Summer Institute
internships.company: Eastern Oregon University - Early College Initiatives
internships.description: One-week interdisciplinary summer institute at Eastern Oregon University in La Grande, June 22-27, 2026, for grades 9-12. Students explore Digital Music, Business, Writing, and Spanish tracks. Cost is $450 plus a $25 application fee. Offers a genuine college-life experience with faculty instruction and campus residence.
internships.whyApply: One of the very few pre-college summer options in Eastern Oregon — rare combination of business and humanities tracks for rural students.

internships.title: Engineering Summer Program (ESP)
internships.description: Free three-week residential program July 11-31, 2026 for rising high school juniors and seniors interested in engineering. Curriculum includes math (pre-calc or calc), physics, chemistry, and engineering design taught by UW-Madison faculty. Participants live in Dejope Hall, take field trips to industry sites, and receive faculty mentoring. U.S. citizens or permanent residents only; minimum 3.0 unweighted GPA. Highly selective holistic admissions.
internships.whyApply: Wisconsin's flagship free residential engineering program for high schoolers — includes housing, meals, and college-level coursework with UW faculty.

internships.title: PEOPLE Program (Pre-college Enrichment Opportunity Program for Learning Excellence)
internships.stipend: Free 4-year program with summer residential component
internships.description: Multi-year college access program for Wisconsin students of color, students from economically disadvantaged backgrounds, and first-generation college attendees. Open to 9th graders entering 10 partner Milwaukee Public Schools. Includes free after-school tutoring, advising, UW System campus tours, college application help, and PEOPLE Summer University (PSU) — a residential summer program at UW-Madison after senior year offering core subject prep, ACT prep, and career exploration. Successful completion can lead to admission and tuition support at UW-Madison.
internships.duration: 4 years (9th grade through summer after senior year)
internships.whyApply: One of the most comprehensive college access pipelines in the Midwest — provides 4 years of support and a clear pathway to UW-Madison admission and aid.

internships.title: Apprenticeship in Medicine (AIM)
internships.description: Six-week paid summer enrichment for rising juniors, rising seniors, and graduating seniors (16+) from Milwaukee-area high schools with a 2.75+ GPA in science/math. Runs mid-June to early August. Activities include classroom lectures in anatomy and physiology, suture clinics, heart dissections, clinical shadowing, simulation training, mentorship by current medical students, and a final oral presentation. Aimed at students from diverse backgrounds interested in medicine, biomedical research, or allied health.
internships.whyApply: Rare paid medical pipeline program for Milwaukee teens — combines classroom medicine, hands-on clinical simulations, and physician mentorship.

internships.title: Badger Summer Scholars
internships.description: Two-week pre-college program for high school students offering college-level seminars in subjects ranging from engineering, computer science, business, music, art, to law. Taught by UW-Madison faculty and Badger Precollege instructors in small class sizes. Commuter or residential options. 2026 application period opens January 12, 2026; rolling admissions and first-come placement.
internships.whyApply: Broad subject menu with UW-Madison faculty — strong fit for academically motivated teens exploring potential majors before college applications.

internships.title: Accelerated Learning Program (ALP)
internships.company: Wisconsin Center for Academically Talented Youth (WCATY) / UW-Madison
internships.description: Three-week intensive residential summer program at UW-Madison for academically talented high school students from across Wisconsin, the U.S., and internationally. Designed to push students to the next academic level in an intimate learning environment. Subjects span engineering, architecture, robotics, marine biology, forensic science, and law. Founded in 1991, WCATY is a UW-Madison outreach program serving rural, urban, and suburban Wisconsin communities.
internships.whyApply: Wisconsin's premier gifted/talented summer residential — small cohorts and serious academic pacing for students who want depth over breadth.

internships.title: Summer Programs at MSOE (Explore & Focus)
internships.description: Week-long residential academic camps in July 2026 for high school freshmen, sophomores, and juniors with a 3.5+ GPA. The Explore program rotates students through half-day sessions across MSOE engineering majors (architectural, biomedical, chemical & biomolecular, civil, computer, electrical, mechanical, software) and healthcare disciplines. The Focus program is for students committed to a single major. Includes hands-on labs and dorm housing on the MSOE Milwaukee campus.
internships.whyApply: One of the few residential STEM camps in Wisconsin where students can sample multiple engineering majors in a single week — useful for narrowing college focus.

internships.title: EnQuest Engineering Camp
internships.company: UW-Milwaukee College of Engineering & Applied Science
internships.description: Hands-on engineering camp for girls in Wisconsin to explore computer, civil, electrical, mechanical, biomedical, materials, and industrial engineering. 2026 Day Camp runs July 13-17 (girls exiting 8th-11th, 9:30am-3:30pm). 2026 Overnight Camp runs July 26-31 (girls exiting 9th-11th). Hosted on the UW-Milwaukee campus by the College of Engineering & Applied Science.
internships.whyApply: One of the longest-running girls-in-engineering camps in Wisconsin — both day and residential options across grade ranges, including 8th graders.

internships.title: Marquette Upward Bound & Upward Bound Math-Science
internships.description: Federally funded TRIO program preparing low-income, first-generation Milwaukee high school students to enter and complete college. Includes specialized classes, tutorials, college placement assistance, career development labs, academic and personal counseling, plus social/cultural activities. UBMS adds specialized math, science, writing, and technology classes for students with serious STEM interest. Target schools: Riverside University HS, Bradley Tech, Washington HS, Marshall, Hamilton HS, Milwaukee Academy of Science. Recruitment in 8th grade (UB) or 9th grade (UBMS).
internships.whyApply: Among the most established TRIO college-access pipelines in Milwaukee — comprehensive, free, and runs throughout high school.

internships.title: Beloit College Center for Language Studies Summer Program
internships.description: Intensive summer language immersion offering Chinese, Japanese, and Russian in two 3-week sessions (June 15 - July 31, 2026). Open to high school juniors and seniors alongside undergraduates and adult learners. Each session earns 4 US credits (1 Beloit Unit); students completing both earn 8 semester hours. Cutting-edge teaching methods, immersive virtual setting, and cultural enrichment activities. Requires $300 non-refundable deposit by May 15.
internships.whyApply: One of the few intensive less-commonly-taught-language summer programs open to high schoolers — earns real college credit in critical languages.

internships.title: Lawrence University Ascent Summer Institute
internships.description: One-week residential pre-college experience for rising high school students at Lawrence University in Appleton. Designed for high-achieving AP-level students and taught by Lawrence faculty, the institute offers a college-level interdisciplinary learning and leadership-development experience on a leading liberal arts campus. Companion Odyssey Immersion Program offers a three-week multi-location interdisciplinary track with college credit.
internships.whyApply: Rare interdisciplinary liberal-arts pre-college experience in Wisconsin — strong fit for students considering small-college humanities programs.

internships.title: International Crane Foundation Conservation Education Naturalist Internship
internships.description: Conservation Education Naturalist internships at the world's only facility holding all 15 crane species. Interns help interpret cranes and conservation strategies for visitors at the 250-acre Baraboo headquarters, support field-ecology activities (prairie restoration, invasive species control, prescribed burns, crane tracking), and learn from leading crane biologists. Volunteer Naturalist roles also open to teens with public-speaking interest. A pipeline opportunity for Wisconsin teens interested in conservation, ornithology, and environmental science.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on conservation work at a globally significant wildlife research site — unmatched ornithology and ecology exposure for Wisconsin students.

internships.title: Milwaukee County Zoo High School Assistant Program
internships.description: High School Assistant (HSA) volunteer role supporting Milwaukee County Zoo Summer Camps. Teen volunteers (15+) work alongside a college mentor to assist children, gain hands-on animal-care and education experience, and develop teaching/leadership skills. Onboarding includes online application, background check, interview, and orientation. Volunteers contribute toward the 55,000 hours of annual service the zoo's volunteer program logs.
internships.whyApply: Best entry point for Milwaukee teens interested in zoology and informal science education — direct mentorship and meaningful kid-facing teaching role.

internships.title: Discovery World Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Teen volunteer roles at Discovery World, Milwaukee's lakefront science and technology center. Volunteers (14+) support exhibits, educational programming, and visitor engagement, becoming partners in the museum's mission to bring STEM learning to hundreds of thousands of guests. Multiple role options with varying minimum hour commitments (12-28 hours) and a once-a-month minimum cadence — flexible enough for school-year participation.
internships.whyApply: One of the most accessible STEM volunteer entry points in Milwaukee for younger teens (age 14+) — flexible schedule that pairs well with school year.

internships.title: Wisconsin School of Business — Business Basics
internships.company: University of Wisconsin–Madison, Wisconsin School of Business
internships.description: Four-day commuter precollege business camp at Grainger Hall on the UW–Madison campus for rising 9th and 10th graders. Students get an introduction to studying business at a top public business school: entrepreneurship challenge led by UW–Madison's StartUp Learning Community, exposure to current business students, faculty, and corporate leaders, panel discussions with women in business, and possible offsite job-shadow experiences. Designed to broaden access for students from diverse backgrounds.
internships.whyApply: Earliest pre-college business exposure at one of the top public business schools — specifically scoped to younger high schoolers (rising 9-10), where most business camps require older students.

internships.title: Wisconsin School of Business — Junior Business Badgers
internships.company: University of Wisconsin–Madison, Wisconsin School of Business
internships.description: Four-day commuter precollege business camp at Grainger Hall for rising 11th and 12th graders. Two sessions offered (June 22-25 and June 29-July 2, 2026). Students explore disciplines across the Wisconsin School of Business — finance, marketing, accounting, supply chain, real estate, and entrepreneurship — with faculty-led sessions, current student panels, and field trips to Madison-area companies. Financial assistance is available; contact wsbprecollege@wsb.wisc.edu.
internships.whyApply: Direct introduction to the Wisconsin School of Business right before college applications — perfect for rising seniors targeting business undergrad programs.

internships.title: Marquette Law Summer Youth Institute (SYI)
internships.description: Free, highly selective (≈24 students) summer institute introducing 8th–10th graders to the legal system, careers in law, and college-readiness skills. Open to students who live or attend school in Milwaukee. First-generation college-bound students, especially those who use English as a second language, are strongly encouraged to apply. Program runs July 23–31, 2026 on Marquette's Eckstein Hall law school campus. Students engage with practicing lawyers and law students, simulate legal proceedings, and work on academic and career planning.
internships.whyApply: One of the few free, structured law-pipeline programs for younger high schoolers (and even rising 9th graders) in the Midwest — perfect for first-gen students exploring law as a career.

internships.title: Badger Boys State
internships.description: 83rd session of Wisconsin's American Legion Boys State, a residential civics and leadership program held at UW–Eau Claire June 14-20, 2026. Approximately 600 rising high school seniors from across Wisconsin become citizens of one of 27 model cities and 9 counties, elect public officials at city, county, and state levels, and run a functioning model government for a week. Sponsorship of $400 per student is typically covered by a local American Legion post. Hands-on civics, public speaking, leadership, and government education at scale.
internships.whyApply: Gold-standard civics and leadership program for college applications — Boys State alumni include presidents, governors, and Supreme Court justices. Sponsored, so cost is rarely an obstacle.

internships.title: Milwaukee Art Museum — Satellite Teens Internship
internships.description: Year-round internship for Milwaukee-area high school students ages 16–18. Interns explore museum operations behind the scenes, collaborate with local artists, visit working studios and galleries, develop and lead tours for elementary school visitors, and produce a final collaborative project culminating in a public reception. Builds direct experience in arts administration, curation, and arts education that's hard to find as a high schooler.
internships.whyApply: Rare year-round museum internship for high schoolers — leadership-track exposure to a major art museum, with real responsibility leading tours and producing a public-facing project.

internships.title: Milwaukee Art Museum — ArtXpress Summer Internship
internships.description: Four-week summer internship for Milwaukee-area high school students ages 16–18. Interns work alongside local artists to design and create a community mural addressing issues facing Milwaukee, mentor elementary students on collection tours, and participate in studio visits. The summer culminates in a public reveal of the mural displayed on a Milwaukee County Transit System bus that travels the city.
internships.whyApply: A rare paid-quality experience: produce real public art that runs through the city on a transit bus, plus gain teaching experience leading elementary tours.

internships.title: Carthage College Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA) Program
internships.description: Three-week intensive CNA certification program at Carthage College in Kenosha, WI for high school students 16+. Two summer sessions offered (June 12 – July 1 and July 7 – July 29, 2026), each $850 (often covered by scholarships). Students complete the classroom and clinical training required to sit for the Wisconsin CNA exam — a real, portable healthcare credential they can use immediately for paid hospital and care-facility work. Ideal for pre-nursing, pre-med, and allied health students.
internships.whyApply: Walks away with an actual state-recognized healthcare credential in three weeks — one of the fastest paths from high school to paid clinical work and a proven pre-nursing/pre-med signal.

internships.title: SUPREMES Program (Marquette / MCW Joint Biomedical Engineering)
internships.company: Marquette University & Medical College of Wisconsin Joint Department of Biomedical Engineering
internships.description: Students Understanding Principles of Research Education through Medicine, Engineering, and Science. Year-long mentored research experience for Milwaukee-area high school sophomores and juniors. Students are placed in real biomedical research labs across the Greater Milwaukee Area — MCW Translational Research Center, MCW Hub for Collaborative Research, Zablocki VA Medical Center, Marquette Biomedical Engineering Labs, Children's Research Institute, and Versiti — working under experienced faculty and graduate-student mentors. Culminates in a written manuscript, research poster, and formal presentation.
internships.whyApply: Authentic, year-long mentored biomedical research at a top medical school — far more substantive than a one-week summer camp, with publishable-quality output (manuscript + poster + presentation).

internships.title: Future Titans High School (FT-HS)
internships.stipend: WI DPI + UW System scholarships cover all expenses for eligible students
internships.description: One-week summer residential pre-college program for students entering grades 10-12 in fall 2026, with two sessions June 21-27 and July 5-11, 2026. Focus is on developing educational proficiency through problem-solving learning experiences, challenging participants to explore new concepts and discover college/career opportunities on the UW-Oshkosh campus. Wisconsin Department of Public Instruction and University of Wisconsin System provide scholarships that cover all expenses for eligible Wisconsin students — free for those who qualify.
internships.whyApply: A rare fully-scholarshipped residential pre-college week for Wisconsin students — 2 sessions, DPI scholarships, and college campus immersion at UW-Oshkosh.

internships.title: Summer Academy of Medicine
internships.description: Hands-on medical experience for high school students at St. Norbert College in De Pere (Green Bay area). Two 4-day sessions: Session 1 June 16-19, 2026 and Session 2 June 23-26, 2026. Participants gain hands-on experience, connect with medical experts, and learn from professionals in the field across multiple specialties. Designed as an introduction to health careers for students seriously considering medicine, nursing, or allied health.
internships.whyApply: Short, accessible medicine exposure program in the Green Bay area — good for rising 11th-12th graders exploring health careers without a multi-week residential commitment.

internships.title: Lawrence Summer Music Institute
internships.stipend: Tuition $850 (early, by Jan 15) or $950; lodging $355; meals $300
internships.description: Week-long residential music intensive at Lawrence University's highly regarded Conservatory of Music in Appleton, running concurrently July 5-12, 2026 across three programs: Classical Voice, Percussion, and Piano & Strings. Open to students currently in 8th through 12th grades (including those graduating high school in 2026). Participants focus on building classical performance and chamber music skills through private lessons, group coaching, performance opportunities, and workshops with Lawrence Conservatory faculty and guest artists.
internships.whyApply: A true conservatory-level residential week with Lawrence faculty — serious pre-professional training at one of the Midwest's top music programs, open to middle schoolers through graduating seniors.

internships.title: Wisconsin Conservatory of Music 2026 Summer Music Camps
internships.description: A range of 2026 summer music camps at the Wisconsin Conservatory of Music in Milwaukee and partner sites, continuing WCM's longstanding Milwaukee music education tradition. Camps include Music Exploration Camps, Piano Camp, Percussion programs, and more across multiple Wisconsin locations, serving elementary through high school students.
