Space is limited and families are encouraged to register early when registration opens February 1, 2026.
internships.whyApply: Milwaukee's longstanding community conservatory — broad K-12 reach, multiple camp options, and access to serious music instruction at a lower commitment than residential institutes.

internships.title: Summer Engineering Experiences (SEE)
internships.company: Marquette University Opus College of Engineering
internships.description: Non-residential half-day summer engineering experiences on Marquette's Milwaukee campus for rising 10th graders and above. Participants learn new skills, lead hands-on experiments, and explore engineering passions across biomedical engineering, coding and automation, community-focused engineering projects, and other tracks offered by Opus College of Engineering faculty and students. Designed to introduce high schoolers to the breadth of modern engineering in a low-cost, commuter-friendly format.
internships.whyApply: Low-barrier, commuter-friendly engineering exposure at a top Jesuit research university — ideal for Milwaukee-area students testing engineering interests before committing to longer residential programs.

internships.title: High School Science Research Internship Program
internships.description: Nine-week summer science research internship for Madison Metropolitan School District 11th grade students. Interns are matched with authentic science research placements across UW-Madison labs and affiliated Madison research institutions, gaining real experimental experience across biology, chemistry, physics, bioengineering, and environmental science. At the end of the program, interns design a scientific poster to share their work at a September poster session. One of the few MMSD-specific programs with priority access to UW-Madison research labs.
internships.whyApply: A rare substantive 9-week wet-lab research experience reserved for Madison public school students — real data, real mentorship, and a September presentation built in.

internships.title: Inspire U
internships.description: Five-day, four-night residential camp June 15-19, 2026 for students entering grades 9-12 in fall 2025. Inspire U focuses on leadership development and college/career pathway exploration on the UW-La Crosse campus, giving Western Wisconsin and Twin Cities-area students an immersive taste of college residence life, academic advising, and career planning. Run by the Multicultural Student Services pre-college team.
internships.whyApply: One of the few residential Wisconsin pre-college weeks explicitly focused on leadership development and first-gen/multicultural college access — includes dorm life and career-pathway exploration.

internships.title: My River Adventures (MRA)
internships.description: Five-day residential environmental field summer camp July 27-31, 2026 on the UW-La Crosse campus and along the Upper Mississippi River for incoming 9th-12th graders. Students conduct hands-on field work in river ecology, water quality, wetland science, and river-corridor biodiversity — a region of exceptional ecological importance where the Mississippi flyway, Driftless Area, and major confluence rivers meet. Strong option for students considering environmental science, ecology, fisheries, or water resources careers.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on Mississippi River field ecology along the Driftless Area — a unique combination of residential college life and real environmental field science for students considering ecology or water-resources careers.

internships.title: Freshwater High School Experience
internships.stipend: $25 participation fee; all lodging, meals, and activities covered by FCW
internships.description: Three-day residential freshwater science camp July 13-15, 2026 at UW-Oshkosh. High school students conduct hands-on freshwater research, pilot water drones, explore Lake Winnebago waterways on a pontoon boat, learn about freshwater-related college majors and careers, and stay two nights in UW-Oshkosh residence halls. Aside from a $25 participation fee, all costs — lodging, meals, hands-on activities — are covered by the Freshwater Collaborative of Wisconsin. Limited to 20 participants, making it highly selective.
internships.whyApply: A near-free residential freshwater research camp limited to 20 students — water drones, pontoon fieldwork, and direct college/career advising from the state's freshwater workforce network.

internships.title: Freshwater Science Field Experience
internships.company: Freshwater Collaborative of Wisconsin (UW-Stout, UW-Eau Claire, UW-River Falls)
internships.stipend: Full-tuition scholarships available to all participants via FCW funding
internships.description: Five-day immersive summer field experience June 21-25, 2026 for high school juniors and seniors, hosted jointly by UW-Stout, UW-Eau Claire, and UW-River Falls. Students explore western Wisconsin's lakes, streams, and groundwater alongside faculty and college students, conducting real freshwater research across multiple campuses and field sites. Chaperoned lodging is provided at UW-Stout. Maximum of 24 spots, and all participants are eligible for full-tuition scholarships thanks to Freshwater Collaborative of Wisconsin funding.
internships.whyApply: A rare fully-scholarshipped multi-campus freshwater field research week — expose rising seniors to real hydrology and ecology fieldwork across three UW campuses and Western Wisconsin ecosystems.

internships.title: Freshwater Summer Scholars Program
internships.company: UW-Green Bay / Freshwater Collaborative of Wisconsin
internships.description: Paid summer research program at UW-Green Bay where high school students conduct authentic freshwater science research alongside faculty mentors. Scholars commit 120 hours across the summer and receive a $1,750 stipend paid in two installments. Applications open March 16, 2026. Focuses on Lake Michigan / Green Bay watershed issues including water quality, fisheries, aquatic invasive species, and coastal ecology — connecting students directly to Wisconsin's freshwater workforce pipeline.
internships.whyApply: One of the few truly paid high school freshwater research positions in Wisconsin — $1,750 stipend, direct faculty mentorship at UW-Green Bay, and a clear on-ramp into the state's freshwater workforce pipeline.

internships.title: Aspiring Scientists Summer Internship Program (ASSIP)
internships.company: George Mason University College of Science
internships.description: Nationally recognized 8-week full-time summer research internship (June 18 – August 12, 2026) placing high school and undergraduate students with faculty mentors at George Mason and collaborating institutions across biology, chemistry, physics, neuroscience, computer science, bioinformatics, environmental science, and engineering. Interns work 30 hours/week on real research projects, attend weekly seminars, and present posters at a symposium. Format can be fully in-person, fully remote, or hybrid depending on the project and mentor. Wet-lab interns must be 16+ by the start date. Accepted students receive 3 undergraduate credits through GMU ($1,299 tuition; need-based fee waivers for the $25 application fee are available). No minimum GPA and no transcripts required at application stage — selection is based on STEM coursework, personal statement, and recommendations. One of the most respected high school research programs on the East Coast and a strong signal for selective college admissions.
internships.whyApply: A real, faculty-mentored research placement with published and presented output — on par with top university research programs, and one of the few that accepts students at 16 across every major STEM discipline.

internships.title: C-Tech² — Computers and Technology at Virginia Tech
internships.company: Virginia Tech Center for the Enhancement of Engineering Diversity (CEED)
internships.description: Two-week residential summer program for rising high school junior and senior girls introducing them to computer science and the many disciplines of engineering through hands-on lab experiences, a biomedical-focused team engineering design project, and visits to Virginia Tech research labs. Participants work with faculty and graduate-student mentors, receive sessions on financial literacy and the college application process, and have critiques from Bloomberg Industry Group engineers. Evening and weekend programming is led by Virginia Tech undergraduate RAs. Part of CEED's long-running diversity-in-engineering pipeline.
internships.whyApply: One of Virginia's flagship residential engineering diversity programs, with real lab time at a top public engineering school and a team design project critiqued by industry judges.

internships.title: Imagination — Virginia Tech CEED Middle School Engineering Program
internships.company: Virginia Tech Center for the Enhancement of Engineering Diversity (CEED)
internships.description: One-week residential summer program for rising 7th and 8th graders that introduces the world of engineering, computer science, and technology through hands-on projects. Three weeklong sessions are offered each summer; two sessions feature an electrical and computer engineering project (building a video-game console or digital camera, learning to solder) with lab tours and activities across engineering disciplines. Run by Virginia Tech's Center for the Enhancement of Engineering Diversity as an early-pipeline introduction to engineering.
internships.whyApply: A hands-on, project-based first exposure to engineering at a top engineering school — ideal for curious middle schoolers before the high school camp ladder begins.

internships.title: JSA / Jefferson Lab High School Summer Honors Program
internships.company: Thomas Jefferson National Accelerator Facility (Jefferson Lab)
internships.description: Six-week paid summer internship at Jefferson Lab — a U.S. Department of Energy Office of Science national laboratory in Newport News, VA — for local high school students (within ~60 miles of the lab). Interns work alongside Jefferson Lab scientists and engineers on projects in nuclear physics, accelerator science, detector engineering, computational physics, and related technical fields. Requires age 16+ by program start, good academic standing, and a minimum 3.5 GPA. Participants are paid a stipend, selected based on merit from a competitive applicant pool, and gain hands-on experience with a unique DOE accelerator facility.
internships.whyApply: A paid summer placement at a DOE national lab doing real nuclear and accelerator research — one of the only opportunities in the country for high schoolers to work directly with a particle accelerator facility.

internships.title: Virginia Aerospace Science and Technology Scholars (VASTS)
internships.company: Virginia Space Grant Consortium & NASA Langley Research Center
internships.description: Free statewide STEM learning experience for Virginia 11th and 12th graders combining a semester-long online course (Nov–Apr) focused on NASA human spaceflight, lunar and Mars mission design, followed by a selective seven-day residential summer academy at NASA Langley Research Center in Hampton, VA, where the top ~180 scholars complete a teamed human-spaceflight mission design project. Participants can earn up to four free dual-enrollment college credits. Run as a partnership between the Virginia Space Grant Consortium, NASA Langley, the Commonwealth of Virginia, and industry.
internships.whyApply: A free, competitive, NASA-branded statewide academy that combines rigorous online coursework, college credit, and a residential week at Langley — one of the most prestigious aerospace experiences available to Virginia public school students.

internships.title: NIAHD Pre-College Program in American History
internships.company: National Institute of American History & Democracy at William & Mary
internships.description: Three-week residential pre-college program at William & Mary for rising juniors, rising seniors, and graduated seniors who have completed at least sophomore year. Students develop college-level reading and writing skills, participate in seminar discussions with William & Mary faculty, and take daily field trips to Williamsburg-area museums and historic sites — including Colonial Williamsburg, Jamestown, Yorktown, and Monticello — to analyze events of early American history where they actually occurred. Optional weekend participation in a Fairfield Foundation archaeological dig. Two summer sessions offered; students earn transferable college credit. No application fee.
internships.whyApply: One of the most distinctive humanities pre-college programs in the country — pairs rigorous William & Mary seminar teaching with hands-on fieldwork at Colonial Williamsburg, Jamestown, and Monticello.

internships.title: UVA Advance Summer Pre-College Program
internships.description: Four-week residential summer pre-college program for highly motivated rising juniors and seniors. Students enroll in two undergraduate UVA courses — a dedicated UVA Advance core course and an open elective — and earn six transferable undergraduate credits taught by UVA faculty. Participants live in an air-conditioned residence hall on Grounds, experience the full rhythm of UVA college life, and work with small, intensive class cohorts. Requires minimum 3.5 GPA in a college-prep curriculum; students must be 15–18 during the program. Runs from early July to early August.
internships.whyApply: Real UVA undergraduate credit from a flagship public university, with genuine residential college life — far more substantive than most for-profit 'pre-college' experiences.

internships.title: NSWC Dahlgren SEAP (Science and Engineering Apprenticeship Program)
internships.company: Naval Surface Warfare Center Dahlgren Division (U.S. Navy)
internships.description: Eight-week paid summer apprenticeship placing academically talented high school sophomores, juniors, and seniors directly into working Navy research labs at NSWC Dahlgren — the Navy's principal R&D and test-and-evaluation site for surface-ship combat systems, ordnance, directed energy, and strategic-systems support. Apprentices are mentored by Navy scientists and engineers on real projects including autonomous maritime systems, cybersecurity, directed-energy and sensor applications, missile trajectory modeling and defense simulation, and AI/machine learning for naval systems. Stipend paid bi-weekly by direct deposit, with higher rates for returning participants. Administered via the ASEE SEAP portal.
internships.whyApply: A paid federal research internship at a major Navy lab working on problems like directed energy, autonomous systems, and cybersecurity — one of the few true 'paid-to-do-real-research' placements open to high schoolers.

internships.title: Janelia-Loudoun County Resident Summer Internship Program
internships.deadline: Spring 2026 (apply through Loudoun County Public Schools)
internships.description: Seven-week full-time paid summer internship (June 22 – August 7, 2026) at HHMI's Janelia Research Campus, the Howard Hughes Medical Institute's flagship neuroscience and imaging research center in Loudoun County, VA. Open to high school students age 16+ who attend a Loudoun County Public School or are Loudoun County residents. Interns spend 40 hours/week performing real research in biology and computational science under the direct guidance of Janelia group leaders, scientists, and engineers, and also attend seminars by Janelia researchers and participate in journal clubs. LCPS students must apply through the school-administered application process — direct applications are not considered.
internships.whyApply: Paid, full-time research at one of the top neuroscience and imaging institutes in the world — Janelia routinely produces Nature and Cell papers and the work here is as close to 'real science' as a high schooler can get.

internships.title: Summer Residential Governor's School — Humanities
internships.company: Virginia Department of Education (hosted at Radford University)
internships.description: Free four-week residential summer Governor's School for approximately 100 rising high school seniors from across Virginia, hosted on the Radford University campus. The Humanities program offers intensive interdisciplinary study in anthropology, economics, history, literature, media studies, philosophy, political science, psychology, and sociology through seminars, individual and group projects, and faculty-led research. Students are nominated by their home school division and selected by a state committee. All costs are covered by the Commonwealth of Virginia.
internships.whyApply: Virginia's premier free humanities honors program — a month of graduate-seminar-style coursework with the state's top humanities students, paid for entirely by the Commonwealth.

internships.title: Summer Residential Governor's School — Visual and Performing Arts
internships.company: Virginia Department of Education (hosted at Radford University)
internships.deadline: Fall 2025 – early winter (division-level nomination + statewide adjudication)
internships.description: Free four-week residential Summer Residential Governor's School in the Visual and Performing Arts for approximately 250 rising Virginia high school seniors, hosted on the Radford University campus. Students are selected through a competitive statewide adjudication process (audition or portfolio review in front of professionals) in one of five areas: instrumental music, vocal music, visual art, dance, or theatre. During the month, students work with conservatory-level faculty, collaborate with peers from across the state, and perform or exhibit their work. Fully funded by the Commonwealth of Virginia.
internships.whyApply: The most selective free arts conservatory experience in Virginia — four weeks of intensive training and collaboration with top faculty, reserved for adjudicated student-artists statewide.

internships.title: VCU Health Sciences Academy (HSA)
internships.company: Virginia Commonwealth University – PATHS / Division of Student Engagement and Impact
internships.description: Free, selective Health Sciences Academy run by VCU's PATHS office for Richmond-area high school students interested in medicine and the health professions. The two-week summer workshop offers hands-on exposure on the VCU Medical Center (MCV) campus: students learn basic suturing techniques, practice CPR and AED training, use simulation and standardized-patient centers, and learn the basics of ultrasound imaging and patient interaction. Designed as an early pipeline for students considering medicine, nursing, dentistry, pharmacy, and allied health careers at one of Virginia's top academic medical centers.
internships.whyApply: Free, hands-on clinical skills exposure — suturing, ultrasound, CPR, and simulated patients — at a major academic medical center, one of the best low-cost pre-med pipelines in Virginia.

internships.title: KEYS Research Internship
internships.description: 7-week summer research internship for Arizona high school students placing them in University of Arizona faculty labs in bioscience, engineering, environmental health, and biostatistics. Includes career development workshops and a final research showcase. Open to motivated AZ residents age 16+.
internships.whyApply: Arizona's flagship bioscience research training program for high schoolers. Real lab placement with University of Arizona faculty.

internships.title: Med-Start Health Careers Program
internships.description: 6-week residential summer academic enrichment program for rising 11th graders interested in health professions. Students live in UArizona residence halls and participate in college-level coursework, clinical exposure, and mentorship designed to strengthen readiness for health professional school admissions.
internships.whyApply: Long-running residential pre-health pipeline for Arizona juniors. Free residential program with strong record of placing first-gen students into health careers.

internships.title: Summer Engineering Academy (SEA)
internships.company: University of Arizona College of Engineering
internships.description: Residential summer engineering academy for rising 10th-12th grade students. Hands-on workshops across multiple engineering disciplines (aerospace, biomedical, electrical, mechanical, mining, optical, and systems engineering), faculty-led labs, and team design projects.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on multi-discipline engineering residential at one of Arizona's top engineering programs (incl. internationally ranked optical sciences).

internships.title: Helios Scholars Program
internships.description: Paid 8-week biomedical research internship for Arizona high school, undergraduate, graduate, and medical students. Interns are placed in TGen labs working on genomics, cancer biology, neurological disorders, infectious disease, and antimicrobial resistance. Phoenix or TGen North (Flagstaff) campuses.
internships.whyApply: Premier paid biomedical research program in Arizona — competitive placement at one of the country's top translational genomics institutes.

internships.title: Summer High School Internship Program
internships.description: Free 5-week neuroscience research internship at Barrow Neurological Institute for students age 16+. Interns work in faculty labs (Ivy Brain Tumor Center, Neuroimaging Innovation Center, Translational Neuroscience), evaluate scientific literature, develop projects, and present findings. Non-research support roles in publications, marketing, and the foundation are also available.
internships.whyApply: 20+ year-old neuroscience research internship at one of the world's leading neurological institutes — free and highly competitive.

internships.title: Mayo Clinic CARES Program
internships.description: Career Advancement, Research, and Education Summer (CARES) Program offering Arizona high school freshmen, sophomores, and juniors hands-on hospital experience and longitudinal mentorship. 4 weeks in-person in June plus 1 week virtual in July. Designed to expand the pipeline of underrepresented students into health careers.
internships.whyApply: Mayo Clinic-branded pipeline program for Arizona high schoolers — rare access to one of the top hospital systems in the world.

internships.title: Fulton Summer Academy
internships.company: Ira A. Fulton Schools of Engineering, Arizona State University
internships.description: Hands-on summer engineering academy on ASU's Tempe, Polytechnic, and West Valley campuses. Students learn engineering design, rapid prototyping (laser cutting, CAD, Arduino, 3D printing), and prototype testing through application-based projects. Multiple discipline tracks available for rising 9-12.
internships.whyApply: ASU Fulton's flagship engineering camp with full prototyping shops — accessible across the Phoenix metro on three campuses.

internships.title: EPICS Summer Program (Engineering Projects in Community Service)
internships.company: Ira A. Fulton Schools of Engineering, Arizona State University
internships.description: Award-winning social-entrepreneurship engineering camp in which high school students design, build, and deploy systems that solve engineering-based problems for charities, schools, and other nonprofits. Two one-week sessions on the Tempe campus.
internships.whyApply: Connects engineering with community impact — students leave with a real prototype delivered to a real client organization.

internships.title: Summer Engineering Explore (SEE@ASU)
internships.company: Ira A. Fulton Schools of Engineering, Arizona State University
internships.description: Pre-college program for high school juniors and seniors providing a comprehensive overview of 27 undergraduate engineering degrees at ASU. Students interact with faculty, current engineering students, and tour cutting-edge labs and facilities.
internships.whyApply: Best fit for students still exploring which engineering discipline to pursue — broad exposure to one of the largest engineering schools in the U.S.

internships.title: Business Careers Awareness Program (BCAP)
internships.company: Eller College of Management, University of Arizona
internships.description: Week-long residential business and accounting program for high school juniors, run by Eller's Dhaliwal-Reidy School of Accountancy in partnership with EY. Includes coursework in accounting, marketing, ethics, and business communication; case study competition with $500 book scholarship; site visit to the EY Phoenix office; on-campus housing.
internships.whyApply: One of the few free residential business/finance pipelines in Arizona — direct exposure to Big Four accounting via the EY partnership.

internships.title: The Advance Program
internships.company: Sandra Day O'Connor College of Law, Arizona State University
internships.description: Pre-law pipeline program at ASU's Sandra Day O'Connor College of Law introducing high school and undergraduate students to legal education through workshops on legal reasoning, the law school admissions process, and exposure to law school faculty and current law students.
internships.whyApply: Direct exposure to a top-50 law school for students considering pre-law — rare structured pre-law experience for AZ high schoolers.

internships.title: Indigenous Youth Media Workshop
internships.company: Northern Arizona University School of Communication
internships.description: 10-day immersive journalism, storytelling, and digital media bootcamp for high school students grades 9-12 at NAU's Flagstaff campus, with preference given to Indigenous students. Students gain hands-on experience with reporting, audio, video, and photography under the mentorship of NAU faculty and working journalists.
internships.whyApply: One of very few Indigenous-focused journalism pipelines in the Southwest — rare humanities/arts opportunity in northern Arizona.

internships.title: Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Teen Volunteer Program for students age 15+ at the Arizona Science Center in downtown Phoenix. Volunteers work with the Science Center's leadership team to develop, test, and implement new content, programs, and exhibits, lead activities for visitors, and prep science kits for schools across Arizona. Eligible for service-learning credit.
internships.whyApply: Accessible year-round STEM education and science-communication experience in the heart of Phoenix — open to ages 15+.

internships.title: Summer Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Summer hospital teen volunteer program at Banner Desert Medical Center in Mesa for high school students. Roles include patient transport, family support, gift shop, and unit support. Summer applications are accepted January through March only. Includes Banner's Medical Explorers Club for students seriously considering healthcare careers.
internships.whyApply: One of the largest hospital teen volunteer pipelines in the East Valley — direct patient-facing healthcare exposure plus the Medical Explorers Club.

internships.title: Astronomy Camp for Teens
internships.description: Residential astronomy camp run by University of Arizona astronomer Don McCarthy for students age 12-19 on Mt. Lemmon and Kitt Peak. Campers operate research-grade telescopes up to 61 inches, conduct astrophotography, spectroscopy, and digital imaging, and complete their own research projects under some of the darkest skies in North America. Beginning Camp ($1700) and Advanced Camp ($1900) include lodging, meals, transportation in Tucson, and all materials.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on access to 61-inch research telescopes with a University of Arizona astronomer under the darkest skies in the country — one of the oldest residential astronomy camps in the world.

internships.title: Biosphere 2 Summer Science Academy
internships.description: Week-long residential summer academy at the iconic Biosphere 2 research facility 30 miles north of Tucson. Middle and high school students conduct hands-on research projects 'under-the-glass' in the tropical rainforest, savanna, ocean, mangrove marsh, and coastal fog desert biomes while working alongside Biosphere 2 researchers. Culminates with presentations of research findings.
internships.whyApply: Rare opportunity to conduct real ecological research inside the world's largest closed ecosystem research facility with Biosphere 2 scientists.

internships.title: Global Environmental Leadership and Sustainability Program
internships.company: UA Biosphere 2 / UC San Diego Academic Connections
internships.description: Week-long intensive residential summer course jointly offered by the University of Arizona Biosphere 2 and UC San Diego Academic Connections. High school students examine the diverse biomes and climate zones of the Biosphere 2 research facility and the surrounding Sonoran Desert, focusing on environmental leadership, sustainability, and climate science.
internships.whyApply: Co-run by two major research universities at Biosphere 2 — combines Sonoran Desert fieldwork with sustainability leadership training.

internships.title: Summer Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Summer teen volunteer program at Phoenix Children's Hospital, one of the nation's top pediatric hospitals, welcoming over 60 high school students each year (age 16+). Roles include assisting at the in-hospital school, helping families navigate the building, and supporting medical teams in patient care. Companion Medical Explorers Club is available for students seriously exploring healthcare careers.
internships.whyApply: Direct patient-facing exposure at one of the top pediatric hospitals in the country, plus the Medical Explorers Club for students serious about medicine.

internships.title: Teen Art Council
internships.description: Nine-month paid arts-leadership program for Valley high school students. Teen Art Council members serve as museum ambassadors, collaborate on community-oriented projects and events, and represent a diverse group of high school students from across the Greater Phoenix area at different socioeconomic levels and schools.
internships.whyApply: One of the only paid teen arts leadership programs in Phoenix — real museum programming work at the largest art museum in the Southwest.

internships.title: Teens in the Garden
internships.description: Monthly service-learning and career-building program at the Desert Botanical Garden for high school students. Teens volunteer in horticulture, planting, and docent stations; contribute observations to the USA National Phenology Network's Nature's Notebook; and participate in educational field trips (e.g., Japanese Friendship Garden curator tours).
internships.whyApply: Ongoing science volunteer program that contributes to real NPN phenology research at one of the top desert botanical gardens in the world.

internships.title: Governor's Youth Commission
internships.company: Arizona Governor's Office of Youth, Faith and Family
internships.description: Statewide youth leadership commission encouraging high school students in grades 10-12 from all 15 Arizona counties to lead in volunteerism and service learning. Commissioners are divided into five workgroups: conservation and sustainability, domestic violence, education, mental health, and substance abuse prevention. Selective application with essays, letter of recommendation, resume, and interview.
internships.whyApply: Direct engagement with the Arizona Governor's Office on statewide policy workgroups — rare government/policy opportunity for AZ high schoolers.

internships.title: Herberger Institute K-12 Summer Programs
internships.company: Arizona State University Herberger Institute for Design and the Arts
internships.description: Summer K-12 arts and design programs at ASU's Herberger Institute for Design and the Arts — one of the nation's largest comprehensive design and arts schools. Offerings span studio art, film, theater, dance, music, design, and media arts for credit or personal enrichment.
internships.whyApply: Access to one of the top comprehensive arts schools in the country — experience all arts disciplines at ASU.

internships.title: Engineering Summer Youth Camps
internships.company: Northern Arizona University Steve Sanghi College of Engineering
internships.description: Middle and high school summer day camps at NAU's Steve Sanghi College of Engineering running June 1-July 11. Camp themes include civil and environmental engineering, robotics, semiconductors and microelectronics, and video game design. Students build and program robots, develop video games, and explore engineering careers. No prior experience required; discounts available.
internships.whyApply: Northern Arizona's flagship engineering college offers broad day-camp exposure to semiconductors, robotics, and game design — rare semiconductor/microelectronics exposure for HS students.

internships.title: Hispanic/Latine Youth Media Workshop
internships.company: Northern Arizona University School of Communication
internships.description: Two-day immersive broadcast journalism and media production workshop at NAU's School of Communication for Hispanic and Latine high school students. Hands-on instruction in video production, reporting, and storytelling, complementing NAU's longer 11-day Indigenous Youth Media Workshop.
internships.whyApply: Rare Latine-focused media production pipeline in Arizona — opens doors to broadcast journalism careers for underrepresented students.

internships.title: Summer Youth Employment Program
internships.description: Pima County's paid Summer Youth Employment Program places income-eligible high school students ages 16-21 in paid summer jobs with Pima County departments, nonprofits, and community partners. Placements span government, parks and recreation, libraries, public health, and human services. Runs June-July with work-readiness training.
internships.whyApply: Rare county-level paid placement program for HS students — real government and nonprofit work experience plus a paycheck.

internships.title: NASA Goddard OSTEM High School Internship
internships.description: NASA Office of STEM Engagement (OSTEM) paid internship at Goddard Space Flight Center. 10-week summer (May–August), with spring/fall options. U.S. citizen, age 16+, GPA 3.0+, full-time HS or undergrad student. Work alongside Goddard scientists and engineers on real mission and research projects.
internships.whyApply: Work at one of NASA's flagship science centers on real spaceflight and Earth-science missions.

internships.title: National Space Club Scholars Program at NASA Goddard
internships.company: National Space Club & Foundation / NASA Goddard
internships.description: Six-week paid summer program for up to 55 high school students working with NASA scientists and engineers at Goddard Space Flight Center (Greenbelt, MD) or Wallops Flight Facility (VA). Students must be in grade 10–12 at time of application, age 16+, U.S. citizens, and live within 50 miles of either NASA facility.
internships.whyApply: One of the most selective high school NASA experiences in the DMV — direct mentorship with Goddard mission scientists.

internships.title: Johns Hopkins Summer Academic Research Experience (SARE)
internships.description: Eight-week biomedical research internship at Johns Hopkins School of Medicine East Baltimore Campus. Hands-on lab research plus advanced courses in scientific writing, data analysis, mathematics, and bioethics. Runs Mon–Fri 9am–5pm. Targeted at academically prepared high school students from the Baltimore area.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on biomedical research at one of the world's top medical schools, with structured coursework alongside lab work.

internships.title: Johns Hopkins Internship in Brain Sciences (JHIBS)
internships.description: Brain-science research program for rising juniors and seniors. 8-week in-person summer internship at JHU (Baltimore City and metro residents) plus a 5-week virtual program (open nationally). Includes lab placements, lectures, and mentoring in neuroscience.
internships.whyApply: Selective neuroscience research experience under JHU faculty — virtual track opens it to students nationwide.

internships.title: NIST Summer High School Internship Program (SHIP)
internships.company: National Institute of Standards and Technology
internships.description: Full-time, in-person research fellowship at NIST's Gaithersburg, MD campus (also Boulder, CO). Open to current high school juniors and seniors who live within 50 miles of the host campus. ~7 weeks in summer (late June – early August), 40 hrs/week, culminates in a poster session. Unpaid; students provide their own housing/transport.
internships.whyApply: Federal research lab placement with NIST scientists across physics, materials, and measurement science.

internships.title: JHU APL ASPIRE Internship
internships.company: Johns Hopkins University Applied Physics Laboratory
internships.description: Intensive summer internship at JHU APL for high school juniors and seniors. On-site 30–40 hours/week working with APL scientists and engineers across STEM fields. Restricted to permanent residents of specified MD counties (Anne Arundel, Baltimore, Calvert, Carroll, Charles, Frederick, Howard, Montgomery, Prince George's), several Northern VA counties, and DC.
internships.whyApply: STEM career exposure inside a top federally funded research and development lab serving NASA, DoD, and intelligence community sponsors.

internships.title: UMD Terp Young Scholars
internships.description: Three-week pre-college summer program at the University of Maryland for rising sophomores through graduating seniors. Students take one 3-credit UMD course (campus or 100% online) for which both grade and credits post to an official UMD transcript. Requires unweighted 3.0 GPA. First-come, first-served admissions; applications open in early January.
internships.whyApply: Earn real University of Maryland transferable credit while sampling a college major.

internships.title: MICA Pre-College Art & Design Residency
internships.description: Residential pre-college program at MICA for rising high school juniors and seniors. College-level art and design studio courses with portfolio development, with the opportunity to earn up to 3 college credits. Includes critiques, gallery visits, and exposure to MICA faculty.
internships.whyApply: One of the country's leading independent art and design colleges — strong portfolio building for arts-focused applicants.

internships.title: Johns Hopkins CTY Summer Programs
internships.company: Johns Hopkins Center for Talented Youth
internships.description: Three-week residential and day programs run by JHU CTY for advanced learners in grades 2–12. 70+ courses spanning math, science, writing, computer science, and humanities, taught at multiple host campuses. Admission is talent-search based (CTY-eligible test scores).
internships.whyApply: The flagship gifted education program at Johns Hopkins — strong enrichment with rigor signaling for college admissions.

internships.title: CTY Baltimore Emerging Scholars
internships.company: Johns Hopkins Center for Talented Youth
internships.description: Free CTY enrichment program for academically promising Baltimore-area students. Reaches nearly 1,000 students annually through school-year and summer programming, including Saturday classes and accelerated math/science courses. Designed to expand access to advanced learning for Baltimore City Public Schools students.
internships.whyApply: Free, sustained access to JHU CTY-quality enrichment for Baltimore City students.

internships.title: JHU APL High School Cybersecurity Workshop
internships.company: Johns Hopkins University Applied Physics Laboratory
internships.description: Two-week hands-on summer cybersecurity workshop at JHU APL. Students discover, exploit, and patch computer vulnerabilities, with mentoring from APL cybersecurity researchers. Open to high school students with an interest in cybersecurity, computer science, or engineering.
internships.whyApply: Direct mentorship from APL's cybersecurity research staff — strong signal for cyber-track college applicants.

internships.title: Hutton Junior Fisheries Biology Program
internships.description: Eight-week paid summer mentorship program for high school students sponsored by the American Fisheries Society (HQ Bethesda, MD) and funded by NOAA. Students are matched with fisheries professionals within ~45 minutes of their home for hands-on field and lab experience in fisheries science. Open to students in all 50 states; placements vary by year.
internships.whyApply: $3,000 paid mentored field research in fisheries biology — one of the few national programs reaching high schoolers in marine and freshwater science.

internships.title: Goucher Baltimore CAP Program
internships.description: Free Baltimore College Access and Preparation program at Goucher College. Supports historically underrepresented and first-generation college-bound high school students from Baltimore City and Baltimore County with summer experiences on Goucher's campus, college-pathway exposure, career exploration, and hands-on skill building.
internships.whyApply: Free college-access pipeline for first-gen and underrepresented Baltimore students with continued mentorship through senior year.

internships.title: High School Scientific Training and Enrichment Program (HiSTEP)
internships.company: NIH Office of Intramural Training & Education
internships.description: Free summer enrichment program at the NIH Bethesda campus for rising high school seniors from Title I schools (where 30%+ of students participate in the Federal Free/Reduced Lunch Program) who live and attend school in Maryland, Virginia, or DC within ~40 miles of NIH. Participants complete coursework in biomedical science, career exploration, professional development, and college readiness. Must be 17 by mid-June, US citizen/LPR, and have a 3.0+ GPA.
internships.whyApply: NIH-run pipeline program designed specifically for Title I-school juniors in the DMV — exposure to NIH scientists and biomedical career pathways without cost.

internships.title: Summer STEM Program
internships.description: One-week residential STEM program at the US Naval Academy in Annapolis for students entering grades 9, 10, and 11. Participants complete hands-on modules in reverse engineering, materials science, robotics, and structural design challenges led by Academy faculty, interact with midshipmen, and tour Academy labs. $450 program fee with financial aid available for fees and travel; transportation provided to/from BWI airport.
internships.whyApply: Selective service academy STEM exposure with hands-on engineering modules and direct interaction with Naval Academy midshipmen — financial aid available.

internships.title: Henry Hall Fellowship
internships.description: After-school work-study fellowship at the National Aquarium in Baltimore for high school students in grades 10-12 attending public schools in Baltimore City and Baltimore County. Fellows receive a stipend, study urban conservation issues, explore careers in marine science and aquatic conservation, and become advocates for environmental change in Baltimore. Funded by the Henry Hall Endowment, established in 1982 to provide free educational opportunities for Baltimore City Public Schools students.
internships.whyApply: Paid year-round mentorship at one of the country's premier aquariums — accessible to Baltimore-area public school students with a focus on urban conservation and career pathways.

internships.title: Youth Exhibit Guide Program
internships.description: Summer volunteer program at the National Aquarium for local high school students who work with Aquarium staff in the exhibits, share the Aquarium's conservation mission with visitors, and gain exposure to careers in marine biology and aquatic science. Provides realistic work experience and the chance to connect with peers who share science and conservation interests.
internships.whyApply: Direct floor-level experience at the National Aquarium with guest engagement and mentorship from professional aquarists — strong stepping stone into marine science careers.

internships.title: Smithsonian Baltimore Forest Science Internship (SBFSI)
internships.description: High school internship offered by the Smithsonian Environmental Research Center in Edgewater, MD, in partnership with Baltimore-based community organizations. High school interns learn about urban heat, biodiversity, pollinators, light, and soil quality, and design their own research questions in urban ecology. Connects Baltimore students directly to Smithsonian field-research scientists working on the Chesapeake Bay watershed.
internships.whyApply: Smithsonian-led mentored field research with a community partnership specifically reaching Baltimore high school students — student-designed projects in urban ecology.

internships.title: Summer Enrichment Academy — High School
internships.description: One-week immersive summer workshops at UMBC for high school students across science, technology, engineering, the arts, and leadership. Programs are led by UMBC faculty, industry professionals, and graduate students and provide a college-campus experience with hands-on, project-based learning. 2026 sessions: June 22-26 and July 6-10. Standard pricing $374-$499 depending on course.
internships.whyApply: Short, focused 1-week workshops at a top public research university with a strong CS, engineering, and arts portfolio — great low-commitment college-campus introduction.

internships.title: Walters Art Museum Teen Advisory Council (WAMTAC)
internships.description: Paid summer council at the Walters Art Museum in Baltimore (July-August) where teens work on real museum projects, gain professional experience, and shape teen-facing programming alongside Walters staff. Provides behind-the-scenes access to museum operations, curatorial staff, and exhibition planning.
internships.whyApply: Rare paid museum-leadership experience at a major encyclopedic art museum — substantive project work, not just shadowing.

internships.title: Walters Art Museum Teen Photography Course
internships.description: 10-week photography course at the Walters Art Museum for teens, culminating in a group exhibition at the museum. Combines technical photography instruction with critical engagement with the Walters' collection.
internships.whyApply: Sustained photography instruction with a real exhibition outcome at a major museum — strong portfolio addition for visual-arts applicants.

internships.title: Zoo Teens Program
internships.description: Teen volunteer program at the Maryland Zoo for high school students ages 14-17. Participants train as Zoo Guides, then choose tracks in Guest Engagement or Conservation and Research. Teens learn about animals, conservation, and zoo operations, earn service-learning hours, and develop leadership and public-speaking skills.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on zoo experience with structured tracks into conservation/research — accessible entry point into wildlife biology and zoology careers.

internships.title: Computer Programming for the Under-Represented (CPU) Camp
internships.description: Summer technology camp at Bowie State University, an HBCU, designed to serve young Black students ages 12-17 with strong interest in computer innovation. Daily 9:30am-4pm sessions cover computer programming, cybersecurity, computer networking, and other STEM topics. $350 fee includes lunch, materials, and supplies.
internships.whyApply: HBCU-led pipeline program for Black middle and high school students into computer science and cybersecurity — affordable entry to tech careers.

internships.title: Girls Who Will (GWW) Summer Camp
internships.description: Summer technology camp at Bowie State University for middle and high school girls in the DC, Maryland, and Virginia area. Provides innovative technology education for an exclusive cohort (limited to ~15 middle school and ~15 high school girls). Run by Bowie State's Department of Technology and Security as part of its broader community-outreach pipeline.
internships.whyApply: Small-cohort girls-only tech program at an HBCU with personalized mentorship — strong fit for DMV girls exploring computer science.

internships.title: Computer Science Applications Summer Camp
internships.company: Bowie State University Center for Cyber Security and Emerging Technologies
internships.description: Free summer bootcamps at Bowie State University for middle and high school students interested in computer science. Topics include mobile app development, web development, cybersecurity, and AI/machine learning. All bootcamps include free lunch and have no registration fee — making them one of the most accessible CS pipelines for DMV students.
internships.whyApply: Free CS bootcamps with broad topic coverage (mobile, web, cyber, AI/ML) at an HBCU — almost no barrier to entry.

internships.title: Career Immersion Program
internships.company: Mayo Clinic School of Health Sciences
internships.stipend: Full scholarship: housing, meals, transportation covered
internships.description: Free 5-day residential program (June 21-25, 2026) for Minnesota high school sophomores and juniors to explore allied health careers including sonography, phlebotomy, respiratory care, paramedic, and histology. Hands-on with Mayo Clinic experts. Open to MN residents with 2.5+ GPA.
internships.whyApply: Top medical institution exposing MN sophomores/juniors to a broad set of allied health careers with full scholarship.

internships.title: Discover STEM Summer Program
internships.company: University of Minnesota College of Science and Engineering
internships.description: Free 1-week summer program (Aug 3-7 or Aug 10-14, 2026) at UMN Twin Cities for students entering 11th or 12th grade. Lab tours, science experiments, technology demos, and Q&A with researchers. Daily 9am-4pm. Sponsored by 3M.
internships.whyApply: Free deep look at STEM disciplines and academic programs at the U of M's flagship engineering college.

internships.title: Go4Brains
internships.company: University of Minnesota Department of Neuroscience
internships.description: Free week-long July neuroscience program for 9th-11th graders who have faced barriers to STEM/medicine. Engaging neuroscience activities, basic experimental skills, faculty/student mentorship, and long-term career support.
internships.whyApply: Pipeline neuroscience exposure for students underrepresented in STEM/medicine, with ongoing mentorship.

internships.title: University of Minnesota Talented Youth Mathematics Program (UMTYMP)
internships.description: Five-year accelerated math program for highly talented students. Two years to complete the high school math sequence followed by three years of college calculus through multivariable, linear algebra, and differential equations. Up to 12 University credits earned. Qualifying exam in spring of grades 5-7.
internships.whyApply: One of the nation's premier accelerated math programs — top math students can finish multivariable calculus before high school graduation.

internships.title: PRE@Mac Pre-College Program
internships.description: Free residential immersion at Macalester for U.S. high school students from historically under-represented communities in higher education. Live in a residence hall, mentored by current Mac students, and experience small liberal arts college academics.
internships.whyApply: Free college-access pipeline for first-gen and underrepresented students into a top national liberal arts college.

internships.title: Minnesota Institute for Talented Youth (MITY) — Expand Your Mind
internships.description: Two-week residential or commuter summer program at Macalester College for bright and motivated students entering grades 8-12. Courses span acting, chemistry, diplomacy, math, architecture, creative writing, psychology, and engineering.
internships.whyApply: Long-running enrichment program with broad academic course catalog at one of MN's best-known liberal arts campuses.

internships.title: Summer Liberal Arts Institute (SLAI)
internships.description: Three-week credit-bearing residential summer institute at Carleton (July 2026) granting 6 Carleton college credits. Six 3-week courses plus six new 1-week non-credit courses. Students may stack two 1-week courses for a 2-week residency.
internships.whyApply: Earn real Carleton credits while experiencing one of America's top liberal arts colleges.

internships.title: Walker Art Center Teen Arts Council (WACTAC)
internships.description: 13-member teen council at the Walker Art Center plans contemporary art events, programs, and workshops for teens. Oldest teen arts council of its kind in the U.S. Members meet weekly to plan teen-centered arts events and engage with contemporary artists.
internships.whyApply: Nation's longest-running teen arts council at a world-class contemporary art museum — paid leadership and curation experience.

internships.title: Mia Art Team
internships.description: Paid teen staff position at the Minneapolis Institute of Art. Year-round commitment (Thursdays 4:30-7pm + 2nd Sundays 9:30am-5:30pm). Teens build facilitation, leadership, and communication skills while creating opportunities for emerging youth artists and Family Day activities.
internships.whyApply: Paid year-round teen position at one of the Midwest's most respected art museums with leadership/facilitation development.

internships.title: Kitty Andersen Youth Science Center (KAYSC) — High School Program
internships.description: Paid employment as a Science Museum of Minnesota STEM youth worker for grades 9-12. Develop STEM and STEM Justice skills while delivering programming to museum visitors. Includes individualized advising, portfolio development, college visits, and career fair support. Pathway extends post-graduation.
internships.whyApply: Long-running paid pipeline that uses STEM Justice pedagogy to prepare youth for STEM careers and post-secondary success.

internships.title: Genesys Works Twin Cities
internships.description: 8-week summer business technology training followed by paid year-long internship during senior year at partner companies including 3M, Ecolab, and Land O'Lakes. Underserved high school juniors gain IT, business operations, and professional development skills. 60+ hours of coaching during the internship.
internships.whyApply: One of the highest-impact paid HS internship pipelines in the country — places underserved students into Fortune 500 corporate roles.

internships.title: Talent Garden Internship Program
internships.description: Paid internship for high school juniors and seniors of color exploring healthcare careers at Hennepin Healthcare (Minneapolis level-1 trauma center). Mentorship and immersion in clinical environments to build the next generation of diverse healthcare providers.
internships.whyApply: Paid healthcare pipeline for diverse Twin Cities students at a major safety-net hospital.

internships.title: ED PEER Program (Emergency Department Volunteer)
internships.description: High school volunteer program in the Hennepin Healthcare Emergency Department. More immersive than typical hospital ED volunteer programs in the Twin Cities — volunteers assist patients and staff in a level-1 trauma ED.
internships.whyApply: Rare immersive HS-accessible ED volunteering at one of MN's busiest level-1 trauma centers.

internships.title: ZooTeen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Volunteer program for ages 13-17 at the Minnesota Zoo in Apple Valley. Hands-on learning, conservation engagement, and assistance with summer camps. Applications accepted January through April 1.
internships.whyApply: Long-running ZooTeen program for ages 13-17 with conservation leadership development.

internships.title: Girls Inc. Eureka! STEM Internship
internships.company: YWCA Minneapolis (in partnership with UMN College of Science & Engineering)
internships.description: Five-year summer + school-year STEM program for 8th-12th grade girls. Summer programming at the University of Minnesota; school-year enrichment at YWCA Midtown. Internship phase is 4 weeks, 20 hrs/week (Mon-Thu). Mentorship from women in STEM fields.
internships.whyApply: Multi-year STEM pipeline for girls from 8th grade through college, with paid internship phase and university partnership.

internships.title: Mayo Clinic High School Volunteers
internships.description: High school volunteer opportunities at Mayo Clinic Rochester. Hands-on hospital experience supporting patients and staff at one of the world's leading medical institutions.
internships.whyApply: Volunteer at the world's #1-ranked hospital — strong signal for pre-med applicants.

internships.title: Summer Liberal Arts Institute — Computer Science
internships.description: 3-week residential pre-college program for current high school sophomores and juniors. Approaches CS through a liberal arts lens — computer vision, web development, and computer organization with hands-on projects in morning classes and afternoon research groups. Earn up to 6 Carleton credits (≈3 transferable semester credits). Some prior coding experience expected.
internships.whyApply: Top-tier liberal arts CS program with credit-bearing rigor and direct mentorship from Carleton CS faculty and undergrad TAs.

internships.title: Concordia Language Villages — Summer Immersion
internships.description: World-renowned language and cultural immersion summer programs at architecturally authentic villages on Turtle River Lake near Bemidji, MN. 1-week, 2-week, and 4-week credit programs in Arabic, Chinese, Finnish, French, German, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Norwegian, Portuguese, Russian, Spanish, Swahili, and Swedish. High school credit programs accredited by Cognia. Open to elementary, middle, and high school students.
internships.whyApply: The premier language and cultural immersion experience in the U.S. — highly differentiated extracurricular for any student pursuing humanities, international studies, or world languages.

internships.title: Bell Museum Research and Collections Summer Internship
internships.company: Bell Museum of Natural History — University of Minnesota
internships.description: Six-week paid summer Research and Collections internship for Twin Cities-area high school students, recruited primarily from communities historically underrepresented in science. Hands-on STEM experience working with the Bell Museum's natural history specimens, exhibits, and research programs. Year-round teen volunteer roles also available as Touch & See Lab assistants and exhibit guides.
internships.duration: 6 weeks (summer); year-round volunteer roles also available
internships.whyApply: Rare paid hands-on natural history research opportunity for HS students at the U of M's flagship natural history museum.

internships.title: STEPS Engineering Camp for Girls
internships.company: University of St. Thomas — School of Engineering
internships.description: Free one-day Science, Technology, and Engineering Preview Summer (STEPS) program for girls in 6th, 7th, or 8th grade, hosted at the University of St. Thomas School of Engineering in St. Paul. Participants engineer, make, connect with mentors, explore engineering labs, and experience college campus life. Multiple sessions in the last week of June.
internships.whyApply: One of the few free middle-school engineering exposure programs in the Twin Cities specifically for girls — a strong on-ramp to high school STEM opportunities.

internships.title: Wolf Ridge Environmental Learning Center — K-12 Programs
internships.description: Accredited residential environmental education center in Finland, MN serving 10,000+ K-12 students each year. Programs include 3-5 day immersive 'coming-of-age' Wolf Ridge trips, summer camps, family programs, virtual field trips, and teacher institutes. Partnership with St. Paul's Murray Junior High culminates in a 2-week summer Wolf Ridge field experience for HS pre-AP biology pathway. Antioch University New England graduate naturalist training also available.
internships.whyApply: Premier residential environmental education center in the Upper Midwest — strong fit for students interested in environmental science, ecology, and the outdoors.

internships.title: Cancer Research High School Program
internships.company: The Hormel Institute — University of Minnesota
internships.description: Two-week online cancer research program (July 20-July 31, 2026) for rising sophomores, juniors, or seniors from Austin and Mower County. Monday-Friday, 4 hours per day. Focused on cancer research in prevention, diagnosis, treatment, care, and patient support. Run by The Hormel Institute, the U of M cancer research arm in Austin, MN.
internships.whyApply: Direct exposure to active cancer researchers at a UMN-affiliated institute, with a stipend, accessible to greater Austin/Mower County HS students.

internships.title: St. Olaf Music Academy
internships.description: Residential summer music academy for highly skilled high school choir, band, and orchestra musicians at St. Olaf College, one of the nation's premier liberal arts music schools. Includes ensemble rehearsals, master classes, one-on-one lessons, and performances led by renowned conductors and St. Olaf music faculty.
internships.whyApply: St. Olaf is internationally known for its music program; the Music Academy gives serious HS musicians a college-conservatory experience and exposure to St. Olaf faculty.

internships.title: Research High School Intern
internships.description: Paid academic-year research internship in the Research Department of the Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis, on-site Monday-Friday for 6-10 hours per week, September through May. Interns assist research economists with statistical analysis, literature reviews, library research, and data work, and attend Research Department seminars. Open to high school students who have completed economics coursework.
internships.whyApply: One of the only paid HS research internships at a Federal Reserve Bank — exceptional credential for students interested in economics, finance, or public policy.

internships.title: Student Volunteer Program
internships.description: Year-round student volunteer program for students age 14+ across the Minnesota Historical Society's 31 historic sites and museums (including the Minnesota History Center in downtown Saint Paul). Roles span public history interpretation, archives, and museum education. One-year commitment, 25+ hours required; volunteers contributing 50+ hours earn free household admission to all MNHS sites. Strong internship pipeline tied to MN Legacy Amendment funding.
internships.whyApply: Substantive humanities and public history experience at the leading historical institution in Minnesota — ideal for HS students considering history, museum work, or public policy.

internships.title: Children's Minnesota Volunteer Program
internships.description: Long- and short-term volunteer opportunities at Children's Minnesota, one of the largest pediatric health systems in the U.S., with hospitals in Minneapolis and Saint Paul. Volunteer roles include reading to patients and siblings, supporting families on the units, and assisting with fundraising events. High school students gain hands-on exposure to pediatric healthcare.
internships.whyApply: Direct pediatric healthcare exposure at a top children's hospital — excellent option for HS students considering pediatrics, nursing, or child psychology.

internships.title: TRIO Upward Bound at St. Olaf College
internships.company: St. Olaf College — TRIO Upward Bound
internships.description: Federally funded TRIO Upward Bound college-access program at St. Olaf College, including a six-week residential summer academic camp where students live in a St. Olaf residence hall Sunday evening through Friday afternoon. Open to first-generation and income-eligible high school students. Year-round academic enrichment and college-prep mentoring.
internships.whyApply: Strong free college-access pipeline with a residential St. Olaf experience, designed to prepare first-generation and lower-income MN students for college.

internships.title: Summer Seminar for High School Juniors
internships.description: Highly selective 12-day residential program for rising seniors at the Oregon Shakespeare Festival in Ashland, Oregon. Participants attend up to nine OSF plays, take workshops on acting, voice and text work, and meet with actors, directors, and designers from one of the largest regional theaters in the United States.
internships.whyApply: A rare chance for serious young theater students to immerse in a working professional regional theater with nine plays and direct access to OSF artists.

internships.title: Reed College Young Scholars Program
internships.description: Long-running (since 1980) program that selects high school seniors from the greater Portland metropolitan area to take one college class at Reed each semester of their senior year while enrolled in high school. Students attend Reed classes alongside Reed undergraduates with full faculty support.
internships.whyApply: One of the country's longest-running dual-enrollment programs at a nationally respected liberal arts college, genuine Reed coursework on the Reed campus.

internships.title: Reed College STEP-UP Summer Research
internships.description: NIDDK-funded Short-Term Research Experience for Underrepresented Persons (STEP-UP) placement at Reed College. Provides hands-on summer biomedical research experience for high school students from underrepresented backgrounds interested in diabetes, digestive, kidney, endocrine or blood research careers.
internships.whyApply: Paid NIH-funded biomedical research experience at Reed for underrepresented high school students. Strong mentorship and a nationally competitive program.

internships.title: Nike Summer Internship Program
internships.description: Selective 10-12 week summer internship at Nike World Headquarters in Beaverton across Design, Engineering, Finance, Marketing, Merchandising, Supply Chain, Technology and more. About 200 interns accepted annually. Summer interns earn $20-$30 per hour plus housing and relocation stipends. Primarily college and high-college-age interns aged 16+.
internships.whyApply: Flagship paid corporate internship at a global brand headquartered in Oregon. Exposure to design, business, finance and technology roles at Nike HQ.

internships.title: Portland State University Dual Enrollment High School Program
internships.description: Portland State's High School Programs allow qualified high school students to earn college credit by enrolling in PSU courses during the summer and academic year. Summer Term at PSU is open to high school students seeking college credit before graduation.
internships.whyApply: Affordable pathway to earn real college credit at Oregon's urban research university before finishing high school.

internships.title: PSU Summer Brass Academy
internships.company: Portland State University School of Music & Theater
internships.description: Intensive summer brass performance academy at Portland State University's School of Music & Theater. High school brass students improve solo and ensemble performance skills with focus on intonation, technique, tone quality and musicianship through PSU faculty instruction.
internships.whyApply: Dedicated brass-focused summer intensive on a university music school campus for serious student brass players.

internships.title: Portland Summer Percussion Academy
internships.company: Portland State University School of Music & Theater
internships.description: One-week intensive summer percussion camp at Portland State University (since 2013) where high school percussionists work directly with professional percussionists across a wide range of styles and techniques. Includes clinics, ensemble work and masterclasses.
internships.whyApply: Long-running PSU percussion intensive giving serious student percussionists access to professional performers and a collegiate music environment.

internships.title: Oregon Coast Aquarium Youth Volunteer Program
internships.description: High school youth volunteer program at the Oregon Coast Aquarium in Newport. Teens rotate to different exhibits, educate guests about marine life and conservation, and learn about marine science careers while developing public-facing professional and leadership skills.
internships.whyApply: Front-line guest-facing experience in marine education and conservation at one of Oregon's premier aquariums on the coast.

internships.title: Randall Children's Hospital Teen Volunteer Program
internships.company: Legacy Health - Randall Children's Hospital at Legacy Emanuel
internships.description: Volunteer program at Randall Children's Hospital at Legacy Emanuel in Portland. High school volunteers support patients, families and staff across pediatric units including acute care floors with infants and toddlers. Applicants must meet Legacy's age and commitment requirements.
internships.whyApply: Rare pediatric hospital volunteer placement in Portland. Strong clinical exposure for students interested in medicine, nursing or child health.

internships.title: Providence Portland Medical Center Volunteer Program
internships.description: Teen and young adult volunteer program at Providence Portland Medical Center. Applicants must be at least 16 years old and commit to a minimum of 6 consecutive months, 2-4 hours per week. Includes opportunities at the Center for Medically Fragile Children.
internships.whyApply: Longitudinal clinical exposure at a major Portland hospital system. Highly relevant for pre-med and nursing applicants who need sustained clinical hours.

internships.title: Willamette University Camp College
internships.description: Free three-day, two-night pre-college experience at Willamette University in Salem for rising high school juniors and seniors. Students take a sample college class, learn how to navigate the college search and application process, and experience residential college life on the Willamette campus.
internships.whyApply: Free residential college-prep experience at a respected Oregon liberal arts university, demystifying the college search process.

internships.title: PNCA Pre-College Weekend Workshops
internships.company: Pacific Northwest College of Art (Willamette University)
internships.description: Weekend pre-college art and design workshops for high school students ages 14-18 at Pacific Northwest College of Art in Portland. Options in studio art, creative technology and design, taught by PNCA faculty. Complements PNCA's two-week Pre-College Summer Intensive with a portfolio review.
internships.whyApply: Lower-commitment on-ramp to a professional art school, useful for students building a portfolio without committing to a full summer intensive.

internships.title: VMFA Summer in the Studio
internships.description: Intensive weeklong visual arts classes for teens ages 13–18 at Virginia's state art museum in Richmond. Professional teaching artists lead studio sessions in drawing, painting, sculpture, film/animation, fashion design, and mixed media, with hands-on access to VMFA galleries, conservation studios, and a permanent collection of over 50,000 works. Students develop portfolio-ready work and can progress through advanced studio tracks year over year. Scholarships available through VMFA's Statewide Partnership.
internships.whyApply: A top-tier, low-cost way to build a college art portfolio inside a major encyclopedic museum — taught by practicing artists with field trips through VMFA's own collection.

internships.title: VCUarts Summer Pre-College Program
internships.company: Virginia Commonwealth University School of the Arts (VCUarts)
internships.description: Three-week residential pre-college program (July 12–31, 2026) for rising high school sophomores, juniors, and seniors at VCUarts — ranked consistently as the #1 public art and design school in the U.S. by U.S. News. Students take a single non-credit studio course taught by VCUarts faculty in fields like graphic design, painting+printmaking, sculpture, fashion, communication arts, kinetic imaging, dance, theatre, or music, while living in VCU dorms in downtown Richmond. Portfolio development is a core focus; participants leave with college-ready creative work and a real sense of life at a major urban art school.
internships.whyApply: Residential deep-dive at the #1 public art school in the country. A strong signal for applications to VCUarts, RISD, Parsons, SCAD, MICA, and similar programs.

internships.title: Inova Fairfax Medical Campus High School Summer Volunteer Program
internships.description: Structured hospital volunteer program that places rising 10th–12th graders inside one of Virginia's largest Level I trauma centers for a dedicated summer session (Inova Fairfax Medical Campus runs June 10–19, 2026; other Inova hospitals run June 22 – August 16/23). Volunteers are assigned to departments such as patient care services, emergency, surgical services, oncology, retail, and hospital administration, with direct exposure to clinical teams and patients. Typical commitment is two 3–4 hour shifts per week. Considered one of the strongest hospital volunteer pipelines in Northern Virginia for students considering medicine, nursing, or allied health.
internships.whyApply: One of the best free, real-hospital exposures in Northern Virginia — direct patient-care area placement at a Level I trauma center, with a track record of feeding students into medical and nursing programs.

internships.title: Oliver Hill/Samuel Tucker Pre-Law Institute
internships.company: Virginia State Bar Young Lawyers Conference + University of Richmond School of Law
internships.description: Week-long residential pre-law institute (July 5–10, 2026) named for civil-rights attorneys Oliver Hill and Samuel Tucker, run by the Virginia State Bar Young Lawyers Conference in partnership with the University of Richmond School of Law. Rising 10th–12th-grade Virginia students live on the University of Richmond campus and attend mock law classes (with overnight reading assignments) on constitutional, criminal, and civil-rights law, plus seminars on law-school admissions, LSAT strategy, and the legal profession. Students meet practicing Virginia lawyers, judges, and law-school faculty and tour Virginia court facilities. Full scholarships — program is tuition-free for admitted students.
internships.whyApply: Free, residential, and named for two of Virginia's greatest civil-rights lawyers — one of the few formal pre-law programs in the state with real law-school faculty and practicing attorneys.

internships.title: Sorensen Institute High School Leaders Program (HSLP)
internships.company: Sorensen Institute for Political Leadership, University of Virginia Cooper Center
internships.description: Two-week residential civics and political-leadership program (July 11–25, 2026) run by the Sorensen Institute for Political Leadership at UVA's Weldon Cooper Center for Public Service. A small cohort of about 32 rising juniors and seniors who attend Virginia high schools (or are Virginia residents) live in UVA dorms in Charlottesville and attend daily sessions on constitutional law, Virginia government, campaign strategy, ethics in politics, media relations, and nonpartisan policy debate, with visits to the Virginia General Assembly, federal agencies, and meetings with elected officials from both parties. Must be 16+ by program start. Need-based financial aid available.
internships.whyApply: Virginia's premier nonpartisan civic-leadership residency — tiny cohort, real legislators, and meaningful for students interested in government, public policy, law, or civic journalism.

internships.title: UVA Inspire Summer
internships.description: Multi-track summer pre-college program at UVA's Northern Virginia campus in Falls Church for rising 10th–12th-graders. Students choose one focused track — Business (finance, marketing, entrepreneurship), Engineering (design thinking, intro coding, robotics), or Creative Writing (fiction, poetry, personal essay) — and spend the session in small cohorts taught by UVA instructors and NoVA industry professionals. Commuter-friendly for students in Fairfax, Arlington, Loudoun, Prince William, and DC-metro suburbs. A low-cost, low-lift alternative to the Charlottesville residential programs.
internships.whyApply: The only UVA pre-college option in Northern Virginia — accessible for DC-metro commuters and useful for students exploring business, engineering, or writing before committing to a residential program.

internships.title: Virginia Tech Explore Architecture Summer Program
internships.description: Weeklong architecture and design immersion at Virginia Tech's top-ranked School of Architecture (historically ranked #1 public graduate architecture program in the U.S.). Rising 9th–12th-graders, offered in two formats: an on-campus residential session in Blacksburg (June 21–27, 2026) where students live in VT dorms and work in the architecture studios, and a fully virtual session (July 14–19, 2026). Led by VT architecture faculty and grad students in partnership with LaBella Associates, with studio projects, model-making, site analysis, and a final portfolio review. Open to all skill levels — no prior architecture experience required.
internships.whyApply: Studio-based introduction at a top-ranked architecture school, with a virtual option that makes it accessible regardless of location — a strong portfolio piece for architecture applications.

internships.title: Virginia Tech Pathways for Future Engineers Pre-College Program
internships.company: Virginia Tech Center for Enhancement of Engineering Diversity (CEED)
internships.description: Two-week residential engineering pre-college program at Virginia Tech's College of Engineering for Virginia-resident rising 10th–12th-graders who are potential first-generation college students (neither parent has graduated from a four-year institution). Hosted by the Center for Enhancement of Engineering Diversity (CEED), participants live in VT dorms in Blacksburg and do hands-on projects across mechanical, civil, electrical, computer, aerospace, biomedical, and industrial engineering. Includes team-based design challenges, lab work with engineering grad students, and engineering admissions advising. Heavily subsidized — low or zero cost for admitted students.
internships.whyApply: One of the most accessible engineering residency experiences in Virginia for first-gen students — real VT engineering labs and advising, at little to no cost.

internships.title: W&M Camp Launch
internships.company: William & Mary Center for Gifted Education
internships.description: Two-week residential summer camp on the William & Mary campus for rising 7th–10th-grade high-ability students from low-income households across Virginia. Students take two courses — a STEM course and a writing course — developed by the W&M Center for Gifted Education specifically for this population, in partnership with the W&M School of Education, College of Arts & Sciences, and the Batten School & VIMS (marine science). Evening sessions focus on mental well-being, empathy, and resilience. Fully funded for admitted students with 216+ participants from 27+ school divisions. Considered a model program for gifted access.
internships.whyApply: Fully funded residential program at a Public Ivy for low-income gifted students — the most accessible gifted-ed residency in Virginia and a strong stepping stone into selective summer programs.
