internships.title: W&M Saturday/Summer Enrichment Program (SEP)
internships.company: William & Mary Center for Gifted Education
internships.description: Long-running gifted enrichment program at William & Mary's School of Education for academically talented students in grades K–10. The Summer Enrichment Program offers single-week residential and commuter sessions in Williamsburg, with advanced, fast-paced courses in STEM, humanities, creative writing, law and public policy, forensic science, marine science, and debate — taught by W&M faculty and specially trained teachers. Saturday sessions run during the academic year. Used as an on-ramp to W&M's Camp Launch, NIAHD, and the Virginia Governor's Schools.
internships.duration: 1 week per session (multiple sessions, summer + Saturdays)
internships.whyApply: Virginia's longest-running gifted enrichment pipeline — flexible weeklong sessions that scale from elementary to rising 11th graders and feed into more selective W&M and Governor's School programs.

internships.title: George Mason ASSIP — Aspiring Scientists Summer Internship Program
internships.company: George Mason University College of Science
internships.description: Eight-week summer research internship at George Mason University in Fairfax for high school and undergraduate students. Interns work in a GMU faculty lab (or industry/government partner lab) in fields ranging from molecular medicine, proteomics, and neuroscience to computational science, chemistry, bioengineering, and environmental science. Wet-lab and hybrid interns must be at least 16 by June 18, 2026; computational tracks accept younger students. Program runs Monday-Friday 9-5 (30 hours/week), kicks off with orientation on June 18, 2026, and culminates in the ASSIP Research Symposium on August 12, 2026. Tuition $1,299; fee waivers and merit awards available.
internships.whyApply: One of the largest and longest-running formal high school research programs on the East Coast — authentic mentored lab research with a culminating symposium and potential publications/poster presentations.

internships.title: NASA Langley OSTEM High School Internship
internships.company: NASA Langley Research Center – Office of STEM Engagement
internships.description: Paid NASA OSTEM internship placed at NASA Langley Research Center in Hampton, VA — NASA's first field center and the aerospace R&D hub that leads work on atmospheric science, advanced materials, aeronautics, hypersonics, and Artemis lunar/Mars exploration. High school interns contribute to active NASA projects under the guidance of NASA mentors. The summer session is 10 weeks (late May/early June through August); 16-week fall and spring sessions are also available. Eligibility: U.S. citizen, full-time student, cumulative GPA at least 3.0. Applications submitted through NASA's central intern portal (intern.nasa.gov).
internships.whyApply: Paid, named NASA internship at a historic NASA field center — the single strongest aerospace credential a Virginia high school student can earn, with a direct pipeline into NASA/industry undergraduate roles.

internships.title: JSA / Jefferson Lab High School Summer Honors Program
internships.company: Thomas Jefferson National Accelerator Facility (Jefferson Lab) + Jefferson Sciences Associates
internships.description: Paid eight-week summer research internship for high school students at the U.S. Department of Energy's Thomas Jefferson National Accelerator Facility (Jefferson Lab) in Newport News — home of the CEBAF 12 GeV continuous electron beam accelerator used for nuclear and particle-physics research. Students are matched with Jefferson Lab scientists, engineers, or computing staff for real technical projects in areas such as nuclear physics, accelerator science, detector engineering, electronics, scientific computing, and data analysis. Eligibility: attend a local high school within 60 miles of Jefferson Lab, be at least 16 by the start of the program, be in good academic standing with a cumulative GPA of at least 3.3.
internships.whyApply: One of only a handful of paid DOE national-lab high school research programs on the East Coast — genuine accelerator/physics research mentorship, a strong signal for physics and engineering applications.

internships.title: HHMI Janelia Loudoun County Resident Summer Internship
internships.company: Howard Hughes Medical Institute – Janelia Research Campus
internships.description: Paid 7-week full-time (40 hr/wk) summer research internship (June 22 – August 7, 2026) at HHMI's Janelia Research Campus in Ashburn for high school students enrolled in Loudoun County Public Schools or residents of Loudoun County aged 16+. Interns work alongside Janelia group leaders, scientists, and engineers on active research projects in neuroscience, imaging, computational biology, and bioengineering at one of the most scientifically intense biomedical research campuses in the country. Interns also attend Janelia seminars, journal clubs, and community events. Requires one recommendation letter from a math or science teacher and a 300–500 word statement of motivation and research goals.
internships.whyApply: Paid, full-time biomedical research at one of the most prestigious biomedical research campuses in the country, designed specifically for Loudoun County students — essentially unmatched as a local NoVA biomedical credential.

internships.title: NIAHD Pre-College Program in Early American History
internships.company: William & Mary — National Institute of American History & Democracy (NIAHD) + Colonial Williamsburg Foundation
internships.description: Three-week residential pre-college program offering college-credit American history courses taught 'on site' at the eastern Virginia museums and historic sites that shaped the founding of the United States — Colonial Williamsburg, Jamestown, Yorktown, Monticello, and Mount Vernon. Rising high school sophomores, juniors, and seniors (and rising college freshmen) live in a W&M dorm in Williamsburg and earn W&M college credit through one of three courses offered in Summer 2026. Activities include participating in an active archaeological dig, college-level reading and writing, and field trips to historic sites most students only read about. No prerequisites, no application fee; need-based financial aid available.
internships.whyApply: A residential pre-college humanities program taught inside Colonial Williamsburg with a working archaeological dig — an unusually distinctive humanities credential and a strong signal for students applying to history, museum studies, public policy, and pre-law programs.

internships.title: NSWC Dahlgren Science and Engineering Apprenticeship Program (SEAP)
internships.company: Naval Surface Warfare Center Dahlgren Division (Department of the Navy)
internships.description: Paid 8-week summer research apprenticeship at the Naval Surface Warfare Center Dahlgren Division — the Navy's premier research and development site for surface warfare, strategic and combat systems, directed energy, autonomous systems, cybersecurity, and weapons integration. Interns are matched with Navy scientist and engineer mentors and contribute to real research, development, test and evaluation, systems engineering, and integration work. Eligibility: U.S. citizen (dual citizens and permanent residents are not eligible), at least 16 by the start of the internship, enrolled in a U.S. high school. Administered centrally through ASEE's SEAP program.
internships.whyApply: One of the most substantive paid DoD research experiences available to high school students in Virginia — authentic Navy research under mentorship, relevant for students pursuing engineering, CS, cybersecurity, and defense careers.

internships.title: U.S. Air Force Academy Summer Seminar
internships.description: Selective 5-day in-person residential program for rising high school seniors interested in the Air Force Academy and military STEM. Participants live in cadet dorms, eat at the cadet dining facility, explore academic facilities and research labs, and complete workshops in engineering, leadership, athletics, and the military experience. Three sessions in June 2026 (June 2-6, 8-12, 15-19). Selection is whole-person — leadership, academics, and athletics — and the program is the most direct on-ramp to a USAFA appointment. Approximate cost ~$300; limited scholarships.
internships.whyApply: The single most important pre-application experience for any student considering a service academy — direct mentorship by cadets, deep academic immersion, and a major signal in the appointment process.

internships.title: Aspen Music Festival and School — P.A.L.S. (Private Arts Lessons in Summer)
internships.description: Summer music study program for local Roaring Fork Valley students entering grades 6-12 who play orchestral or chamber instruments. Participants receive five private one-hour lessons from members of the Aspen Music Festival and School community, exclusive access to the AMFS summer season (one of the most prestigious classical music festivals in the world), and chamber music coaching. The full-session AMFS runs June 24 to August 23, 2026 with more than 450 advanced students from around the world.
internships.whyApply: P.A.L.S. is one of the only ways for younger and pre-conservatory students to access the world-class faculty and performance environment of one of the top classical music festivals on the planet.

internships.title: Colorado School of Mines Engineering Design Summer Camp
internships.company: Colorado School of Mines — Conference and Event Services / Youth Camps
internships.description: Five-day residential or commuter engineering design camp on the Mines campus in Golden, CO for rising high school sophomores, juniors, and seniors (grades 10-12). Students work hands-on with Mines faculty and college mentors on the engineering design process, prototyping, and team challenges that mirror real Mines coursework. Part of the broader Mines Youth Camps program that also includes Magic of Science (June 15-18), High Voltage (June 22-25), and LEGO Mindstorms Robotics (June 29-July 2) camps for younger students.
internships.whyApply: Direct exposure to Mines engineering culture for students who may want to apply to one of the country's top STEM-focused public universities — the design camp builds a portfolio piece and a relationship with Mines.

internships.title: CS@Mines Cyber Camp
internships.company: Colorado School of Mines — Department of Computer Science
internships.description: Cybersecurity-focused summer camp for high school students hosted by the Mines Computer Science department. Students learn the fundamentals of network security, ethical hacking, cryptography, and secure systems through hands-on labs led by Mines CS faculty and graduate students. Designed to introduce high schoolers to the cybersecurity career pipeline and the CS@Mines program — one of the strongest CS programs in the Mountain West.
internships.whyApply: Cybersecurity is one of the fastest-growing CS subfields and CS@Mines provides a direct line to faculty and labs at a top-tier engineering school.

internships.title: Walking Mountains Science Center — High School Natural Resource Internship
internships.description: Paid summer high school internship in Eagle County for local students that combines outdoor learning, conservation work, and community engagement. Funded by the National Forest Foundation, interns work with Walking Mountains naturalists and US Forest Service staff on trail and habitat stewardship, environmental education, and visitor center programming on Vail Mountain and across the Eagle River Valley. Interns earn college credit and a stipend.
internships.whyApply: One of the only paid environmental field internships in the Colorado mountains specifically designed for high school students — a strong fit for students interested in conservation, ecology, or US Forest Service careers.

internships.title: Colorado Rocky Mountain School — High School High Scholar
internships.description: Five-week residential summer academic and outdoor enrichment program at Colorado Rocky Mountain School (CRMS) in Carbondale, CO, running June 22 to July 25, 2026. Students take immersive courses in writing, science, art, math, and humanities taught by CRMS faculty while also participating in CRMS's signature outdoor program (rock climbing, kayaking, mountaineering, backpacking). Combines academic rigor with adventure-based learning in the Roaring Fork Valley.
internships.whyApply: A rare program that pairs serious academic coursework with serious outdoor expedition learning — appealing to students who want both intellectual challenge and significant time outside.

internships.title: CU Denver Pre-College High School Program
internships.company: University of Colorado Denver — Center for Pre-Collegiate and Academic Outreach Programs
internships.description: CU Denver's Pre-College High School Program offers a portfolio of summer camps and credit-bearing high school programs on the downtown Denver Auraria campus, including Architecture in the City (College of Architecture and Planning), BOLT (Bioengineering Opportunities and Leadership Training), and GenCyber (free NSA/NSF-funded cybersecurity). Students experience college-level coursework, urban field study, and hands-on prototyping with CU Denver faculty.
internships.whyApply: Direct access to CU Denver labs and studios in the heart of downtown Denver — and GenCyber is fully free thanks to NSA/NSF funding, removing the cost barrier that blocks most pre-college programs.

internships.title: Denver Health High School Internships
internships.company: Denver Health — Office of Education / Career Center
internships.description: Denver Health offers a portfolio of high school internship and shadowing experiences through its Office of Education, designed to introduce Denver-area high schoolers to careers in medicine, nursing, public health, and allied health professions at Colorado's primary safety-net hospital. Programs include career exploration, hospital shadowing, and structured exposure to physicians, nurses, and clinical staff across departments.
internships.whyApply: Denver Health is the city's safety-net hospital and Level I trauma center — a uniquely high-volume, high-acuity environment for high schoolers exploring medicine and public health.

internships.title: AccessDU — University of Denver Pre-College Access Program
internships.description: AccessDU is the University of Denver's signature college access program for high school students from across the country. Scholars attend a three-day immersive summer experience on the DU campus connecting with current students, faculty, and DU staff, then receive ongoing support, mentoring, and college search guidance through their senior year. Scholars who later apply to DU receive a fee waiver and may receive a Pathways Scholarship of up to $7,500/year, renewable for four years.
internships.whyApply: Combines college exposure with a real, renewable financial aid pathway — about 20-25% of AccessDU scholars ultimately enroll at DU, and Pathways Scholarships make a private college affordable for qualifying families.

internships.title: Bemis School of Art at the Colorado Springs Fine Arts Center — Pre-College Studios
internships.company: Bemis School of Art at the Colorado Springs Fine Arts Center at Colorado College
internships.description: Studio art classes and pre-college portfolio courses for teens at the Bemis School of Art, the educational arm of the Colorado Springs Fine Arts Center at Colorado College. Programs include painting, drawing, ceramics, sculpture, printmaking, photography, and mixed media taught by working artists and Colorado College arts faculty. Teens have access to the FAC's museum collections and exhibitions and can build a college-application portfolio.
internships.whyApply: One of the most respected community art schools in the Mountain West, attached to a museum and Colorado College — a serious option for students building a fine arts portfolio.

internships.title: Yale Young Global Scholars (YYGS)
internships.description: Academically intensive two-week residential program on Yale's New Haven campus for rising juniors and seniors. Thematic sessions include Politics/Law/Economics, Literature/Philosophy/Culture, Innovations in Science & Technology, and Solving Global Challenges. Small seminars, lectures from Yale faculty, and collaborative capstone projects. Yale provides over $3M in need-based financial aid, including full-tuition discounts.
internships.whyApply: One of the flagship Ivy League pre-college experiences. Global cohort, serious academics, and substantial need-based aid make it accessible to students from all backgrounds.

internships.title: Yale Summer Session Pre-College
internships.description: Credit-bearing Yale courses opened to qualified high school students entering their senior year (or current seniors). Students take actual Yale University courses for a grade and transcript alongside Yale undergraduates. Both 5-week and 2-week session options. Must be at least 16 years old at program start.
internships.whyApply: Take real Yale courses with real Yale professors and a real Yale transcript. Best option for students who want genuine college academics rather than summer-camp-style pre-college.

internships.title: UConn Pre-College Summer (PCS)
internships.description: Residential one-week non-credit sessions (and a two-week credit option) at UConn's Storrs campus. Students live in dorms, eat in dining halls, and take classes in STEM, humanities, social sciences, and arts taught by UConn faculty and industry professionals. For current freshmen, sophomores, and juniors aged 14-17 with at least a 2.0 GPA.
internships.whyApply: Affordable, in-state flagship pre-college experience. A strong fit for CT students exploring majors before applying to UConn or similar flagships.

internships.title: UConn Health High School Student Research Apprentice Program
internships.company: UConn Health / UConn School of Medicine
internships.description: Five-week paid biomedical research apprenticeship at UConn Health in Farmington or at Central Connecticut State University in New Britain. Students work in the laboratories of UConn School of Medicine or School of Dental Medicine researchers on real projects involving cell culture, molecular biology, and biomedical techniques. Designed to introduce CT students from underrepresented backgrounds to careers in health and biomedical sciences.
internships.whyApply: A rare paid in-state biomedical research experience at Connecticut's academic medical center — designed specifically for CT high schoolers and a strong pipeline into medical and dental careers.

internships.title: Yale New Haven Hospital High School Volunteer Program
internships.description: Established high school volunteer program at one of the top academic medical centers in the United States. Volunteers must be at least 15 years old. Roles include assisting patients and visitors with wayfinding and supporting patients and staff on inpatient units. Summer and year-round sessions available. Hospital provides college and scholarship references for students who fulfill their commitment.
internships.whyApply: Direct clinical exposure at a nationally ranked academic medical center — one of the strongest ways for a CT teen to test interest in medicine and nursing.

internships.title: Choate Rosemary Hall Summer Programs
internships.description: Since 1916, Choate Rosemary Hall has offered academic enrichment summer programs with over 100 courses. Options include Immersion Geometry, the Theater Arts Institute, the JFK '35 Institute in Government, the English Language Institute, Competition Robotics, Oil Painting, World Issues, Economic Principles, and more. Residential and day options, 2-5 week sessions on the historic Wallingford campus.
internships.whyApply: One of the oldest and most respected boarding-school summer programs in the country, with signature institutes in government, theater, and geometry.

internships.title: Wesleyan Summer Session Pre-College
internships.description: Wesleyan University's Summer Session opens its intensive, credit-bearing undergraduate courses to high school students. Students complete full semester-length Wesleyan courses in just over four weeks, earning Wesleyan credit. Offerings include writing, literature, government, biology, data analysis, and more. Two sessions: Session I (May 27 - June 23) and Session II (June 29 - July 24).
internships.whyApply: A genuine liberal-arts college experience — credit-bearing Wesleyan classes at one of the top liberal arts colleges in the country.

internships.title: Wesleyan Summer Science Camp for Girls
internships.company: Wesleyan University — Joint Center for Community Programs
internships.description: One-week summer science camp at Wesleyan for middle and high school girls from Middletown and surrounding CT towns. Hands-on experiments, interactive STEM activities, and field trips, mentored by Wesleyan science faculty and student program assistants. Free or very low cost for local students.
internships.whyApply: A free or near-free STEM camp for local girls at a highly ranked liberal arts college — strong early-career science exposure for middle and high school students in central Connecticut.

internships.title: Connecticut Science Center Teen Innovation Program
internships.description: Six-week summer teen ambassador program at the Connecticut Science Center in downtown Hartford. Teens (primarily from under-resourced Greater Hartford communities) create original STEM programs and exhibits to teach museum visitors. Prerequisites include completing the Investigate! program or one full school year on the Teen Advisory Council.
internships.whyApply: A paid-adjacent museum teen leadership experience with a strong equity focus for Greater Hartford students — pairs STEM content with real communication and exhibit-design practice.

internships.title: Quinnipiac University Summer Programs for High School Students
internships.description: Short-form summer programs for high school students at Quinnipiac University's Hamden campus. Fields include data science, healthcare career exploration, game design, interactive media, cybersecurity, communications, photography, film, and creative writing. Both residential and commuter options available.
internships.whyApply: Career-focused short programs with strong offerings in healthcare, game design, and sports media — fields where Quinnipiac has nationally recognized undergraduate programs.

internships.title: Hartford Magnet Trinity College Academy Summer Writing Institute
internships.description: Summer writing program run by Trinity College faculty for Hartford Magnet Trinity College Academy high school students. Introduces students to the rigors of college-level writing in a fun, interactive environment, with a focus on academic and creative writing. Companion 'Designing Your College Experience' program also runs concurrently for college-readiness coaching.
internships.whyApply: A structured, Trinity-faculty-led writing program specifically for Hartford public school students — a strong, free, college-level humanities experience.

internships.title: Yale Peabody Museum EVOLUTIONS Internship
internships.company: Yale Peabody Museum of Natural History
internships.description: Paid summer research internship for New Haven and West Haven public high school students. Work alongside Yale scientists in fields like vertebrate paleontology, geochemistry, anthropology, and cultural history. Approximately 15-20 hours per week for 100 total hours, culminating in a research symposium presentation. Advanced juniors/seniors may qualify for the Lab Internship track with Yale faculty.
internships.whyApply: Conduct real research at one of the world's premier natural history museums, working directly with Yale graduate students and faculty.

internships.title: Yale ITS High School Internship Program
internships.description: 8-week paid summer internship for high school students from New Haven, West Haven, or Orange, CT. Students work within Yale's IT department gaining hands-on experience in technology operations, software development, cybersecurity, and IT project management. School nomination required.
internships.whyApply: Gain real IT experience at an Ivy League institution. Excellent for students interested in computer science, cybersecurity, or tech careers.

internships.title: Youth@Work — New Haven Work-Based Learning
internships.company: City of New Haven Youth and Recreation
internships.description: Paid summer work-based learning program for New Haven youth ages 14-21. Students are placed with local businesses, nonprofits, and city agencies in roles spanning finance, marketing, customer service, administration, and project support. Develops workplace readiness, professional skills, and career exploration in a real employment setting.
internships.whyApply: Accessible paid work experience for New Haven students — great first professional experience across multiple career fields.

internships.title: ReadyCT Career-Connected Learning Internships
internships.description: Statewide paid internship program placing Connecticut public high school students with 100+ employers across business, insurance, IT, finance, healthcare, and manufacturing sectors. Students interview for positions, work on real projects, and gain professional experience. Focuses on providing access to low-income and minority students. Includes the G.R.I.T. (Get Ready! Immersive Traineeship) track with team-based client projects.
internships.whyApply: One of Connecticut's largest career-connected learning programs with 100+ employer partners. Paid internships with real workplace expectations.

internships.title: Bank of America Student Leaders Program — Connecticut
internships.description: Nationally competitive 8-week paid internship connecting rising juniors and seniors with nonprofits in Hartford, Fairfield, or New Haven communities. Includes a week-long Student Leaders Summit in Washington, D.C. for leadership development and civic engagement training. Approximately 300 students selected nationwide.
internships.whyApply: Prestigious national program combining nonprofit internship with financial literacy and leadership training. Strong resume builder.

internships.title: Greater New Haven Bioscience Collaborative Internship
internships.description: Paid internship for high school students providing real-world bioscience research experience in the New Haven biotech corridor. Students work up to 10 paid hours per week alongside researchers and industry professionals. Availability to start in May or June 2026. Hands-on experience in laboratory techniques, data analysis, and scientific communication.
internships.whyApply: Access New Haven's thriving biotech ecosystem. Part-time paid schedule makes it combinable with other summer activities.

internships.title: Mystic Seaport Museum Maritime Adventure Program (MAP)
internships.description: Experiential maritime education program engaging under-resourced high school youth through hands-on sailing, navigation, marine ecology, and boatbuilding. Anchored in positive youth development to build social, emotional, and leadership skills. Students gain real seamanship experience at America's leading maritime museum.
internships.whyApply: Unique experiential program at America's premier maritime museum. Builds confidence, teamwork, and environmental awareness through real sailing and seamanship.

internships.title: Music Theatre of Connecticut Summer Internship
internships.description: 10-week summer theater internship covering administration, box office operations, education programming, and production. Education component lasts 5 weeks at 6-7 hours/day. Interns gain hands-on experience in all aspects of running a professional theater company, from marketing to stage management. Weekly stipend provided.
internships.whyApply: Comprehensive professional theater experience covering both creative and business sides. Paid stipend makes this accessible.

internships.title: Children's Theater of Madison Summer Stage Internship
internships.description: High school interns assist professional teaching artists during CTM's Summer Stage productions in 1-3 week programs. Responsibilities include supporting student performers, modeling positive classroom behavior, and helping with production elements. Earn credit toward CTM classes and community service hours.
internships.whyApply: Great entry-level theater experience working alongside professional teaching artists. Ideal for students interested in education or performing arts.

internships.title: Connecticut Science Center Investigate! Program
internships.description: After-school STEM exploration program for teens at Connecticut Science Center. Covers engaging topics not typically taught in school: food science, computer coding, sound engineering, architecture & design, and more. Completing Investigate! qualifies teens for the competitive Summer Teen Innovation Program as assistant managers and peer mentors. Pathway from Investigate! → Teen Advisory Council → Teen Innovation internship.
internships.whyApply: Gateway to the prestigious Teen Innovation Program. Explore STEM fields you won't encounter in school, at Hartford's premier science museum.

internships.title: Seattle Indian Health Board Youth Internship
internships.description: 8-week paid summer internship for high school students aged 16-18 interested in American Indian/Alaska Native public health. 20 hours/week from late June through mid-August. Exposure to community health, behavioral health, dental, and medical programs serving urban Native populations.
internships.whyApply: Unique opportunity to learn about Indigenous health equity in an urban setting. Strong hourly pay and hands-on community health experience.

internships.title: Dallas Bar Association Summer Law Intern Program (SLIP)
internships.description: 8-week paid summer legal internship placing high school students with law firms and corporate legal departments throughout the Dallas area. Students shadow attorneys, attend court proceedings, and gain exposure to various legal practice areas. Partnership with Dallas Independent School District.
internships.whyApply: Direct placement with real law firms in a major legal market. Excellent for students considering law careers.

internships.title: HBA Communities In Schools Legal Internship
internships.company: Houston Bar Association / Communities In Schools
internships.description: Paid 8-week summer legal internship for Houston-area high school students from at-risk schools. Placements at major firms including Jones Day, Baker & Hostetler, Gibbs & Bruns, and others. Students gain exposure to corporate law, litigation, and legal operations while earning money for college.
internships.whyApply: Placement at top Houston law firms. Specifically designed for students from underserved communities.

internships.title: California Attorney General Student Intern Program
internships.description: Summer internship at the California Attorney General's office for students interested in public interest law and government. Interns work alongside Deputy Attorneys General on civil and criminal matters, policy analysis, and legal research across divisions including civil rights, environmental law, and consumer protection.
internships.whyApply: Prestigious government legal experience. Exposure to state-level civil rights, environmental, and consumer protection law.

internships.title: Kaiser Permanente Legal Careers Internship
internships.description: Paid summer internship for current high school students and graduating seniors ages 16-19. Explore legal careers within one of the nation's largest healthcare organizations. Work alongside Kaiser Permanente attorneys on health law, compliance, contracts, and regulatory matters. June 16 through August 1.
internships.whyApply: Well-paid legal internship at a Fortune 500 healthcare company. Unique intersection of law and healthcare.

internships.title: UW INSIGHT Summer Research Training Program
internships.company: Harborview Injury Prevention & Research Center / UW
internships.description: Summer research training program at UW's Harborview Injury Prevention & Research Center. Students work on injury prevention research projects including data analysis, literature review, and community health assessment. Focus on health equity and reducing disparities in injury outcomes.
internships.whyApply: Research experience at one of the nation's top trauma centers. Focus on health equity and injury prevention.

internships.title: Texas Attorney General Student Internship
internships.company: Office of the Texas Attorney General
internships.description: Unpaid internship for students interested in law and government at the Texas AG's office. Work alongside state attorneys on legal research, case preparation, and policy analysis. Departments include consumer protection, Medicaid fraud, human trafficking, and environmental enforcement.
internships.whyApply: Direct exposure to state-level legal practice and policy. Excellent for students considering law or public service careers.

You'll work alongside talented professionals and gain insights into industry best practices.
